mainIndustries.en: Millet, rice, and sorghum agriculture, pastoralism, gold extraction and transit, salt and copper trade, weaving, leatherworking, ironworking, river transport, and caravan services

tradeGoods.zh: 黄金、可乐果、奴隶、皮革、布匹、谷物、阿拉伯与撒哈拉盐、铜、马匹、书籍与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Gold, kola nuts, slaves, leather, textiles, grain, Saharan and Arab salt, copper, horses, books, and luxury goods

name.zh: 金粉、金块与外来金币并用货币体系
name.en: Gold dust, bullion, and imported coin mixed currency system

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富主要表现为粮食储备、牲畜、亲属网络、工具和少量金粉或布匹；城市商人与学者家庭财产明显高于普通农户。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was held in grain stores, livestock, kinship ties, tools, and small amounts of gold dust or cloth; urban merchant and scholarly households were substantially richer than ordinary farming families.

economicSystem.zh: 以王权控制关键资源与商路、地方市场交换、家族生产和附庸贡赋相结合的前近代贸易农业经济
economicSystem.en: A premodern trade-agrarian economy combining royal control over key resources and routes, local market exchange, household production, and tributary extraction

source.zh: 黄金生产与王室矿权收入
source.en: Gold production and royal mining rights

source.zh: 罚金、渡口税与杂项收入
source.en: Fines, ferry tolls, and miscellaneous income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库仍拥有相当可观的黄金与储备物资，但已不复曼萨穆萨时代的充裕。财政更依赖维持贸易节点和地方忠诚，面对边疆流失时弹性下降。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury still held substantial gold and reserve stores, but no longer possessed the abundance associated with the age of Mansa Musa. Fiscal resilience depended increasingly on maintaining trade nodes and provincial loyalty, and weakened as frontier territories slipped away.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持宫廷威望、军队忠诚和商路税收，而非大规模制度化改革；在衰退期更重视从贡赋、关税与王室资源中榨取现有收入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized preserving court prestige, military loyalty, and customs revenue rather than undertaking major systemic reform; in decline, it focused on extracting existing income from tribute, customs, and royal resources.

description.zh: 以长矛、弓箭、盾牌和部分铁制武器装备，是帝国军队主力，负责驻防、会战和护卫商路。
description.en: Armed mainly with spears, bows, shields, and some iron weapons, they formed the main strength of the imperial army for garrison duty, field battles, and caravan-route protection.

description.zh: 由贵族与精锐侍从构成，马匹多依赖撒哈拉贸易输入，是快速打击、追击和王权象征的重要兵种。
description.en: Composed of nobles and elite retainers, and reliant on horses imported through Saharan trade, cavalry served as the key arm for rapid strikes, pursuit, and royal prestige.

description.zh: 由附庸与地方首领按需提供，训练和装备差异较大，适用于防御、围困和区域作战。
description.en: Supplied by tributaries and local chiefs as needed, they varied greatly in training and equipment and were used for defense, siege support, and regional campaigning.

description.zh: 负责宫廷安全、渡口控制和尼日尔水道节点护卫，规模较小但政治上可靠。
description.en: Responsible for palace security, ferry control, and guarding key Niger waterway points, they were relatively small but politically reliable.

commanderInChief.zh: 曼萨马哈茂德二世（由高级法林与诸将辅佐）
commanderInChief.en: Mansa Mahmud II, assisted by senior farins and leading commanders

technology.zh: 军事技术以传统西苏丹步骑兵体系为主，使用弓、矛、刀剑、皮盾、锁子或棉甲等防具；火器几乎未形成决定性装备体系，攻城能力有限。
technology.en: Military technology remained centered on the classic western Sudanic infantry-cavalry system, using bows, spears, swords, leather shields, and some mail or quilted armor; firearms had not yet become a decisive arm, and siege capabilities were limited.

threats.zh: 最大威胁来自东部和东北方向日益强盛的桑海帝国，以及内部附庸离心、金矿区控制力下降和商路转移；南方森林边缘与西部地方势力也不断蚕食中央权威。
threats.en: The greatest threat came from the rising Songhay Empire to the east and northeast, along with internal tributary separatism, waning control over goldfields, and shifting trade routes; southern forest-frontier and western local powers also eroded central authority.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约四百万，主要分布在上尼日尔河流域、萨赫勒边缘农牧地带、商路城镇与森林-草原过渡地带。帝国名义疆域广于实际有效控制区域，人口结构高度分散。
populationDescription.en: The population was about four million, concentrated in the upper Niger basin, Sahelian agro-pastoral zones, caravan towns, and forest-savanna transition regions. The empire’s nominal extent exceeded its effective control, and its population was highly dispersed.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为曼萨及凯塔王族、贵族、地方首领和穆斯林法学家；中间层包括商人、书记、自由农民、牧民与工匠；下层为依附民、债务从属者与奴隶。世袭职业集团和口传史家在社会中具有明确地位。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the mansa, the Keita royal house, nobles, provincial chiefs, and Muslim jurists; the middle ranks included merchants, scribes, free farmers, herders, and artisans; the lower ranks included dependents, debt-bound persons, and slaves. Hereditary occupational groups and oral historians held defined social positions.

allies.zh: 缺乏稳定的大型对外盟友，更多依靠与部分曼丁地方首领、撒哈拉商人网络及若干边地势力的临时合作关系
allies.en: It lacked stable major external allies and relied more on temporary cooperation with some Mande local rulers, Saharan merchant networks, and selected frontier powers

enemies.zh: 桑海帝国是最主要的地缘政治对手；同时与部分脱离控制的附庸和边境首领长期处于敌对或反复叛服状态。
enemies.en: The Songhay Empire was its main geopolitical rival; it also faced chronic hostility or recurrent rebellion from tributaries and frontier chiefs slipping out of control.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保住核心曼丁地区、维持跨撒哈拉贸易联系、争取边疆首领效忠和避免被桑海进一步挤压为主，整体呈防御性与保守性。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed above all to retain the core Mande lands, preserve trans-Saharan commercial links, secure the loyalty of frontier chiefs, and prevent further encroachment by Songhay, making it broadly defensive and conservative.

era.zh: 文艺复兴时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力在于黄金称量与贸易体系、萨赫勒农业适应、河运与商队组织、泥砖建筑和阿拉伯文书行政传统；其创新更多体现为跨区域商业与文化整合，而非火器或机械突破。
keyInnovations.en: Its key capabilities lay in gold-weight trade systems, Sahelian agricultural adaptation, riverine and caravan organization, mud-brick architecture, and Arabic scribal administration; innovation was expressed more in regional commercial and cultural integration than in firearms or mechanical breakthroughs.

strengths.zh: 仍拥有深厚的王朝合法性、丰富的黄金资源传统、连接萨赫勒与森林地带的贸易网络、伊斯兰学术声望以及曼丁核心地区的人口与文化凝聚力。
strengths.en: It still possessed deep dynastic legitimacy, a strong legacy of gold wealth, trade networks linking the Sahel and forest zones, Islamic scholarly prestige, and demographic and cultural cohesion in the Mande core.

weaknesses.zh: 中央集权减弱、地方割据加剧、对远方附庸控制有限、军事机动与攻坚能力不足，并在商路和政治上遭到桑海压制。
weaknesses.en: Central authority had weakened, provincial fragmentation had deepened, control over distant tributaries was limited, military mobility and siege power were inadequate, and it was being outcompeted politically and commercially by Songhay.

outlook.zh: 若无强有力的军事与财政重整，马里在16世纪将继续缩回曼丁核心区，难以恢复曼萨穆萨时代的帝国规模，但其王朝名号、文化影响和地方王权传统仍会长期延续。
outlook.en: Without strong military and fiscal reorganization, Mali was likely to contract further into its Mande heartland during the sixteenth century and was unlikely to recover the imperial scale of Mansa Musa’s age, though its dynastic name, cultural influence, and traditions of local kingship would endure.

description.zh: 马里帝国已不复曼萨·穆萨时期盛况，但仍是西非重要王权。
description.en: Mali no longer matched its peak under Mansa Musa, but it remained an important West African monarchy.

governmentForm.zh: 中央集权君主制，带有分封性省域统治结构
governmentForm.en: Centralized monarchy with a provincial tributary structure

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、地方领主、自由民、手工业者、商人、依附民与奴隶构成；亲族关系、地方共同体和对国王的效忠共同塑造政治与社会秩序。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house and nobility, provincial lords, free commoners, artisans, merchants, dependents, and slaves; kinship ties, local communities, and loyalty to the king jointly shaped political and social order.

rulingClass.zh: 基卢凯尼王室、姆韦内贵族、各省总督与宫廷显贵
rulingClass.en: The Kilukeni royal house, mwene nobles, provincial governors, and leading court elites

succession.zh: 王位通常由王族内部推举与协商决定，重视宗族资历、政治支持与首都贵族的认可，而非严格长子继承。
succession.en: Succession was generally decided within the royal lineage by election and negotiation, emphasizing clan seniority, political backing, and recognition by the capital’s nobles rather than strict primogeniture.

structure.zh: 国王统治首都与核心地区，通过王室亲族、贵族和受任命的地方统治者管理各省；中央对附属地区征收贡赋、调集劳役与军事力量。
structure.en: The king ruled the capital and core territories through royal kin, nobles, and appointed provincial rulers; the center extracted tribute, labor, and military service from subordinate regions.

function.zh: 负责国王顾问事务、仪式、使节接待、王室财产与高层裁决。
function.en: Handled royal counsel, ceremony, reception of envoys, royal estates, and high-level adjudication.

function.zh: 监督各省服从、贡物征收、地方任命与道路联络。
function.en: Oversaw provincial obedience, tribute collection, local appointments, and communications along road networks.

function.zh: 处理土地、婚姻、继承、债务与刑事纠纷，结合王命与习惯法裁判。
function.en: Dealt with disputes over land, marriage, inheritance, debt, and criminal matters, applying royal authority alongside customary law.

function.zh: 管理税赋、贡物、贝币、纺织品、金属物资与宫廷仓储分配。
function.en: Managed taxes, tribute goods, shell currency, textiles, metal supplies, and court storehouse distribution.

function.zh: 登记可征召兵员、组织王室卫队、统筹省军与边境动员。
function.en: Registered manpower for levy, organized the royal guard, and coordinated provincial forces and frontier mobilization.

function.zh: 负责与葡萄牙使节、传教士和商人往来，协调礼物交换、贸易与宗教事务。
function.en: Managed relations with Portuguese envoys, missionaries, and merchants, coordinating gift exchange, trade, and religious matters.

localAdmin.zh: 地方以省为核心，由受王权承认的总督、领主和村社首领管理；地方保有较强习惯法与亲族自治，但需进贡、出兵并参加朝觐。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on provinces governed by royal-recognized governors, lords, and village chiefs; local society retained strong customary and kin-based autonomy but owed tribute, military service, and attendance at court.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以习惯法为基础，由国王与贵族会议进行最终裁决；案件常通过宣誓、证言、调解、罚物和社会性制裁解决，重视恢复秩序而非成文法统一。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on customary law, with final decisions by the king and noble councils; cases were commonly resolved through oaths, testimony, mediation, fines in goods, and social sanctions, emphasizing restoration of order rather than uniform written law.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖各省贡赋、王室直辖地出产、市场征收、劳役、战争俘获与贸易收益；贡物常以布匹、贝币、象牙、铜器、农产与奴隶形式缴纳。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue relied chiefly on provincial tribute, production from royal domains, market dues, labor service, war captives, and trade proceeds; tribute was commonly paid in cloth, shell currency, ivory, copper goods, agricultural produce, and slaves.

religion.zh: 1500年时王室已接受天主教并与传统信仰并存；祖先崇拜、圣物、地方祭仪与新引入的基督教礼拜共同存在，形成早期宗教融合。
religion.en: By 1500 the royal court had accepted Catholicism, which coexisted with traditional religion; ancestor veneration, sacred objects, local rites, and newly introduced Christian worship existed side by side, producing an early religious syncretism.

philosophy.zh: 政治观念强调神圣王权、祖先合法性、亲族责任、共同体和解与等级秩序；受葡萄牙接触影响，宫廷开始接纳部分基督教君主观念与文字文化。
philosophy.en: Political thought emphasized sacred kingship, ancestral legitimacy, kin obligations, communal reconciliation, and hierarchical order; through Portuguese contact, the court began to absorb some Christian monarchical concepts and literacy culture.

writingSystem.zh: 本地行政长期以口述、记忆和礼仪传承为主；宫廷在与葡萄牙交往中开始使用拉丁字母书写葡萄牙语及少量刚果语记录。
writingSystem.en: Local administration long relied on oral transmission, memory, and ritual performance; through dealings with Portugal, the court began using the Latin alphabet for Portuguese and limited Kongo-language records.

culturalAchievements.zh: 形成高度发展的宫廷礼仪、王都规划、木雕与象牙工艺、纺织品生产、鼓乐与公共仪式文化；王室皈依后，基督教象征开始进入宫廷艺术与外交礼制。
culturalAchievements.en: The kingdom developed sophisticated court ritual, capital-city organization, woodcarving and ivory craftsmanship, textile production, drumming, and public ceremonial culture; after royal conversion, Christian symbols began entering court art and diplomatic protocol.

languageFamily.zh: 班图语系；宫廷与民间主要使用刚果语（基刚果语），外交中也接触葡萄牙语。
languageFamily.en: Bantu language family; Kongo society mainly used Kikongo, while diplomacy also brought contact with Portuguese.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、地方手工业、贡赋体系和区域贸易为基础，货币化程度有限但政治整合较强。与葡萄牙的海上联系提升了象牙、铜器与奴隶贸易价值，使王室掌握更多高价值交换品。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, local craft production, a tribute system, and regional trade, with limited monetization but strong political integration. Maritime contact with Portugal increased the value of ivory, copper goods, and slave trading, giving the monarchy access to more high-value exchange goods.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业（粟、黍、香蕉、豆类、棕榈产品）、渔猎、纺织、制陶、冶铁、铜器加工、象牙雕刻与奴隶贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture (millets, sorghum, bananas, legumes, palm products), fishing and hunting, textile production, pottery, ironworking, copperworking, ivory carving, and slave trading

tradeGoods.zh: 象牙、铜十字与铜器、织物、棕榈产品、盐、铁器、陶器、奴隶；输入品包括葡萄牙布匹、金属制品、酒类、宗教器物与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Ivory, copper crosses and copper goods, textiles, palm products, salt, iron tools, pottery, and slaves; imports included Portuguese cloth, metalwares, wine, religious objects, and luxury goods

name.en: Nzimbu shell currency and copper crosses

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、亲族网络、住房、农具、织物、牲畜与储粮；精英财富则集中在奴隶、铜器、象牙、进口货和政治依附者。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly embodied in land-use rights, kin networks, dwellings, tools, textiles, livestock, and stored food; elite wealth was concentrated in slaves, copper goods, ivory, imported items, and dependent followers.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业村社和王室贡赋为基础的再分配经济，辅以区域市场交换和受王权影响的长距离贸易。
economicSystem.en: A redistributive economy based on agrarian communities and royal tribute, supplemented by regional market exchange and crown-influenced long-distance trade.

source.zh: 贸易关征与礼物交换收益
source.en: Trade dues and diplomatic exchange gains

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非完全以铸币或银块形式保存，而是由贝币、铜器、象牙、织物、储粮、王室牲畜和进口奢侈物共同构成；其价值受政治控制能力影响很大。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not held entirely as coined money or bullion but as a combination of shell currency, copper goods, ivory, textiles, stored food, royal livestock, and imported luxuries; its real value depended heavily on political control.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持王权威望、稳定各省效忠和保障宫廷再分配为核心；在稳定时期倾向保持适度盈余，以应对继承争议、边境战争或饥荒。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on preserving royal prestige, stabilizing provincial loyalty, and sustaining court redistribution; in stable times it tended toward modest surplus to prepare for succession disputes, frontier wars, or famine.

description.zh: 驻守都城与王宫，负责国王安全、仪式护卫和快速镇压叛乱。
description.en: Stationed in the capital and palace, responsible for royal security, ceremonial escort, and rapid suppression of unrest.

description.zh: 由各省在征召时提供，为军队主力，装备长矛、弓箭、盾牌和部分铁制兵器。
description.en: Provided by the provinces when levied, forming the main body of the army and equipped with spears, bows, shields, and some iron weapons.

description.zh: 由贵族和地方领主率领的亲随武装，战斗力和忠诚度依附于个人关系网络。
description.en: Armed followings led by nobles and local lords, whose effectiveness and loyalty depended on personal patronage networks.

description.zh: 负责河流交通、沿岸联络与有限的海岸监视，但并非成熟海军。
description.en: Responsible for river transport, coastal liaison, and limited shore surveillance, though not a fully developed navy.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王亲自兼任最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: The king personally served as commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制冷兵器、木盾、弓矛和大规模步兵动员为主；与葡萄牙接触后已知火器存在，但1500年前后尚未广泛标准化装备于全军。
technology.en: Military technology was based on iron melee weapons, wooden shields, bows, spears, and mass infantry mobilization; firearms were known through contact with Portugal, but by around 1500 they were not yet widely standardized across the army.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自附属地区离心、继承争端、邻近政治体的边境冲突，以及对沿海贸易控制权的竞争。葡萄牙关系带来机遇，也可能引发内部派系依赖外来支持。
threats.en: Primary threats came from centrifugal tendencies in subordinate regions, succession disputes, border conflict with neighboring polities, and competition over control of coastal trade. Relations with Portugal brought opportunities but could also foster internal factions reliant on outside support.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约两百万，集中于农业村落、河流走廊与政治核心区。人口密度在首都周边和较稳定的省份较高，边缘地区较为稀疏。
populationDescription.en: The population was about two million, concentrated in agrarian villages, river corridors, and the political core zone. Population density was higher around the capital and in more stable provinces, while frontier areas were more sparsely settled.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与高级贵族、地方领主与省官、祭司与新兴教会人员、商人和工匠、自由农民、依附民与奴隶。社会流动存在，但高度依赖亲族、政治庇护与战争结果。
socialClasses.en: Royalty and high nobility, local lords and provincial officials, ritual specialists and emerging church personnel, merchants and artisans, free farmers, dependents, and slaves. Social mobility existed but depended heavily on kinship, political patronage, and the outcomes of war.

allies.zh: 与葡萄牙保持友好和互惠关系，并通过使节、传教与贸易建立稳定联系；同时与部分周边附属和朝贡地区维持宗主—臣属式合作。
allies.en: Kongo maintained friendly and mutually beneficial relations with Portugal through envoys, missionary activity, and trade; it also sustained suzerain-subordinate cooperation with some neighboring tributary regions.

enemies.zh: 无持续性的单一宿敌，但与不服从的边缘领地、竞争性邻邦及控制贸易通道的地方势力常有冲突。
enemies.en: There was no single permanent archenemy, but conflict was frequent with disobedient frontier territories, rival neighboring polities, and local powers controlling trade routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以巩固王国在西中非的区域主导地位为核心，同时利用与葡萄牙的关系获取宗教合法性、外交 prestige 与贸易利益，避免完全受制于海上外来力量。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on consolidating the kingdom’s regional predominance in west-central Africa while using relations with Portugal to gain religious legitimacy, diplomatic prestige, and trade benefits, without becoming wholly dependent on external maritime power.

era.zh: 文艺复兴时代

keyInnovations.zh: 冶铁技术成熟，铜器与象牙工艺发达，王都与省域道路体系有较高组织能力；通过葡萄牙接触开始引入拉丁字母书写、天主教礼仪、欧洲金属器物和有限火器知识。
keyInnovations.en: Ironworking was mature, copper and ivory craftsmanship were well developed, and the capital and provincial road systems showed considerable organizational capacity; contact with Portugal began introducing Latin-alphabet literacy, Catholic liturgy, European metal goods, and limited firearms knowledge.

strengths.zh: 王权具有较强整合力，首都和核心省份稳定，贡赋体系成熟，区域贸易与对葡联系提升了外交与经济资源，文化适应力强。
strengths.en: Royal authority possessed substantial integrative power, the capital and core provinces were stable, the tribute system was mature, regional trade and Portuguese ties enhanced diplomatic and economic resources, and the culture showed strong adaptive capacity.

weaknesses.zh: 政治秩序高度依赖王室个人威望和宗族平衡，地方分权与继承竞争始终潜伏；经济货币化有限，对沿海外贸和精英再分配的依赖使财政韧性不足。
weaknesses.en: Political order depended heavily on the personal prestige of the monarchy and on lineage balance, while provincial autonomy and succession rivalry remained latent; limited monetization and reliance on coastal trade and elite redistribution reduced fiscal resilience.

outlook.zh: 1500年前后的刚果王国总体稳定，且因与葡萄牙往来而拥有扩展影响力的机会；但若未来外贸、传教和王位继承相互交织，内部派系化和地方离心可能削弱王国长期统一。
outlook.en: Around 1500 the Kingdom of Kongo was broadly stable and had opportunities to expand its influence through relations with Portugal; however, if foreign trade, missionary activity, and royal succession became intertwined, internal factionalism and provincial centrifugalism could weaken long-term unity.

description.zh: 刚果王国是中西非重要集权国家，并与葡萄牙保持联系。
description.en: Kongo was a major centralized kingdom of west-central Africa and maintained ties with Portugal.

governmentForm.zh: 高度集中的世袭君主制地区国家，由卡松齐通过贵族、祭司与地方总督治理湖区与周边行省
governmentForm.en: A highly centralized hereditary regional monarchy in which the Cazonci ruled the lake basin and surrounding provinces through nobles, priests, and provincial governors

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室与贵族为上层，其下为祭司、军官、工匠、商贩、农民与附庸社区；国家对工匠尤其是金属工匠有较强组织能力
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the royal house and nobility, followed by priests, military officers, artisans, merchants, cultivators, and subject communities; the state exercised especially strong organization over artisans, notably metalworkers

rulingClass.zh: 卡松齐王室、世袭贵族、军事领袖、祭司集团以及受王权任命的地方统治者
rulingClass.en: The Cazonci’s royal household, hereditary nobles, military leaders, priestly elites, and local rulers appointed under royal authority

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部世袭继承，并由贵族与宫廷核心集团认可，重视血统合法性与政治支持
succession.en: The throne was generally inherited within the royal lineage, with recognition by nobles and the core court elite, emphasizing dynastic legitimacy and political backing

structure.zh: 国家以辛聪聪赞宫廷为行政中心，卡松齐统领军政、司法与宗教事务；下设王室内廷、贡赋管理、军事指挥、宗教祭祀和地方行省治理体系
structure.en: The state was administered from the court at Tzintzuntzan, where the Cazonci oversaw military, administrative, judicial, and religious affairs; beneath him operated a royal household, tribute management, military command, priestly institutions, and provincial administration

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室土地、仓储、礼仪与核心政令传达
function.en: Managed the court, royal lands, storehouses, ceremony, and transmission of central orders

function.zh: 征收各地贡赋，登记玉米、棉布、金属器、羽饰与劳役，并负责再分配
function.en: Collected provincial tribute, recorded maize, cotton cloth, metal goods, featherwork, and labor dues, and handled redistribution

function.zh: 组织边境防御、动员战士、武器生产与要塞守备
function.en: Organized frontier defense, mobilization of warriors, weapons production, and garrisoning of fortified positions

function.zh: 主持国家祭祀、历法仪式、神庙管理与统治合法性的宗教表达
function.en: Oversaw state rituals, calendrical ceremonies, temple management, and the religious expression of royal legitimacy

function.zh: 监督附属城镇与边地总督，协调征税、治安、道路与劳役征发
function.en: Supervised subject towns and frontier governors, coordinating taxation, security, roads, and labor levies

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依靠受王权控制的城镇首领、总督和驻军网络运行。核心湖区控制最强，边缘地区则通过附庸首领、贡赋义务和军事威慑维系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration functioned through town chiefs, governors, and garrison networks controlled by royal authority. Control was strongest in the core lake basin, while outer areas were held through subject lords, tribute obligations, and military deterrence.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以君主命令、习惯法和宗教规范结合为主，对叛乱、逃贡、盗窃和违抗王令处罚严厉；司法多由地方官与贵族裁断，重大案件上达宫廷。
legalSystem.en: Law combined royal decree, customary norms, and religious sanction, with severe penalties for rebellion, tribute evasion, theft, and disobedience. Justice was usually administered by local officials and nobles, with major cases referred to the court.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖实物贡赋与劳役，包括玉米、豆类、棉布、羽毛、铜器、工艺品和军事服务；核心地区承担更稳定的供给，附属地区按地位与距离缴纳贡赋。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal system relied mainly on tribute in kind and labor service, including maize, beans, cotton cloth, feathers, copper goods, craft products, and military service; the core region provided more regular supplies, while subject provinces paid according to status and distance.

religion.zh: 普雷佩查多神信仰，以火神与太阳相关神祇崇拜为核心，国家祭祀与王权合法性紧密相连
religion.en: Purépecha polytheistic religion centered on fire and solar-associated deities, with state ritual closely tied to royal legitimacy

philosophy.zh: 思想基础主要体现为祖先崇拜、神圣王权、祭祀秩序与共同体义务，而非成体系的书面哲学传统
philosophy.en: Its intellectual foundations were expressed through ancestor veneration, sacred kingship, ritual order, and communal obligation rather than a systematic written philosophical tradition

writingSystem.zh: 无完整成熟的本土文字体系，行政与记忆主要依赖口传、仪式记录、图像符号与官员传述
writingSystem.en: There was no fully developed indigenous writing system; administration and historical memory relied chiefly on oral transmission, ritual record-keeping, pictorial symbols, and official recitation

culturalAchievements.zh: 以精湛铜、铜合金与贵金属工艺闻名，擅长羽饰、木工、陶器、建筑平台与礼仪中心营造；辛聪聪赞的圆形神庙群体现出鲜明地方风格。
culturalAchievements.en: The civilization was renowned for sophisticated copper, copper-alloy, and precious-metal craftsmanship, as well as featherwork, woodworking, ceramics, platform architecture, and ceremonial centers; the circular temple complex of Tzintzuntzan displayed a distinctive regional style.

languageFamily.zh: 普雷佩查语系，属中部墨西哥罕见的语言孤立体系
languageFamily.en: The Purépecha language family, a rare linguistic isolate in central-western Mexico

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高产农业、贡赋再分配和国家组织下的专业手工业为基础。与欧亚大型帝国相比总体规模较小，但在中美洲属于强大而高效的区域国家经济，尤其以金属制造能力突出。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on productive agriculture, tribute redistribution, and specialized crafts organized under state authority. Small in absolute terms compared with major Eurasian empires, it was nevertheless a powerful and efficient regional economy within Mesoamerica, particularly notable for metal production.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、渔业、黑曜石与石器制作、陶器、纺织、羽饰、木工、铜与铜合金冶铸
mainIndustries.en: Maize farming, bean and squash cultivation, fishing, obsidian and stone-tool production, pottery, textiles, featherwork, woodworking, and copper and copper-alloy smelting and casting

tradeGoods.zh: 铜铃、斧形货值器、装饰金属器、棉布、玉米、盐、鱼产、黑曜石、羽毛、陶器与奢侈工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Copper bells, axe-money forms, ornamental metal goods, cotton cloth, maize, salt, fish products, obsidian, feathers, ceramics, and luxury craft items

name.zh: 无统一铸币；以贡赋与物品价值交换为主
name.en: No unified coinage; exchange relied chiefly on tribute and valued goods

unitName.zh: 斧形铜器与布匹等值单位
unitName.en: Value units in copper axes, cloth, and other commodities

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、住房、农具、储粮、衣物和家族网络；贵族家庭则控制更多劳力、贡赋和奢侈工艺品。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was expressed in access to farmland, dwellings, tools, stored grain, clothing, and kin networks; noble households controlled larger labor pools, tribute flows, and luxury craft goods.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导的贡赋再分配经济与地方市场交换并存，农业社区提供基础产出，王权整合手工业与战略资源，边境贸易受政治与军事条件影响明显。
economicSystem.en: A state-directed tribute redistribution economy coexisted with local market exchange; farming communities provided the base output, royal power integrated artisanal production and strategic resources, and frontier trade was strongly shaped by political and military conditions.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 贸易、边境征收与附属贡献
source.en: Trade, Frontier Levies, and Subject Contributions

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要以储粮、布匹、金属器、礼仪用品和可再分配的实物形式存在，而非大量标准化贵金属货币；其价值在丰年较为稳定。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly of stored grain, cloth, metal goods, ritual items, and redistributable goods rather than large stocks of standardized precious-metal currency; its value was relatively stable in good years.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持边境军事压力、保障宫廷与祭祀中心、储备物资并通过再分配巩固忠诚为核心，整体偏向审慎保守。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining frontier military pressure, supporting the court and ritual centers, building stores, and reinforcing loyalty through redistribution; overall it was relatively cautious and conservative.

description.zh: 驻守都城与核心湖区，保护卡松齐、宫廷和主要宗教中心，是最可靠的常备武装。
description.en: Stationed in the capital and core lake basin, they protected the Cazonci, court, and major ritual centers and formed the most reliable standing force.

description.zh: 由附属城镇和地方首领在战时提供，人数众多，适合边境防御与大规模动员。
description.en: Supplied by subject towns and local rulers in wartime, these forces were numerous and suitable for frontier defense and large-scale mobilization.

description.zh: 部署在与纳瓦诸邦接壤地区，负责瞭望、阻截袭扰和守卫边防据点。
description.en: Deployed along borders with Nahua polities, they handled surveillance, repelling raids, and guarding frontier strongpoints.

commanderInChief.zh: 卡松齐祖安瓜
commanderInChief.en: Cazonci Zuangua

technology.zh: 军备以木盾、长矛、投矛器、弓箭、石刃武器与棉甲为主，同时在铜与铜合金器物制作上具有中美洲领先优势，但尚未发展火器、骑兵或铁器战争体系。
technology.en: Armament centered on shields, spears, atlatls, bows, stone-edged weapons, and quilted cotton armor, while the state possessed one of Mesoamerica’s leading traditions in copper and copper-alloy manufacture; however, it had not developed firearms, cavalry, or iron-based warfare.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自东部与东南方向的纳瓦诸邦，尤其是阿兹特克势力的长期竞争、边境冲突与代理战争；1490年代后还出现西方海岸外来者传闻带来的潜在不确定性。
threats.en: The main threats came from Nahua states to the east and southeast, especially long-term rivalry, frontier conflict, and proxy struggles with the Aztec sphere; by the 1490s there was also growing uncertainty from reports of foreign newcomers off western coasts.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于帕茨夸罗湖区及其周边高地、农耕谷地与附属城镇，是一个在西墨西哥人口密度较高、组织程度较强的区域国家。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the Lake Pátzcuaro basin and surrounding highlands, farming valleys, and subject towns, making it a densely settled and well-organized regional state in western Mexico.

socialClasses.zh: 社会大体可分为王族与贵族、祭司、军事首领、专业工匠、商贩、普通农民与渔民、劳役人口以及被征服附属社群；社会流动有限但在军功和宫廷服务中存在上升空间。
socialClasses.en: Society may be broadly divided into the royal line and nobility, priests, military leaders, specialist artisans, merchants, ordinary farmers and fishers, laboring populations, and conquered subject communities; mobility was limited but advancement existed through military merit and court service.

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的大规模正式盟友体系，但与部分反阿兹特克边境社群、贸易伙伴和附属首领保持实用性合作关系。
allies.en: There was no large-scale formal alliance system in the later sense, but the state maintained pragmatic ties with some anti-Aztec frontier communities, trade partners, and subordinate lords.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手为纳瓦文明中的阿兹特克三方联盟及其势力范围内的敌对边境政体。
enemies.en: Its principal enemy was the Aztec Triple Alliance within the Nahua sphere, along with hostile frontier polities under its influence.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以遏制阿兹特克扩张、稳固边境缓冲区、保障贸易路线和吸纳忠诚附庸为主，倾向防御性强权平衡而非远距离扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was aimed at containing Aztec expansion, stabilizing buffer zones, securing trade routes, and binding loyal subjects to the state, favoring a defensive balance-of-power strategy rather than distant expansion.

era.zh: 文艺复兴时代

keyInnovations.zh: 在中美洲范围内，普雷佩查最突出的技术优势是铜与铜合金冶金、失蜡或复杂铸造工艺、武器与礼器金属制造，以及高地湖区农业与渔业结合的资源利用体系。
keyInnovations.en: Within Mesoamerica, the Purépecha’s key technological strengths lay in copper and copper-alloy metallurgy, sophisticated casting methods, the manufacture of weapons and ceremonial metalwork, and an integrated highland-lake resource system combining agriculture and fishing.

strengths.zh: 国家集中度高，边境防御经验丰富，核心地区稳固，金属工艺领先，能有效整合贡赋、手工业与军事资源，是阿兹特克最具实力的西方对手。
strengths.en: The state had strong centralization, extensive frontier defense experience, a secure core region, leading metalworking traditions, and an effective ability to integrate tribute, craft production, and military resources, making it the strongest western rival of the Aztecs.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏成熟文字行政体系，财政主要依赖实物与劳役，外缘控制力不如核心区稳固，经济与军事规模有限，且对跨洋来者、瘟疫与新式战争技术毫无准备。
weaknesses.en: It lacked a mature writing-based bureaucracy, depended heavily on in-kind revenue and labor, controlled peripheral regions less securely than the core, and possessed limited economic and military scale; it was also wholly unprepared for transoceanic arrivals, epidemic disease, and new modes of warfare.

outlook.zh: 在1500年前后，普雷佩查王国仍然稳定且足以遏制阿兹特克西进，但其长期前景将很快因欧亚疾病传播和西班牙征服力量进入中美洲而急剧恶化。
outlook.en: Around 1500, the Purépecha kingdom remained stable and capable of checking Aztec expansion westward, but its longer-term prospects would soon deteriorate sharply with the spread of Eurasian disease and the arrival of Spanish conquest forces in Mesoamerica.

description.zh: 塔拉斯坎国是阿兹特克在西部最强劲的对手，以金属工艺著称。
description.en: The Tarascan State was the Aztecs' strongest western rival and was noted for its metalworking.

name.zh: 纳瓦文明（阿兹特克三方同盟）

governmentForm.zh: 由特诺奇蒂特兰、特斯科科与特拉科潘组成的霸权性三方同盟帝国，以特诺奇蒂特兰为主导的朝贡君主制
governmentForm.en: A hegemonic Triple Alliance empire of Tenochtitlan, Texcoco, and Tlacopan, functioning as a tribute-based monarchy dominated by Tenochtitlan

socialStructure.zh: 社会按贵族、平民、附庸民、工匠商人、战士、祭司与奴隶分层，地方城邦保留自身统治者但需纳贡并服役
socialStructure.en: Society was stratified into nobles, commoners, subject peoples, artisans and merchants, warriors, priests, and slaves, while local city-states retained their own rulers but owed tribute and service

rulingClass.zh: 特诺奇卡贵族、军事精英、祭司集团以及三方同盟核心城邦的世袭统治家族
rulingClass.en: Tenochca nobility, military elites, priestly authorities, and hereditary ruling families of the core alliance cities

succession.zh: 统治者通常由贵族会议自王族中推举，偏重具军事功绩与政治能力者，而非严格长子继承
succession.en: The ruler was generally chosen by a noble council from the royal lineage, favoring military achievement and political ability rather than strict primogeniture

structure.zh: 中央政权以大特拉托阿尼为核心，下设贵族议政、军事统帅、祭司体系、贡赋监督与市场管理机构；地方由阿尔特佩特尔城邦及其特拉托阿尼负责日常统治
structure.en: The central state was centered on the Huey Tlatoani, supported by noble councils, military command, priestly institutions, tribute supervisors, and market regulators; locally, altepetl city-states and their tlatoani handled everyday rule

function.zh: 协助大特拉托阿尼处理继承、战争、外交、贵族任命与重大司法事务
function.en: Assisted the Huey Tlatoani in succession, war, diplomacy, noble appointments, and major judicial affairs

function.zh: 统计附庸城邦贡物、管理中央仓储、分配衣料、粮食、武器与奢侈品
function.en: Recorded tribute from subject polities and managed central storehouses distributing cloth, food, weapons, and luxury goods

function.zh: 组织征召、远征、驻防、军功记录与战俘处置
function.en: Organized levies, campaigns, garrisons, military records, and the handling of captives

function.zh: 主持宗教祭仪、维持神庙秩序、解释历法并安排国家节庆
function.en: Conducted religious rites, maintained temple order, interpreted calendars, and arranged state festivals

function.zh: 监管特拉特洛尔科与各地市场、度量衡、交易纠纷、地方审判与治安
function.en: Oversaw Tlatelolco and other markets, weights and measures, trade disputes, local adjudication, and public order

localAdmin.zh: 帝国通过阿尔特佩特尔城邦治理地方，各城邦保有本地贵族与行政单位卡尔普利；只要按期纳贡、出兵并忠于同盟，中央通常不直接改造其内部制度
localAdmin.en: The empire governed locally through altepetl city-states, which retained local nobles and calpulli administrative units; so long as they paid tribute, supplied troops, and remained loyal, the center usually did not directly remake their internal institutions

legalSystem.zh: 法律为成文习惯法与王权裁断并行，重视秩序、贡赋、婚姻、土地与市场管理；盗窃、叛乱、通奸及严重渎职处罚严厉，贵族违法常受更重惩罚
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined codified custom and royal judgment, emphasizing order, tribute, marriage, land, and market regulation; theft, rebellion, adultery, and severe official misconduct were harshly punished, and nobles could face stricter penalties

taxationSystem.zh: 核心财政依赖附庸城邦按贡册定期输送玉米、豆类、棉布、羽毛、可可、战服、武器与劳役；核心地区居民另承担公共工程、军事与祭仪服务义务
taxationSystem.en: Core finance depended on scheduled tribute from subject polities in maize, beans, cotton cloth, feathers, cacao, war costumes, weapons, and labor; inhabitants of the core region also owed public works, military, and ritual service

religion.zh: 多神教，以维齐洛波奇特利、特拉洛克、特斯卡特利波卡与羽蛇神等神祇崇拜为核心，国家祭祀与人祭是王权合法性的关键部分
religion.en: Polytheistic religion centered on deities such as Huitzilopochtli, Tlaloc, Tezcatlipoca, and Quetzalcoatl, with state ritual and human sacrifice forming key elements of royal legitimacy

philosophy.zh: 纳瓦思想重视宇宙循环、命运无常、战争荣誉、祭祀责任与“在大地上寻找真理”的诗哲传统，特斯科科宫廷尤以哲思与诗歌著称
philosophy.en: Nahua thought emphasized cosmic cycles, the instability of fate, martial honor, ritual obligation, and a poetic-philosophical search for truth on earth, especially famed at the Texcoco court

writingSystem.zh: 使用纳瓦图像文字与表意符号系统，辅以数字记号与历法符号，主要记录贡册、族谱、地界、祭历与战争功绩
writingSystem.en: Used a Nahua pictographic and ideographic writing system, supported by numerical and calendrical signs, mainly for tribute lists, genealogies, land boundaries, ritual calendars, and war records

culturalAchievements.zh: 特诺奇蒂特兰的堤道、水利与人工浮园农业高度发达；羽毛工艺、石雕、金工、诗歌、神庙建筑、历法计算与大型市场制度均达到中美洲顶峰
culturalAchievements.en: Tenochtitlan's causeways, hydraulic works, and chinampa agriculture were highly developed; featherwork, stone carving, metalworking, poetry, temple architecture, calendrical calculation, and large-scale market institutions ranked among the greatest achievements in Mesoamerica

languageFamily.zh: 乌托-阿兹特克语系，以纳瓦特尔语为政治与高地中部通用语之一
languageFamily.en: Uto-Aztecan language family, with Nahuatl serving as one of the principal political and lingua franca languages of central Mexico

unit.en: 1990 international dollars per person per day

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高强度农业、朝贡再分配、区域市场交换与长途奢侈品贸易为支柱。以1500年前后的中美洲标准看，三方同盟处于高度繁荣状态，首都与中央高地的财富集中度极高。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on intensive agriculture, tribute redistribution, regional market exchange, and long-distance luxury trade. By the standards of Mesoamerica around 1500, the Triple Alliance was highly prosperous, with extraordinary concentration of wealth in the capital and central highlands.

mainIndustries.zh: 人工浮园农业、玉米与豆类种植、龙舌兰与辣椒生产、棉布与纺织、黑曜石加工、陶器、羽毛工艺、石工、金工、军需制造与市场贸易
mainIndustries.en: Chinampa agriculture, maize and bean cultivation, maguey and chili production, cotton cloth and textiles, obsidian working, pottery, featherwork, stonecraft, metalworking, military equipment production, and market trade

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、苋籽、辣椒、可可、棉布、黑曜石刀片、盐、羽毛、玉石、绿松石、橡胶、陶器、铜器、金饰与战士服饰
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, amaranth, chili, cacao, cotton cloth, obsidian blades, salt, feathers, jade, turquoise, rubber, pottery, copper goods, gold ornaments, and warrior regalia

name.zh: 可可豆与棉布货币体系

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为住房、衣物、工具、土地产出权、玉米储备与家庭劳力；城市商人、成功战士与贵族则积累棉布、可可、羽毛、宝石与奴隶等高价值财物
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was expressed mainly in housing, clothing, tools, rights to agricultural output, maize stores, and family labor; urban merchants, successful warriors, and nobles accumulated higher-value assets such as cotton cloth, cacao, feathers, gemstones, and slaves

unit.en: 1990 international dollars per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 国家朝贡与市场交换并行的再分配经济。帝国并非统一征收货币税，而是通过附庸贡赋、劳役、战利品和受监管的地方市场网络来组织资源流动。
economicSystem.en: A redistributive economy combining state tribute with market exchange. The empire did not rely on uniform monetary taxation; instead it organized resource flows through subject tribute, labor service, war booty, and regulated local market networks.

source.zh: 核心地区劳役与实物征收
source.en: Labor and in-kind levies from the core region

source.zh: 市场税费与交易监管收入
source.en: Market dues and trade regulation income

category.zh: 王廷、贵族赏赐与同盟分配
category.en: Royal court, noble rewards, and alliance distributions

category.zh: 仓储、公共工程与水利维护
category.en: Storehouses, public works, and hydraulic maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要不是金银铸币储备，而是衣料、粮食、可可、羽毛、宝石、军械与礼器等可再分配财富的集中仓储；贵金属在象征和礼仪上的意义大于货币意义
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not primarily a reserve of coined gold or silver, but concentrated storehouses of redistributable wealth such as cloth, food, cacao, feathers, gemstones, arms, and ceremonial goods; precious metals had greater symbolic and ritual than monetary importance

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调通过军事扩张与威慑维持贡赋网络，通过大型祭仪和赏赐巩固政治忠诚，并以仓储体系缓冲歉收和战争需求
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining the tribute network through military expansion and deterrence, reinforcing loyalty through large rituals and rewards, and using storehouse systems to cushion poor harvests and wartime demands

description.zh: 由特诺奇蒂特兰及核心盟邦的精锐战士组成，负责王都防卫、宫廷护卫与快速出征
description.en: Elite warriors from Tenochtitlan and the core allies, responsible for capital defense, palace guard duties, and rapid deployment

description.zh: 战时由各阿尔特佩特尔按义务提供的主力部队，人数众多，装备与训练水平差异较大
description.en: The main wartime force supplied by altepetl polities according to obligation, numerous but uneven in equipment and training

name.zh: 鹰武士与豹武士等精锐战团
name.en: Elite Orders such as Eagle and Jaguar Warriors

description.zh: 由高军功战士构成，承担突破、俘获敌军与指挥示范任务，是军事荣誉制度的核心
description.en: Composed of highly decorated veterans, they carried out shock actions, captive-taking, and exemplary battlefield leadership, forming the core of the martial honor system

name.en: Logistics, Canoe Transport, and Labor Corps

description.zh: 负责粮秣、道路、堤道、湖上运输、攻城辅助与军队随行供给
description.en: Handled provisions, roads, causeways, lake transport, siege support, and marching supply for the armies

commanderInChief.zh: 阿维索特尔大特拉托阿尼
commanderInChief.en: Huey Tlatoani Ahuitzotl

technology.zh: 军队以黑曜石刃木剑、矛、投矛器、弓箭、棉甲、盾牌和仪式化战服为主，擅长大规模步兵作战、围攻、堤道与湖区机动，但不具欧亚式骑兵、铁器或火药武器
technology.en: The army relied on obsidian-edged swords, spears, atlatls, bows, cotton armor, shields, and ritualized war costumes, excelling in mass infantry warfare, sieges, causeway operations, and lake-zone mobility, but lacking Eurasian-style cavalry, iron weapons, or gunpowder arms

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自宿敌普雷佩查王国、东南部尚未完全臣服的瓦哈卡与海湾地带势力、边缘地区反叛，以及帝国扩张过快所引发的广泛怨恨
threats.en: Major threats came from the rival Purépecha state, incompletely subdued powers in Oaxaca and the Gulf region, frontier rebellions, and broad resentment caused by rapid imperial expansion

populationDescription.zh: 约六百万人口分布于墨西哥谷地、中部高原及其附庸地区，人口密度在湖区与高地农业核心尤高；首都所在盆地是全中美洲最密集、最富庶的人口中心之一
populationDescription.en: About 6 million people were distributed across the Valley of Mexico, the central highlands, and tributary regions, with especially high density in the lake zone and agricultural corelands; the basin of the capital was among the densest and wealthiest population centers in Mesoamerica

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括王族与世袭贵族、祭司、职业商人波奇特卡、战士团体、平民马塞瓦尔廷、附庸社群、农民工匠以及奴隶特拉科廷。阶层可通过军功、婚姻与政治赏赐有限流动，但总体仍高度等级化。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata included royal and hereditary nobles, priests, professional pochteca merchants, warrior orders, commoner macehualtin, subject communities, peasant artisans, and tlacotin slaves. Some limited mobility existed through military achievement, marriage, and political reward, but society remained strongly hierarchical.

allies.zh: 正式盟友为特斯科科与特拉科潘；部分附庸城邦在利益与威慑下与帝国合作，远方商贸网络也与玛雅和海湾地区政权保持务实联系
allies.en: Formal allies were Texcoco and Tlacopan; some tributary city-states cooperated under interest and coercion, and long-distance commercial networks maintained pragmatic ties with Maya and Gulf polities

enemies.zh: 主要敌对势力是普雷佩查文明，以及特拉斯卡拉等拒绝臣服或频繁对抗的中部墨西哥政体；部分被征服地区长期存在潜在叛乱倾向
enemies.en: The principal hostile power was the Purépecha civilization, along with central Mexican polities such as Tlaxcala that refused submission or frequently opposed the empire; many conquered regions also harbored long-term rebellious tendencies

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以征服、威慑、朝贡、联姻、示威性祭仪与商贸渗透并用，目标是扩大贡赋来源、控制交通节点并维护特诺奇蒂特兰在中美洲的霸权地位
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy combined conquest, intimidation, tribute, marriage alliances, demonstrative ritual, and commercial penetration, aiming to expand tribute sources, control transit nodes, and preserve Tenochtitlan's hegemonic position in Mesoamerica

era.zh: 文艺复兴时代

keyInnovations.zh: 人工浮园高强度农业、湖区堤道与引水工程、双历法体系、精细市场管理、黑曜石工艺、羽毛镶嵌、彩绘抄本与大型神庙建筑体现了其技术与组织能力；但技术路径独立于旧大陆，未发展铁冶金、轮式运输的军事化运用与火药体系
keyInnovations.en: Intensive chinampa agriculture, lacustrine causeway and aqueduct engineering, dual calendrical systems, sophisticated market regulation, obsidian craftsmanship, feather mosaic work, painted codices, and monumental temple architecture demonstrated major technical and organizational capacity; however, its technological path remained independent of the Old World and did not develop iron metallurgy, militarized wheeled transport, or gunpowder systems

strengths.zh: 帝国拥有中美洲最强大的首都、极高的农业生产率、成熟的贡赋网络、庞大人口基础、强烈的军事文化与发达的市场体系，足以在1500年前后维持地区霸权
strengths.en: The empire possessed the strongest capital in Mesoamerica, very high agricultural productivity, a mature tribute network, a large demographic base, a powerful martial culture, and advanced market institutions, enabling it to maintain regional hegemony around 1500

weaknesses.zh: 政权高度依赖对外征服与附庸贡赋，核心统治带有压迫性，许多被征服社群忠诚薄弱；同时缺乏骑兵、铁器与火药，军事与疾病生态对跨洋接触极为脆弱
weaknesses.en: The regime depended heavily on external conquest and tributary extraction, and its rule was often coercive, leaving many conquered communities weakly loyal; it also lacked cavalry, iron, and gunpowder, making its military system and disease ecology extremely vulnerable to transoceanic contact

outlook.zh: 在1500年时，阿维索特尔治下的纳瓦帝国仍处于扩张与繁荣顶点，短期内前景强劲；但其结构性脆弱性——特别是附庸不满与技术隔绝——意味着一旦遭遇罕见的外来冲击，霸权可能迅速动摇
outlook.en: In 1500 under Ahuitzotl, the Nahua empire stood near the peak of expansion and prosperity, with a strong short-term outlook; yet its structural vulnerabilities—especially tributary discontent and technological isolation—meant that under an unusual external shock, its hegemony could be shaken rapidly

description.zh: 阿兹特克三邦同盟支配墨西哥谷地与中部高原，是中美洲最强政权。
description.en: The Aztec Triple Alliance dominated the Valley of Mexico and central highlands and was the strongest polity in Mesoamerica.

capital.zh: 无统一首都；玛雅潘已陷落，主要中心分散于马尼、索图塔、伊察诸地
capital.en: No single capital; Mayapan had fallen, with major centers dispersed among Mani, Sotuta, and Itza domains

governmentForm.zh: 分裂的城邦与族群政体体系，由世袭王权、贵族议事和地方宗教权威共同运作
governmentForm.en: A fragmented system of city-states and ethnic polities, governed through hereditary kingship, noble councils, and local religious authority

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为王族与贵族、祭司、武士、商人、工匠、农民及附庸与奴隶，各地结构相似但因地区而异
socialStructure.en: Society was divided into royal families and nobles, priests, warriors, merchants, artisans, commoner farmers, and dependents or slaves, with similar but regionally varied structures

rulingClass.zh: 地方王族、贵族家族、祭司领袖与军事首领
rulingClass.en: Local royal lineages, noble houses, priestly leaders, and military chiefs

succession.zh: 通常为父系世袭，但在内战、联盟重组与外部压力下常出现兄弟继承、旁支夺位或贵族拥立
succession.en: Usually patrilineal hereditary succession, though civil strife, alliance shifts, and external pressure often produced succession by brothers, collateral branches, or noble acclamation

structure.zh: 1500年时玛雅地区并无统一国家，尤卡坦半岛与危地马拉低地、高地分布着多个库奇卡巴尔、城邦与部族性领地。每一政体通常由一位阿豪或哈拉赫·乌伊尼克统治，下设贵族家臣、贡赋征收者、祭司与军事首领。
structure.en: By 1500 the Maya region was not a unified state; the Yucatan Peninsula and the Guatemalan lowlands and highlands were divided among multiple kuchkabals, city-states, and chiefly territories. Each polity was typically ruled by an ajaw or halach uinic, supported by noble retainers, tribute collectors, priests, and military leaders.

function.zh: 管理王室礼仪、继承、外交接待与宫廷土地
function.en: Managed dynastic ritual, succession, diplomatic reception, and palace lands

function.zh: 征收玉米、棉布、可可、蜂蜜、盐和劳役，并分配至宫廷、军队与祭祀中心
function.en: Collected maize, cotton cloth, cacao, honey, salt, and labor service, and redistributed them to the court, military, and ritual centers

function.zh: 主持宗教仪式、占卜、历法计算、节庆安排与圣地维护
function.en: Oversaw religious ceremonies, divination, calendrical reckoning, festival scheduling, and maintenance of sacred sites

function.zh: 组织战士、堡寨、袭击、俘虏征集与边境防务
function.en: Organized warriors, fortified settlements, raids, captive taking, and frontier defense

function.zh: 联络乡村首领、安排劳役、调解土地与水源纠纷
function.en: Liaised with village headmen, organized labor drafts, and mediated disputes over land and water

function.zh: 维护市场秩序、征收通行与交易税，并保护长途商路
function.en: Maintained market order, collected transit and market dues, and protected long-distance trade routes

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以城镇中心和村社为基础，由巴塔卜等地方贵族、村长和家族长老执行统治。政治权力高度地方化，外围村落多通过贡赋、婚盟与军事依附关系联系中心城镇。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was based on town centers and village communities, governed by batabs and other local nobles, village headmen, and lineage elders. Political authority was highly localized, and outlying settlements were tied to central towns through tribute, marriage alliances, and military dependence.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依据习惯法、王室裁断和宗教规范。谋杀、叛乱、通奸、盗窃和贡赋逃避可受严厉惩罚，赔偿、奴役、处死和公开羞辱并行。
legalSystem.en: Law rested primarily on customary norms, royal judgment, and religious sanction. Murder, rebellion, adultery, theft, and tribute evasion could incur severe penalties, including compensation, enslavement, execution, and public humiliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以实物贡赋和劳役为主，包括玉米、豆类、棉布、盐、蜂蜜、蜡、可可、羽毛与建筑劳役；沿海与商贸中心还征收市场费和通行费。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied chiefly on payment in kind and labor service, including maize, beans, cotton cloth, salt, honey, wax, cacao, feathers, and construction labor; coastal and commercial centers also collected market dues and transit tolls.

religion.zh: 玛雅宗教为多神信仰，崇拜伊察姆纳、库库尔坎、恰克等神祇，重视祖先崇拜、圣井、祭仪、占卜与历法。1500年时基督教尚未深入大多数玛雅政体。
religion.en: Maya religion was polytheistic, venerating deities such as Itzamna, Kukulkan, and Chaac, with strong emphasis on ancestor worship, sacred cenotes, ritual offerings, divination, and calendrical observance. By 1500 Christianity had not yet penetrated most Maya polities.

philosophy.zh: 其思想核心强调宇宙循环、时间神圣性、人与神灵间的互惠，以及王权通过祭祀维系宇宙秩序的责任。
philosophy.en: Its core worldview emphasized cosmic cycles, the sacred nature of time, reciprocity between humans and gods, and the duty of rulership to sustain cosmic order through ritual.

writingSystem.zh: 使用玛雅象形文字传统，但到1500年其完整碑铭传统已较古典时代衰落；同时广泛采用口传传统、图像记录与少量抄本书写。
writingSystem.en: The Maya employed a hieroglyphic writing tradition, though by 1500 its full monumental inscriptional use had declined from the Classic era; oral transmission, pictorial record, and limited codical writing remained important.

culturalAchievements.zh: 在历法、天文观测、金字塔与庙宇建筑、壁画、彩陶、羽饰工艺、织物和口述史诗方面成就显著；《波波尔·乌》等高地传统反映了深厚的神话与历史记忆。
culturalAchievements.en: The Maya excelled in calendrics, astronomical observation, pyramid and temple construction, mural painting, polychrome ceramics, featherwork, weaving, and oral epic traditions; highland works such as the Popol Vuh tradition reflect a deep reservoir of myth and historical memory.

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系，包含尤卡坦玛雅语、基切语、卡克其克尔语、策尔塔尔语等多种地方语言
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family, including Yucatec Maya, K'iche', Kaqchikel, Tzeltal, and many other regional languages

gdpDescription.zh: 玛雅经济以自给农业为基础，辅以区域贸易和贡赋再分配。由于缺乏统一国家、货币标准和完整统计，经济总量只能以跨文化白银等值粗略估计，整体低于同时代大型欧亚帝国，但在中美洲仍具重要区域性活力。
gdpDescription.en: The Maya economy rested on subsistence agriculture, supplemented by regional trade and tribute redistribution. Owing to the lack of a unified state, standardized currency, and comprehensive records, total output can only be estimated roughly in cross-cultural silver equivalents; it was smaller than that of large contemporary Eurasian empires but remained regionally significant within Mesoamerica.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、棉花生产、盐业、蜂蜜与蜂蜡、可可贸易、陶器、纺织、石器与建筑
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and squash cultivation, cotton production, salt making, honey and beeswax, cacao trade, pottery, textiles, stoneworking, and construction

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、可可豆、棉布、玉米、黑曜石、玉石、羽毛、蜂蜜、蜡、陶器、鱼干与海产
tradeGoods.en: Salt, cacao beans, cotton cloth, maize, obsidian, jade, feathers, honey, wax, pottery, dried fish, and marine products

name.zh: 可可豆与棉布货币体系
name.en: Cacao Bean and Cotton Cloth Currency System

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、住房、工具、衣物、储粮、家禽和社会关系，而非金银积累；贵族则控制土地、贡赋和奢侈品网络。
householdWealth.en: For most households, wealth consisted chiefly of land-use rights, dwellings, tools, clothing, stored food, poultry, and social ties rather than precious-metal accumulation; nobles controlled land, tribute, and luxury-goods networks.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业、地方市场交换、长途商人流通与贵族贡赋再分配并存的混合经济体系
economicSystem.en: A mixed economic system combining village agriculture, local market exchange, long-distance merchant circulation, and aristocratic tribute redistribution

source.zh: 盐业、可可与沿海贸易收益
source.en: Salt, Cacao, and Coastal Trade Revenues

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备多为分散保存在各城邦宫廷、仓储设施和神庙中的粮食、布匹、可可、盐和奢侈品，而非集中金属库存。因持续战争与政局分裂，财政余裕有限。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were held largely in dispersed palace, storehouse, and temple stocks of grain, cloth, cacao, salt, and luxury goods rather than as concentrated metal reserves. Ongoing warfare and political fragmentation kept fiscal margins narrow.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持宫廷威望、战争能力和祭祀开支为核心，倾向通过提高劳役和实物贡赋应对危机，而非形成统一税制改革。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on sustaining court prestige, war-making capacity, and ritual expenditure, tending to answer crises by intensifying labor drafts and in-kind tribute rather than through unified tax reform.

description.zh: 由王族、贵族及其随从组成，是各城邦核心作战力量，负责突袭、决战与俘虏获取。
description.en: Composed of royals, nobles, and their retainers, these formed the core combat force of the city-states and were used for raids, pitched fighting, and captive taking.

description.zh: 由村社和附属聚落按需征召，装备较简陋，以长矛、投枪、弓箭和棉甲为主。
description.en: Raised as needed from village communities and subordinate settlements, these troops were more lightly equipped, relying on spears, darts, bows, and cotton armor.

description.zh: 负责防守城寨、道路节点、海岸盐场和边境要地，兼具警戒与治安功能。
description.en: These forces guarded fortified towns, road nodes, coastal salt works, and frontier positions, serving both defensive and policing functions.

description.zh: 活跃于尤卡坦沿海、河口与湖区，用于运输、伏击、登陆和护卫贸易航线。
description.en: Active along the Yucatan coast, estuaries, and lake zones, these units were used for transport, ambush, landing actions, and trade-route escort.

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦阿豪及其任命的战首
commanderInChief.en: Each polity's ajaw and his appointed war captains

technology.zh: 武器以黑曜石刃器、木矛、弓箭、投枪器、石刀和棉甲为主；善于利用堡寨、堤道、森林与湿地地形。尚未拥有本土火器与骑兵，面对未来西班牙入侵将在技术上处于不利地位。
technology.en: Weaponry centered on obsidian-edged arms, wooden spears, bows, atlatls, stone blades, and cotton armor; Maya forces were adept at using fortifications, causeways, forests, and wetlands. They possessed neither indigenous firearms nor cavalry and would be technologically disadvantaged against impending Spanish invasion.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自玛雅内部长期城邦战争、尤卡坦诸派系冲突、危地马拉高地族群竞争，以及即将到来的西班牙海上入侵与疾病传播。
threats.en: Major threats came from chronic inter-Maya warfare, factional conflict among Yucatan polities, competition in the Guatemalan highlands, and the approaching Spanish maritime invasion and spread of disease.

populationDescription.zh: 1500年玛雅总人口约两百万，分布于尤卡坦半岛、佩滕低地、恰帕斯部分地区和危地马拉高地。人口较古典时代若干核心区有所转移，但仍保持广泛而连续的玛雅聚居带。
populationDescription.en: Around 1500 the total Maya population was about two million, distributed across the Yucatan Peninsula, the Peten lowlands, parts of Chiapas, and the Guatemalan highlands. Population had shifted from some Classic-era core zones, but a broad and continuous Maya settlement belt remained.

name.zh: 奇琴伊察遗址地区聚居点

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族与贵族，其次为祭司、军事首领和富商；中层包括工匠、地方管理者与专业商人；下层为广大农民、劳役承担者、附庸和奴隶。不同地区的家族与族群身份对社会地位影响很大。
socialClasses.en: At the top were royal and noble lineages, followed by priests, military leaders, and wealthy merchants; middle ranks included artisans, local administrators, and professional traders; the lower strata consisted of common farmers, labor-service bearers, dependents, and slaves. Lineage and ethnic identity strongly affected status in different regions.

allies.zh: 无稳定统一盟友体系；各玛雅政体通常通过婚盟、共同敌对关系和短期军事协定形成临时联盟
allies.en: There was no stable unified alliance system; Maya polities typically formed temporary alliances through marriage ties, common enemies, and short-term military agreements

enemies.zh: 主要敌人为彼此竞争的玛雅城邦与高地诸政体；在大西洋一侧，西班牙势力已开始接近加勒比与中美洲海域，构成新兴外部威胁
enemies.en: Their principal enemies were rival Maya city-states and highland polities; on the Atlantic side, Spanish power was beginning to approach the Caribbean and Central American seas, creating a new external threat

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以地方生存和均势为核心，通过联姻、贡纳、贸易联系、俘虏交换和报复性战争维持权力关系，缺乏跨玛雅世界的一体化战略。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on local survival and balance-of-power politics, using marriage, tribute, trade ties, prisoner exchange, and retaliatory warfare to manage power relations, with no integrated strategy across the Maya world.

era.zh: 文艺复兴时代

keyInnovations.zh: 延续成熟的历法计算、石造与灰泥建筑、水利与蓄水设施、棉甲制造、海岸独木舟航行和区域市场网络；但相较欧亚同时代，缺乏铁器、火药兵器、轮式运输畜力化应用和远洋帆船。
keyInnovations.en: The Maya maintained mature calendrical calculation, stone-and-stucco construction, water-management and storage systems, cotton armor production, coastal canoe navigation, and regional market networks; compared with contemporary Eurasia, however, they lacked iron metallurgy, gunpowder weaponry, practical wheeled transport with draft animals, and oceangoing sailing ships.

strengths.zh: 人口基础稳固，农业适应多样生态区，地方认同强烈，商业网络覆盖内陆与海岸，宗教文化传统深厚，并具丰富的战争与联盟经验。
strengths.en: Its strengths included a solid population base, agriculture adapted to diverse ecological zones, strong local identities, commercial networks linking inland and coastal zones, deep religious-cultural traditions, and extensive experience in warfare and alliance-making.

weaknesses.zh: 最大弱点是长期分裂、城邦竞争激烈、缺乏统一军事指挥与财政整合，技术上没有火器、钢铁和骑兵，且对旧大陆疾病毫无免疫准备。
weaknesses.en: Its greatest weaknesses were chronic fragmentation, intense city-state rivalry, lack of unified military command and fiscal integration, technological absence of firearms, steel, and cavalry, and complete vulnerability to Old World diseases.

outlook.zh: 1500年的玛雅世界仍具活力，但正处于脆弱转折点。若无跨地区统一，未来数十年将难以抵御西班牙征服、传教和瘟疫冲击；不过在偏远森林、高地与湖区，玛雅政体和文化仍可能长期顽强延续。
outlook.en: The Maya world in 1500 remained vigorous but stood at a fragile turning point. Without regional unification, it would struggle over the coming decades to resist Spanish conquest, missionary intrusion, and epidemic shock; nevertheless, in remote forests, highlands, and lake regions, Maya polities and culture could endure tenaciously for a long time.

description.zh: 尤卡坦与危地马拉地区由多个玛雅城邦和族群政权分治。
description.en: Yucatán and Guatemala were divided among numerous Maya city-states and ethnic polities.

governmentForm.zh: 高度集权的神权君主制与再分配型帝国行政体系
governmentForm.en: Highly centralized sacral monarchy with a redistributive imperial administrative system

socialStructure.zh: 社会以阿尤共同体为基础，自上而下分为萨帕·印卡及王族、贵族与省级长官、祭司与军事将领、地方库拉卡、专业工匠与国家劳役人口、广大农民与牧民。帝国通过亲族、贡赋、劳役与国家仓储将各地整合起来。
socialStructure.en: Society was built on the ayllu communal system and ranked from the Sapa Inca and royal kin, to nobles and provincial governors, priests and military commanders, local kurakas, specialized craftsmen and state laborers, and the broad mass of farmers and herders. The empire integrated diverse regions through kinship, tribute, labor obligations, and state storehouses.

rulingClass.zh: 印卡王族、库斯科世袭贵族、因功受封的地方精英以及受国家控制的高级祭司与军事首领
rulingClass.en: The Inca royal lineage, hereditary Cusco nobility, rewarded provincial elites, and senior priests and military leaders under state control

succession.zh: 名义上由王族内部继承，通常由在世君主在嫡系与有能力的王子中选择继承人，并需获得库斯科贵族与宗族的支持；并非严格长子继承。
succession.en: Succession was nominally dynastic within the royal lineage, usually determined by the reigning ruler among legitimate and capable sons, with support from Cusco nobles and royal kin groups; it was not strict primogeniture.

structure.zh: 帝国以库斯科为核心，由萨帕·印卡统治，全国划分为四大行省即“四方之地”，并向下设省、县级与阿尤共同体单位。行政与军事高度结合，依靠道路、驿站、结绳记事和层级官员进行治理。
structure.en: The empire was centered on Cusco and ruled by the Sapa Inca. It was organized into the four great quarters of Tawantinsuyu, further divided into provinces, lower districts, and ayllu communities. Administration and military authority were closely linked and operated through roads, relay stations, quipu record-keeping, and a tiered hierarchy of officials.

function.zh: 管理钦查苏尤、安蒂苏尤、孔蒂苏尤和科亚苏尤四大区域的征税、征役、治安、道路和地方精英监督
function.en: Oversaw taxation, labor drafts, security, roads, and supervision of local elites in the four quarters of Chinchaysuyu, Antisuyu, Kuntisuyu, and Qullasuyu

name.zh: 仓储与再分配管理机构

function.zh: 负责帝国粮仓、纺织品、武器、工具和救荒物资的征集、登记与调拨
function.en: Managed collection, registration, and redistribution of grain, textiles, weapons, tools, and famine reserves in imperial storehouses

function.zh: 维护印加大道、桥梁、驿站和传令体系，保障军队与官员调动
function.en: Maintained the Inca road system, bridges, relay stations, and message network, ensuring movement of armies and officials

name.zh: 结绳记事与人口土地统计机构

function.zh: 负责人口、土地、劳役、仓储和贡赋数据的记录与核对
function.en: Recorded and verified data on population, land, labor obligations, storehouses, and tribute

name.zh: 宗教礼仪与太阳神庙管理机构
name.en: Religious Rites and Sun Temple Administration

function.zh: 主持国家祭祀、历法礼仪、圣地管理及与王权相关的宗教合法性维护
function.en: Directed state rituals, calendrical ceremonies, sacred site management, and the religious legitimacy of royal authority

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依靠被帝国承认的库拉卡与十户、五十户、百户、五百户、千户等层级官员。国家通常保留地方首领，但要求其提供劳役、兵员与贡赋，并接受人口迁徙与政治监督。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied chiefly on imperial-recognized kurakas and decimal officials overseeing units of ten, fifty, one hundred, five hundred, and one thousand households. The state usually retained local chiefs but required labor, troops, and tribute from them, while subjecting them to resettlement policies and political oversight.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命和地方规制结合而成，强调秩序、服从与公共义务。盗窃、叛乱、逃役和破坏国家财产处罚严厉；审判多由地方与省级官员执行，重在迅速裁决而非成文法典。
legalSystem.en: Law combined customary norms, royal commands, and local regulations, emphasizing order, obedience, and public obligation. Theft, rebellion, labor evasion, and damage to state property were punished severely. Adjudication was handled mainly by local and provincial authorities, with stress on swift decision rather than a formal written code.

taxationSystem.zh: 帝国主要通过米塔劳役税而非货币税获取资源。臣民按人口与土地承担农业、筑路、采矿、服兵役、运输和工艺生产等义务，产出进入国家仓储后再分配给军队、官员、祭祀与灾荒救济。
taxationSystem.en: The empire drew resources primarily through the mita labor tax rather than monetary taxation. Subjects owed agricultural work, road building, mining, military service, transport, and craft production according to population and landholdings, with outputs entering state storehouses for redistribution to armies, officials, religious institutions, and famine relief.

religion.zh: 以太阳神因蒂崇拜为国家核心宗教，同时尊奉维拉科查、月神、雷神及各地瓦卡圣物与祖先崇拜。国家宗教具有强烈整合功能，允许地方信仰在帝国框架内并存。
religion.en: State religion centered on the worship of Inti the sun god, while also venerating Viracocha, the moon, thunder deities, local huacas, and ancestral cults. Imperial religion had a strongly integrative role and allowed local cults to persist within an imperial framework.

philosophy.zh: 印加思想并非抽象哲学传统，而是以宇宙秩序、祖先权威、互惠义务、等级和共同体责任为核心。治国理念强调平衡、服从、再分配与神圣王权。
philosophy.en: Inca thought was not an abstract philosophical tradition in the Eurasian sense but centered on cosmic order, ancestral authority, reciprocal obligation, hierarchy, and communal duty. Statecraft emphasized balance, obedience, redistribution, and sacred kingship.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟表音文字；国家主要依赖结绳记事系统与口述传统记录行政、贡赋、人口和历史信息
writingSystem.en: No fully developed phonetic writing system; the state relied primarily on quipu recording and oral tradition to preserve administrative, tribute, census, and historical information

culturalAchievements.zh: 成就包括高山梯田农业、巨石建筑、道路与悬索桥网络、精密仓储体系、纺织艺术、金银工艺以及对多民族帝国礼仪的整合。库斯科及其神庙、萨克塞瓦曼等建筑展现出强大的组织能力与工程水平。
culturalAchievements.en: Achievements included high-altitude terrace agriculture, monumental stone architecture, road and suspension bridge networks, sophisticated storehouse systems, textile arts, gold and silver work, and the ceremonial integration of a multiethnic empire. Cusco, its temples, and sites such as Sacsayhuaman demonstrated remarkable organizational and engineering capacity.

languageFamily.zh: 官方整合语言主要为克丘亚语，属于克丘亚语系；帝国内并存艾马拉语及众多地方语言
languageFamily.en: The principal integrative language was Quechua, of the Quechuan family; Aymara and many local languages also coexisted across the empire

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 帝国经济规模在美洲属顶尖，但主要是非货币化、以实物和劳役再分配为基础。估值只能折算为白银等价，不能与欧亚货币经济完全对比。其强项在于组织能力、粮食储备、道路运输和动员劳力，而非市场化金融。
gdpDescription.en: The imperial economy was one of the largest in the Americas, but it was primarily non-monetized and based on goods and labor redistribution. Estimates can only be expressed in silver equivalent and are not fully comparable with Eurasian money economies. Its strengths lay in organization, food reserves, road transport, and labor mobilization rather than market finance.

mainIndustries.zh: 高山农业、梯田灌溉、驼类畜牧、纺织、陶器、建筑、道路维护、采矿与金银工艺
mainIndustries.en: Highland agriculture, terrace irrigation, camelid herding, textiles, pottery, construction, road maintenance, mining, and gold and silver craftsmanship

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、马铃薯、藜麦、羊驼与羊驼毛织物、可可叶、盐、干肉、陶器、铜器、金银饰品、羽毛与热带特产
tradeGoods.en: Maize, potatoes, quinoa, llama and alpaca wool textiles, coca leaves, salt, dried meat, pottery, copper goods, gold and silver ornaments, feathers, and tropical specialty products

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、亲族劳力、储粮、纺织品、家畜和与国家仓储体系的联系，而非金银积蓄。国家在灾年可提供一定保障，但地方差异明显。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in access to farmland, kin labor, stored food, textiles, livestock, and ties to the state storehouse system rather than bullion savings. The state provided some security in bad years, though local conditions varied considerably.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以国家计划性再分配和共同体生产为主，市场交换存在但处于次要地位。帝国通过劳役征发、仓储调配和人口迁徙实现区域整合。
economicSystem.en: The economy was dominated by state-directed redistribution and communal production, with market exchange present but secondary. The empire integrated regions through labor drafts, storehouse distribution, and managed population resettlement.

source.en: Regional specialty tribute and subordinate contributions

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓国库主要不是集中铸币，而是分布于全帝国的粮仓、纺织品仓、武器库、牲畜群及贵金属制品储备。其价值很大程度上体现在可调用的实物和劳力，而非流通现金。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not chiefly a concentrated coin reserve but a distributed network of granaries, textile depots, arsenals, herds, and precious metal holdings across the empire. Its value lay largely in mobilizable goods and labor rather than circulating cash.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持仓储充足、保障军需和道路系统、并通过再分配巩固忠诚为目标。国家倾向于积累实物储备，在扩张与赈灾时统一调拨。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to keep storehouses full, sustain military needs and the road system, and reinforce loyalty through redistribution. The state preferred to accumulate real goods and allocate them centrally for expansion and relief.

description.zh: 以长矛、投石器、战棍、斧和盾为主，是帝国军队主体，来自各地征发部众
description.en: Armed mainly with spears, slings, clubs, axes, and shields, this was the core of the imperial army and was raised from levies across the empire

name.zh: 精锐近卫与库斯科部队

description.zh: 由王室近卫、库斯科贵族战士和经验丰富的精锐单位组成，负责核心作战与护卫萨帕·印卡
description.en: Composed of royal guards, Cusco noble warriors, and experienced elite units, responsible for key combat roles and protection of the Sapa Inca

description.zh: 负责修路、架桥、营地、搬运和补给，依托帝国道路和驮运系统保障远征
description.en: Handled road repair, bridge building, camp work, transport, and supplies, sustaining campaigns through the imperial road and pack system

description.zh: 驻守新征服地区、战略要道和边疆据点，兼具镇压叛乱与警戒外敌功能
description.en: Stationed in newly conquered regions, strategic corridors, and frontier posts, serving both counterinsurgency and border defense roles

commanderInChief.zh: 瓦伊纳·卡帕克，兼由王族将领与高阶阿普辅佐
commanderInChief.en: Huayna Capac, assisted by royal commanders and senior apu lords

technology.zh: 军事实力依赖大规模动员、山地行军能力、道路后勤、投石器与冷兵器作战。无铁器和火器，也无骑兵，但在安第斯地形中具极强组织优势。
technology.en: Military strength rested on mass mobilization, mountain campaigning ability, road logistics, slings, and shock weapons. It lacked iron arms, firearms, and cavalry, but possessed exceptional organizational advantages in Andean terrain.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方和东部边地整合困难、地方反抗、丛林地带控制有限，以及与基多地区和其他新并入族群的长期军事压力。1500年尚未面对欧洲征服，但海洋彼岸的新威胁即将出现。
threats.en: The main threats came from difficulties integrating northern and eastern frontier zones, local rebellions, limited control in jungle regions, and ongoing pressure in Quito and other newly incorporated areas. In 1500 the empire had not yet faced European conquest, though a transoceanic danger was approaching.

populationDescription.zh: 帝国人口约一千万，涵盖安第斯高地、沿海谷地和部分东坡地区的众多民族。人口集中于高原农业区和灌溉河谷，受国家迁徙政策影响，族群分布具有明显行政重组特征。
populationDescription.en: The empire held about 10 million people, encompassing many ethnic groups across the Andean highlands, coastal valleys, and parts of the eastern slopes. Population was concentrated in highland farming zones and irrigated valleys, and state resettlement policies gave ethnic distribution a strongly administrative character.

socialClasses.zh: 主要包括王族与库斯科贵族、地方库拉卡与附庸精英、祭司、军人、工匠、米塔劳役人口、普通阿尤农民牧民，以及被迁置的米特马克人口。社会等级明确，但国家也承担一定供养与灾荒调节职能。
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included the royal lineage and Cusco nobility, local kurakas and subordinate elites, priests, soldiers, craftsmen, mita laborers, ordinary ayllu farmers and herders, and resettled mitmaq populations. Social hierarchy was pronounced, though the state also assumed provisioning and famine-management roles.

allies.zh: 帝国无稳定意义上的对外盟友体系，主要通过附庸、婚姻、招抚和强制整合周边族群来维持秩序
allies.en: The empire had no stable foreign alliance system in the usual sense, relying instead on subordination, marriage ties, conciliation, and coercive incorporation of neighboring peoples

enemies.zh: 顽强抵抗的边疆族群、未完全纳入统治的安第斯和丛林地区部族，以及潜在叛乱的被征服地方精英
enemies.en: Resistant frontier peoples, unconquered tribes in Andean and jungle regions, and potentially rebellious conquered local elites

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以持续扩张、道路控制、人口迁置、保留地方首领并纳入贡役体系为核心。帝国倾向于先施以威慑和招抚，后通过驻军、宗教和行政重组巩固统治。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on continued expansion, control of roads, resettlement of populations, and retention of local rulers within the tribute-and-labor system. The empire tended to begin with intimidation and conciliation, then consolidate rule through garrisons, religion, and administrative reorganization.

era.zh: 文艺复兴时代

keyInnovations.zh: 山地梯田与灌溉工程、结绳记事行政技术、覆盖帝国的道路与驿传网络、悬索桥建造、巨石切配建筑、冻干食品保存和高海拔农业适应技术
keyInnovations.en: Mountain terracing and irrigation engineering, quipu-based administrative recording, empire-wide roads and relay communications, suspension bridge construction, fitted-stone architecture, freeze-dried food preservation, and high-altitude agricultural adaptation

strengths.zh: 国家组织力极强，能够在复杂地形下高效动员劳力与军队；仓储、道路和再分配体系提高了抗灾与征服能力；王权与宗教结合增强了统治合法性。
strengths.en: The state possessed exceptional organizational capacity, enabling efficient mobilization of labor and armies in difficult terrain. Its storehouse, road, and redistributive systems strengthened both resilience and conquest, while the union of kingship and religion reinforced legitimacy.

weaknesses.zh: 经济货币化和市场化程度低，跨区域信息依赖口传与结绳官僚；统治高度依赖君主权威与强制劳役；地方认同强烈，新征服地区容易不稳；缺乏铁器、火器、骑兵和海上能力。
weaknesses.en: The economy was weakly monetized and only lightly marketized, while long-distance information depended on oral transmission and quipu bureaucracy. Rule relied heavily on royal authority and compulsory labor; local identities remained strong, making newly conquered areas unstable. The empire also lacked iron weapons, firearms, cavalry, and maritime power.

outlook.zh: 1500年的印加帝国正处于鼎盛扩张期，短期前景强劲，尤其在北方和边疆整合方面仍有增长空间。然而其巨大版图、继承不确定性和对中央权威的依赖埋下隐患；若遭遇疾病传播、内战或陌生外来军事力量，帝国结构可能迅速承压。
outlook.en: In 1500 the Inca Empire was in a vigorous phase of expansion, with strong short-term prospects and room for further integration in the north and frontier zones. Yet its vast scale, uncertain succession, and dependence on central authority created vulnerabilities; if struck by disease, civil war, or unfamiliar foreign military forces, the imperial structure could come under rapid strain.

description.zh: 印加帝国正在扩张并整合安第斯山脉广大地区。
description.en: The Inca Empire was expanding and integrating vast regions of the Andes.

name.zh: 奇布查文明（穆伊斯卡联盟）

governmentForm.zh: 由巴卡塔的西帕与洪萨的萨克主导的松散双核心酋邦联盟，下辖诸属邦与地方酋长国
governmentForm.en: A loose dual-centered confederation of chiefdoms led by the Zipa of Bacatá and the Zaque of Hunza, with subordinate polities and local chiefdoms

socialStructure.zh: 社会分层明显，以统治家族、祭司、战士、工匠、商人、农民和劳役人口构成；亲族、贡赋与宗教义务共同维系秩序
socialStructure.en: Society was clearly stratified, consisting of ruling lineages, priests, warriors, artisans, traders, farmers, and laboring dependents; kinship, tribute, and religious obligations together sustained order

rulingClass.zh: 西帕、萨克、乌萨克与各级卡西克酋长家族，以及与其结盟的祭司贵族
rulingClass.en: The Zipa, the Zaque, uzaques and other cacique chiefly lineages, together with allied priestly nobles

succession.zh: 以王族与母系亲属网络中的继承传统为主，重要职位通常由统治家族内部或旁系贵族承接，但地方上也受实力与联盟关系影响
succession.en: Succession primarily followed traditions within royal and matrilineal kinship networks; major offices usually passed within ruling families or collateral noble lines, though local outcomes were also shaped by power and alliance ties

structure.zh: 联盟并非统一官僚国家，而是由西帕与萨克两大核心政权统合周边酋邦。地方由卡西克管理，中心通过贡赋、婚盟、军事威慑与宗教权威进行协调。
structure.en: The confederation was not a unitary bureaucratic state but a regional system in which the Zipa and Zaque integrated surrounding chiefdoms. Local caciques governed their own communities, while the centers coordinated through tribute, marriage alliances, military pressure, and religious authority.

function.zh: 征集棉布、盐、金器、祖母绿、粮食与劳役，并管理区域仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected cotton cloth, salt, goldwork, emeralds, foodstuffs, and labor dues, and managed regional storage and redistribution

function.zh: 主持神庙、祭祀、节庆、天象观察与农业时令安排
function.en: Oversaw temples, ceremonies, festivals, celestial observation, and the scheduling of agricultural seasons

function.zh: 动员战士、组织边境防御、协调对敌征讨及属邦兵役
function.en: Mobilized warriors, organized frontier defense, and coordinated campaigns and levies from subordinate polities

function.zh: 维系与各地酋长、附庸和盟友的关系，监督贡赋履行与纠纷调停
function.en: Maintained relations with local chiefs, vassals, and allies, and supervised tribute compliance and dispute mediation

function.zh: 管理盐泉、祖母绿矿区、金工生产与定期市集秩序
function.en: Managed salt springs, emerald mining zones, goldworking production, and order in periodic markets

name.zh: 道路、田地与公共劳役事务
name.en: Roads, Fields, and Public Labor Affairs

function.zh: 组织道路、梯田、排水、神庙与统治者设施的公共劳动
function.en: Organized public labor for roads, terraces, drainage, temples, and chiefly facilities

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以村社和区域酋长为核心。各卡西克向上对西帕或萨克纳贡，同时保留较强自治，负责征收实物、组织劳役和调解社区纠纷。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was centered on villages and regional caciques. Each cacique rendered tribute upward to the Zipa or Zaque while retaining substantial autonomy, collecting goods in kind, organizing labor, and mediating community disputes.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、酋长裁决与宗教规范为基础。重视对婚姻、土地使用、贡赋义务、盗窃和叛服行为的处罚，刑罚可包括财物赔偿、劳役、体罚乃至处死。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on customary law, chiefly adjudication, and religious norms. It emphasized regulation of marriage, land use, tribute obligations, theft, and disloyalty, with penalties ranging from compensation and labor to corporal punishment and execution.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要表现为贡赋与劳役，而非统一货币税。各地以盐、棉布、玉米、马铃薯、金器、祖母绿、可卡及手工制品纳贡，并在战争或大型祭祀时加派劳役。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation took the form chiefly of tribute and labor rather than standardized monetary taxes. Different areas paid in salt, cotton cloth, maize, potatoes, goldwork, emeralds, coca, and crafted goods, with additional labor obligations during war or major ritual events.

religion.zh: 穆伊斯卡宗教为多神信仰与祖灵崇拜体系，尊奉太阳神苏埃、月亮女神奇亚及与自然、湖泊、繁衍和秩序相关的诸神；圣湖与祭祀中心具有重要地位。
religion.en: Muisca religion was a polytheistic and ancestral cult system venerating Sué the sun deity, Chía the moon deity, and other gods associated with nature, lakes, fertility, and order; sacred lakes and ceremonial centers held great importance.

philosophy.zh: 其思想体系并非成文哲学传统，而是以宇宙秩序、祖先合法性、祭祀循环、农业时序与社会等级义务为核心的宗教—伦理世界观。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual framework was not a written philosophical tradition but a religious-ethical worldview centered on cosmic order, ancestral legitimacy, ritual cycles, agricultural timing, and obligations within social hierarchy.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字体系，知识主要通过口传、祭司记忆、仪式实践、象征物与工艺图像传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no fully developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, priestly memory, ritual practice, symbolic objects, and imagery in craftwork.

culturalAchievements.zh: 以精细金工、失蜡铸造、祖母绿采掘、盐业、织造、宗教圣地与复杂的区域交换网络著称；“黄金人”式圣湖献祭传统后来成为外界关于黄金国传说的重要源头之一。
culturalAchievements.en: They were noted for refined goldwork, lost-wax casting, emerald extraction, salt production, weaving, sacred ritual sites, and complex regional exchange networks; the sacred-lake offering tradition associated with the 'Golden Man' later became one of the principal sources of outside El Dorado legends.

languageFamily.zh: 奇布查语系
languageFamily.en: Chibchan language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高地农业、盐业、织造、金工和区域交换为基础，在北安第斯属高度发达的地方性经济体，但并非跨大陆商业帝国。估值为现代学界难以精确量化的折算值，仅用于相对比较。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on highland agriculture, salt production, weaving, goldworking, and regional exchange, making it one of the more developed local economies in the northern Andes, though not a transcontinental commercial empire. The estimate is a rough comparative conversion, as precise quantification is difficult in modern scholarship.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米、马铃薯、藜麦等农业；盐泉开采；棉纺织；金工与铜金合金工艺；祖母绿采掘；陶器与区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture in maize, potatoes, and quinoa; salt extraction; cotton textiles; gold and tumbaga metalwork; emerald mining; pottery; and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、棉布、祖母绿、金器、通巴加工艺品、可卡、羽毛、陶器、玉米、马铃薯与其他山地农产
tradeGoods.en: Salt, cotton cloth, emeralds, goldwork, tumbaga crafts, coca, feathers, pottery, maize, potatoes, and other highland produce

name.zh: 无统一铸币，以物易物与贡赋计值
name.en: No unified coinage; barter and tribute valuation

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、房舍、粮食储备、纺织工具和家族劳动力；精英财富则集中于金器、祖母绿、附庸贡赋与多妻家庭网络。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in access to fields, dwellings, food reserves, weaving tools, and family labor; elite wealth was concentrated in gold objects, emeralds, tributary rights, and extensive polygynous household networks.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业、酋长再分配、实物贡赋、定期市集和长短途交换并存的区域经济；国家控制有限，更多依赖权威中心与地方首领协调。
economicSystem.en: A regional economy combining village agriculture, chiefly redistribution, tribute in kind, periodic markets, and short- and medium-distance exchange; state control was limited, with coordination depending more on central authority and local chiefs.

source.en: Salt tribute and controlled production revenue

source.zh: 棉布、工艺品与手工业贡赋
祖母绿、金工及矿产收益

category.zh: 宫廷、酋长网络与再分配

category.en: Public works, roads, and water management

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非单一金属仓库，而是分散于首领中心、神庙与仓储点的贵重器物、粮食储备、盐货、织物和礼仪财物总和。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a single metallic hoard but the combined value of precious objects, food reserves, salt stocks, textiles, and ritual wealth distributed across chiefly centers, temples, and storehouses.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持首领权威、宗教合法性与再分配能力。通过稳定贡赋、控制盐与矿产要地，并在歉收或战争时调配仓储，整体政策偏保守稳健。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on preserving chiefly authority, religious legitimacy, and redistributive capacity. By maintaining regular tribute, controlling strategic salt and mineral zones, and reallocating stores in times of poor harvest or war, policy remained generally conservative and stable.

description.zh: 直属西帕、萨克及重要酋长的核心武装，用于护卫、威慑属邦和关键战斗。
description.en: Core forces attached directly to the Zipa, Zaque, and major chiefs, used for guard duty, tributary coercion, and decisive fighting.

description.zh: 由各地卡西克提供的主要兵力，装备长矛、棍棒、飞镖、投石器和盾牌，适合山地作战。
description.en: The main body of forces supplied by local caciques, armed with spears, clubs, darts, slings, and shields, suited for highland warfare.

description.zh: 负责山口警戒、通信、巡逻和对邻近敌对部族的快速反应。
description.en: Responsible for guarding passes, communications, patrols, and rapid reaction against neighboring hostile groups.

commanderInChief.zh: 由西帕或萨克亲自主军，地方则由各大酋长统兵
commanderInChief.en: The Zipa or Zaque personally headed major forces, while local chiefs commanded their own contingents

technology.zh: 军备以石、木、硬化木与少量金铜合金礼仪装备为主，无铁器、骑兵或火器；擅长高地地形运用、伏击和人力动员。
technology.en: Military equipment relied on stone, wood, hardened wood, and limited gold-copper alloy ceremonial gear, with no iron weaponry, cavalry, or firearms; they excelled in highland terrain use, ambush, and manpower mobilization.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与周边部族和邻近政体的区域战争、内部附庸反复以及双核心联盟间的权力竞争；1500年尚未直接面对西班牙征服，但数十年内将出现致命外来冲击。
threats.en: Main threats came from regional warfare with neighboring peoples and polities, recurring unrest among subordinates, and rivalry between the confederation’s two major centers; in 1500 it had not yet directly confronted the Spanish conquest, but a fatal external shock would emerge within decades.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于哥伦比亚东科迪勒拉高原及其谷地，尤其是昆迪博亚森斯高地，是北南美人口较稠密的农业区之一。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Eastern Cordillera highlands and valleys of present-day Colombia, especially the Cundiboyacense plateau, one of the more densely populated agricultural regions in northern South America.

name.zh: 伊拉卡宗教中心（苏阿莫克斯）

socialClasses.zh: 上层为西帕、萨克、乌萨克与地方酋长；其下为祭司、战士与富裕工匠商人；主体为自耕农与村社成员；另有服劳役者、俘虏与依附人口。社会地位受亲族、贡赋责任和宗教角色影响显著。
