socialClasses.en: At the top stood the Zipa, Zaque, uzaques, and local chiefs; beneath them were priests, warriors, and prosperous artisans and traders; the majority consisted of cultivators and village communities; below them were laboring dependents, captives, and attached populations. Social status was strongly shaped by kinship, tribute obligations, and religious role.

allies.zh: 联盟内部的附庸酋邦与阶段性结盟的高地社群；对外关系多为地区性、流动性和实用性，而非远距离固定盟约。
allies.en: Allies consisted mainly of subordinate chiefdoms within the confederation and periodically aligned highland communities; external relations were mostly regional, fluid, and pragmatic rather than fixed long-distance alliances.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者包括帕切人、潘切人及其他与穆伊斯卡高地政权争夺边境与贸易通道的邻近群体，也包括联盟内部反叛势力。
enemies.en: Principal enemies included the Panche and other neighboring groups contesting frontiers and trade routes with the Muisca highland polities, as well as rebellious elements within the confederation.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以控制高地资源区、维持贡赋网络、通过婚盟和威慑稳定属邦为核心。对远方世界并无直接认知，国际体系基本局限于北安第斯与邻近低地的区域互动。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling highland resource zones, maintaining tribute networks, and stabilizing subordinate polities through marriage alliances and deterrence. There was no direct awareness of distant Old World states; the effective international system was confined to regional interaction across the northern Andes and adjacent lowlands.

era.zh: 文艺复兴时代（就全球并时代而言）；本地技术处于前哥伦布时期北安第斯高地复杂酋邦阶段
era.en: Renaissance era in global contemporaneous terms; locally, a Late Pre-Columbian northern Andean complex chiefdom stage

keyInnovations.zh: 先进盐业加工、祖母绿开采、失蜡法金工、山地农业组织、纺织生产与适应高地环境的区域交换体系。
keyInnovations.en: Advanced salt processing, emerald mining, lost-wax metalworking, organized highland agriculture, textile production, and regional exchange systems adapted to mountainous environments.

strengths.zh: 人口密集、农业稳固、盐与祖母绿资源突出、金工精湛、地方交换网络发达，且宗教与贡赋体系足以支撑稳定的地区霸权。
strengths.en: Its strengths included dense population, solid agriculture, exceptional salt and emerald resources, sophisticated goldworking, and well-developed local exchange networks, while its religious and tributary systems were sufficient to sustain stable regional predominance.

weaknesses.zh: 政治结构松散、双核心权力分裂、缺乏统一文字和高度官僚制、军备技术有限，面对内部竞争和突发外来征服时协调能力不足。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a loose political structure, divided dual leadership, absence of writing and high bureaucracy, and limited military technology, leaving it poorly coordinated in the face of internal rivalry or sudden foreign conquest.

outlook.zh: 1500年前后整体仍然稳定并保持区域强势，但这种稳定高度依赖地方忠诚与传统权威。若仅面对本地竞争者，联盟可继续维持；然而在16世纪西班牙入侵、疫病传播与金银掠夺压力下，其秩序将迅速瓦解。
outlook.en: Around 1500 the polity remained broadly stable and regionally strong, but that stability depended heavily on local loyalty and traditional authority. Against only indigenous rivals, the confederation could likely continue; under 16th-century Spanish invasion, epidemic spread, and pressures of treasure extraction, however, its order would collapse rapidly.

description.zh: 穆伊斯卡联盟统治哥伦比亚高原，是北安第斯重要区域政体。
description.en: The Muisca Confederation ruled the Colombian highlands and was a major regional polity of northern South America.

dynasty.en: No dynasty; polity of the Five Nations Confederacy

governmentForm.zh: 易洛魁联盟是由莫霍克、奥奈达、奥农达加、卡尤加与塞内卡诸族组成的协商性联盟政体，以《大和平法》维系统合。
governmentForm.en: The Iroquois Confederacy was a consultative confederation of the Mohawk, Oneida, Onondaga, Cayuga, and Seneca peoples, integrated through the Great Law of Peace.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以母系氏族、长屋社区和村落联盟为核心组织。酋长、战首和长老并非绝对统治者，重要事务须通过氏族母亲、议事会与共识程序决定。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around matrilineal clans, longhouse communities, and village alliances. Sachems, war leaders, and elders were not absolute rulers; major issues were decided through clan mothers, councils, and consensus procedures.

rulingClass.zh: 联盟不存在严格意义上的世袭贵族阶层，但确有由氏族母亲推举的酋长与资深长老构成的政治核心。其权威主要来自声望、亲族纽带、口才和调解能力。
rulingClass.en: The confederacy lacked a strict hereditary nobility, but it did have a political core made up of sachems and senior elders selected through clan-mother authority. Their influence rested chiefly on prestige, kinship, oratory, and mediation.

succession.zh: 职位通常在特定母系氏族内部延续，由氏族母亲提名并经议事程序确认。若首领失职，也可被撤换。
succession.en: Office normally continued within designated matrilineal clans, with nominees chosen by clan mothers and confirmed through council procedure. Leaders who failed in their duties could also be removed.

structure.zh: 联盟政府以各族议事会和中央性的奥农达加火种议会为核心，决策强调平衡与一致。其行政规模远小于欧亚国家，没有常设官僚机器，而是由首领、使者与亲族网络执行公共事务。
structure.en: The confederate government centered on the councils of each nation and the central council fire at Onondaga, with decisions emphasizing balance and unanimity. Its administrative scale was far smaller than Eurasian states, lacking a standing bureaucracy and relying instead on leaders, messengers, and kin networks to carry out public affairs.

function.zh: 处理联盟外交、争端调解、战争与和平决策，并维系各族共识。
function.en: Handled confederate diplomacy, dispute mediation, decisions on war and peace, and maintenance of inter-nation consensus.

function.zh: 在各村落之间传递议会决定、组织会盟、召集战士与协调礼仪事务。
function.en: Transmitted council decisions among settlements, organized gatherings, summoned warriors, and coordinated ceremonial affairs.

localAdmin.zh: 地方事务由各村落长老、氏族母亲、酋长与战首共同处理。村落自治很强，联盟主要在跨族事务上发挥作用。
localAdmin.en: Local matters were handled jointly by village elders, clan mothers, sachems, and war leaders. Villages possessed strong autonomy, while the confederacy acted chiefly in inter-nation affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以口传传统、赔偿原则、议会调解和仪式性修复为主。《大和平法》为政治秩序和联盟关系提供规范框架。
legalSystem.en: Law relied on oral tradition, compensation, council mediation, and ritual restoration. The Great Law of Peace provided the constitutional framework for political order and confederate relations.

taxationSystem.zh: 联盟没有欧亚国家式常规税制，公共事务主要依靠氏族义务、礼物交换、集体储备和战利品再分配支持。战时动员更多依赖亲族责任而非货币征税。
taxationSystem.en: The confederacy had no regular Eurasian-style tax system; public affairs were supported through clan obligations, gift exchange, communal stores, and redistribution of spoils. Wartime mobilization relied more on kinship duties than on monetary taxation.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 易洛魁联盟最重要的成就在于构建了跨部族的和平联盟、成熟的议事制度与复杂的口传政治传统。长屋社会、农耕节庆、哀悼战争与贝珠带礼仪共同塑造其独特文化。
culturalAchievements.en: The confederacy's greatest achievement lay in creating a cross-tribal peace union, a mature council system, and a complex oral political tradition. Longhouse life, agricultural festivals, mourning-war practices, and wampum ceremonialism together shaped its distinctive culture.

languageFamily.zh: 易洛魁语系
languageFamily.en: Iroquoian language family

philosophy.zh: 《大和平法》、亲族平衡、互惠伦理与灵性自然观
philosophy.en: The Great Law of Peace, kinship balance, reciprocal ethics, and a spiritual view of nature

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟本土文字系统；政治法律和历史主要依赖口传、演说与贝珠带记忆编码
writingSystem.en: No fully developed indigenous writing system; law, history, and politics were preserved through oral tradition, formal speech, and wampum-based mnemonic encoding

unit.en: silver florin equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 联盟经济以玉米、豆类、南瓜农业为基础，辅以狩猎、渔猎、采集和地区交换。虽然货币化程度低，但其粮食生产、村落储备与区域互助足以支撑较稳定的人口与战时动员。
gdpDescription.en: The confederacy's economy was based on maize, beans, and squash agriculture, supplemented by hunting, fishing, gathering, and regional exchange. Though only lightly monetized, its food production, village storage, and regional reciprocity were sufficient to sustain a stable population and wartime mobilization.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、狩猎、皮毛处理、木工、陶器、渔猎与贝珠制作
mainIndustries.en: maize agriculture, hunting, hide processing, woodworking, pottery, fishing, and wampum production

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、兽皮、干鱼、石器、木制品、陶器、贝珠与礼物性物资
tradeGoods.en: maize, furs, dried fish, stone tools, wood goods, pottery, wampum, and gift goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为粮仓储备、长屋居住权、农地使用、工具、皮毛和亲族支持网络。财富差距存在，但通常不表现为欧亚式大规模私产集中。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through stored food, longhouse residence rights, access to fields, tools, furs, and kin support networks. Differences in wealth existed, but usually not as large concentrations of private property in the Eurasian sense.

unit.en: silver florin equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村落自给、母系分配、礼物交换和区域互惠为主的部族联盟经济。
economicSystem.en: A confederate tribal economy based on village subsistence, matrilineal allocation, gift exchange, and regional reciprocity.

treasuryDescription.zh: 联盟并无集中金属货币国库，其“财政储备”更接近贝珠、礼物物资、粮食公储与可动员资源的总和。储备规模不大，但在低官僚成本体制下足以维系常态运作。
treasuryDescription.en: The confederacy had no centralized metallic treasury; its 'treasury' was better understood as a pool of wampum, gift goods, food stores, and mobilizable resources. The reserve was modest but adequate for normal functioning in a low-bureaucracy system.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践强调维持公共储备、通过礼物与互惠巩固联盟关系，并在需要时迅速集中资源而非征收固定税。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized maintaining communal reserves, strengthening confederate ties through gifts and reciprocity, and concentrating resources quickly when needed rather than levying fixed taxes.

description.zh: 由各族成年男子在需要时集结，擅长伏击、突袭、追踪和森林机动。
description.en: Drawn from adult men of the nations when required, skilled in ambush, raiding, tracking, and woodland mobility.

description.zh: 围绕有威望的战首组成，负责远征、报复性袭击和高风险军事行动。
description.en: Formed around prestigious war leaders and used for expeditions, retaliatory raids, and high-risk operations.

commanderInChief.zh: 无单一总司令；由各族战首与议会共同决定
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; decisions were made by war leaders and councils of the nations

technology.zh: 军事技术以弓箭、木棍、石骨工具、木栅寨防御、独木舟机动和森林战术为主。1500年前后尚未形成普遍火器化，但在地形利用与小规模战法上极为有效。
technology.en: Military technology centered on bows, clubs, stone-and-bone tools, palisaded villages, canoe mobility, and woodland tactics. Around 1500 firearms were not yet widespread, but the confederacy was highly effective in terrain use and small-unit warfare.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自周边阿尔冈昆语族与圣劳伦斯地区敌对群体的冲突、复仇循环以及资源竞争。
threats.en: Major threats came from conflicts with neighboring Algonquian-speaking and St. Lawrence-area rivals, cycles of revenge, and competition over resources.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于今日纽约州中部和西部的防御性村落及长屋聚居点，以农业村落为主并伴随季节性狩猎流动。联盟人口规模在北美东北部属较强政治体之一。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed across defensive villages and longhouse settlements in what is now central and western New York, with agricultural villages supplemented by seasonal hunting movement. By northeastern North American standards, the confederacy was one of the stronger political populations.

socialClasses.zh: 氏族母亲、酋长与长老、战首、普通长屋家庭成员、收养者与俘虏。
socialClasses.en: clan mothers, sachems and elders, war leaders, ordinary longhouse household members, adoptees, and captives

allies.zh: 联盟内部五族互为盟友，并通过婚姻、收养和礼物网络维持周边部分友好关系。
allies.en: The Five Nations were allied internally and maintained some friendly surrounding ties through marriage, adoption, and gift networks.

enemies.zh: 与部分阿尔冈昆语族群体、休伦相关网络及周边竞争部族存在长期敌对或紧张关系。
enemies.en: They had enduring hostility or tension with certain Algonquian-speaking groups, Huron-linked networks, and neighboring rival peoples.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持联盟内部和平、对外平衡敌友、通过使节与仪式修复关系并在必要时发动报复战争为特点。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy sought to preserve internal peace, balance enemies and allies externally, repair relations through embassies and ritual, and conduct retaliatory war when necessary.

era.zh: 北美东北部晚期林地时代联盟社会
era.en: Late Woodland confederate society of northeastern North America

keyInnovations.zh: 长屋聚居、木栅寨防御、三姐妹农业、贝珠记忆系统与跨部族议事制度。
keyInnovations.en: longhouse settlement, palisaded defense, Three Sisters agriculture, wampum mnemonic systems, and intertribal council governance

strengths.zh: 联盟制度稳固、社会凝聚力强、村落粮食基础可靠，且在森林机动作战和政治整合方面表现突出。
strengths.en: The confederate system was durable, social cohesion was strong, the village food base was reliable, and the polity excelled in woodland warfare and political integration.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏集中财政与常备官僚机构，对疫病、长期人口损失及外来技术冲击较脆弱。
weaknesses.en: It lacked centralized finance and a standing bureaucracy, and was vulnerable to epidemics, sustained demographic losses, and external technological shocks.

outlook.zh: 在1500年前后联盟前景总体稳健，但16世纪后与欧洲接触将深刻改变其军事、贸易与人口格局。
outlook.en: Around 1500 the confederacy's outlook was broadly stable, but contact with Europeans in the sixteenth century would profoundly alter its military, commercial, and demographic landscape.

description.zh: 1500年前后的易洛魁联盟是一个以母系氏族、共识政治和农业村落为基础的强韧北美联盟政体。
description.en: Around 1500, the Iroquois Confederacy was a resilient North American union built on matrilineal clans, consensus politics, and agricultural villages.

governmentForm.zh: 克里米亚汗国是由格来家族统治的鞑靼伊斯兰汗国，兼具游牧贵族联盟与定居城市统治特征，并受奥斯曼宗主权强烈影响。
governmentForm.en: The Crimean Khanate was a Tatar Islamic khanate ruled by the Giray dynasty, combining a nomadic aristocratic confederation with control over settled towns and operating under strong Ottoman suzerainty.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由格来王族、贝伊贵族、穆尔扎武士、部族牧民、城镇商人、手工业者与农业人口构成。草原政治依赖氏族和贵族网络，而沿海与山地城镇则更具多族群、多宗教商业社会特征。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the Giray royal house, bey aristocrats, mirza warriors, tribal pastoralists, town merchants, artisans, and agricultural populations. Steppe politics relied on clan and noble networks, while the coastal and mountain towns displayed more multiethnic and multireligious commercial characteristics.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由格来王室及四大贝伊家族等鞑靼贵族组成，他们掌握军力、部众与地方影响。汗必须在王朝权威与贵族自治之间维持平衡。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was composed of the Giray royal house and major Tatar noble families, including the great beys, who controlled armed followers and local influence. The khan had to balance dynastic authority against aristocratic autonomy.

succession.zh: 汗位由格来家族成员中产生，通常遵循成年的王族资格与贵族、奥斯曼支持相结合的原则。继承常伴随内争、废立与外部干预。
succession.en: The khan was chosen from among the adult members of the Giray dynasty, usually through a combination of dynastic eligibility, noble backing, and Ottoman approval. Succession was often accompanied by rivalry, deposition, and outside intervention.

structure.zh: 汗国中央以汗廷、迪万议事会和王族属官构成，行政规模不及奥斯曼本土，但对军事动员和外交颇为有效。地方统治在草原地区依赖贝伊与部族首领，在城市则通过总督、法官与税吏运作。
structure.en: The khanate's center consisted of the khan's court, a divan council, and dynastic officers; while less extensive than the Ottoman core, it was effective in mobilization and diplomacy. Local rule relied on beys and tribal chiefs in the steppe, and on governors, judges, and tax officials in towns.

function.zh: 处理政务、贵族协商、外交事务与重大司法问题。
function.en: Handled government business, noble consultation, diplomacy, and major judicial matters.

function.zh: 征收关税、贡赋、城镇税收，并管理对奥斯曼及地方贵族的支付。
function.en: Collected customs, tribute, and urban taxes, and managed payments to the Ottomans and local nobles.

function.zh: 组织骑兵征召、边境防务、袭掠远征与使节护送。
function.en: Organized cavalry levies, border defense, raiding expeditions, and escort for envoys.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理呈双重结构：草原与部族地区由贝伊和穆尔扎主导，定居城市则设有总督、卡迪和税官。沿海重要港口与前热那亚殖民地的治理受奥斯曼影响尤深。
localAdmin.en: Local administration had a dual structure: steppe and tribal zones were led by beys and mirzas, while settled towns had governors, qadis, and tax officers. Key ports and former Genoese colonies were especially influenced by Ottoman administrative practices.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合伊斯兰教法、草原习惯法和汗的敕令。不同族群与城镇在实际司法中可保留部分各自传统。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Islamic sharia, steppe customary law, and the khan's decrees. Different communities and towns could retain elements of their own traditions in practice.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收来源包括关税、商税、农业税、属民贡赋和奴隶贸易收益。游牧贵族亦通过战利品分配和部众义务维持其政治经济地位。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation drew on customs duties, trade taxes, agrarian taxes, subject tribute, and slave-trade proceeds. Nomadic aristocrats also maintained their political economy through booty distribution and obligations from followers.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教
religion.en: Islam

culturalAchievements.zh: 汗国在黑海北岸形成了融合突厥、伊斯兰、草原与地中海影响的宫廷与城市文化。巴赫奇萨赖及周边地区逐渐发展出宫殿建筑、伊斯兰学术和多语商业网络。
culturalAchievements.en: The khanate developed a courtly and urban culture on the northern Black Sea that blended Turkic, Islamic, steppe, and Mediterranean influences. Bakhchysarai and nearby centers fostered palace architecture, Islamic learning, and multilingual commercial networks.

languageFamily.zh: 突厥语系
languageFamily.en: Turkic language family

philosophy.zh: 逊尼派伊斯兰、突厥—草原贵族伦理与黑海多族商业文化
philosophy.en: Sunni Islam, Turco-steppe aristocratic ethics, and the multiethnic commercial culture of the Black Sea

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯字母书写的克里米亚鞑靼文与奥斯曼土耳其文；宗教和法律文书亦广用阿拉伯文与波斯文传统
writingSystem.en: Crimean Tatar and Ottoman Turkish written in Arabic script; religious and legal texts also drew heavily on Arabic and Persian traditions

unit.en: silver akce equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 克里米亚汗国经济建立在牧业、农业、黑海贸易、过境税与奴隶贸易之上。其财富并不均匀，草原动员力与港口商业收入相结合，使汗国在东欧—黑海间具有显著经济活力。
gdpDescription.en: The Crimean Khanate's economy rested on pastoralism, agriculture, Black Sea trade, transit duties, and the slave trade. Wealth was unevenly distributed, but the combination of steppe mobilization and port commerce gave the khanate significant economic dynamism between eastern Europe and the Black Sea.

mainIndustries.zh: 牧马与畜牧、谷物农业、盐业、奴隶贸易、转口贸易、皮革、金属工艺与港口商业
mainIndustries.en: horse-breeding and pastoralism, grain agriculture, salt production, slave trade, entrepot trade, leatherworking, metal crafts, and port commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 奴隶、马匹、皮革、盐、谷物、蜂蜡、毛皮、布匹与东方奢侈品转运货物
tradeGoods.en: slaves, horses, leather, salt, grain, beeswax, furs, textiles, and re-exported eastern luxury goods

householdWealth.zh: 贵族家庭财富主要体现在马群、牲畜、依附人口、战利品和贸易收益，城镇商人则持有银币、库存与地产。普通牧民和农户财富较脆弱，易受战乱与气候影响。
householdWealth.en: Noble household wealth lay chiefly in horse herds, livestock, dependents, booty, and trade profits, while town merchants held silver coin, stocks, and property. Ordinary pastoralists and cultivators had more fragile wealth, vulnerable to war and climate.

unit.en: silver akce equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以草原部族资源、汗廷贡赋、黑海港口贸易和奥斯曼体系联动的混合型游牧—定居经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed nomadic-settled economy linking steppe tribal resources, khanal tribute, Black Sea port trade, and the Ottoman system.

source.zh: 奴隶贸易与战利品分成
source.en: slave trade and shares of booty

treasuryDescription.zh: 汗廷国库保持一定盈余，部分依靠贸易税与袭掠收益补充，但财政稳定性受战争季节、贵族忠诚与黑海贸易波动影响较大。
treasuryDescription.en: The khanal treasury maintained a modest surplus, replenished in part by trade taxes and raiding profits, but its stability was heavily affected by campaign seasons, noble loyalty, and fluctuations in Black Sea trade.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持贵族支持、保障骑兵动员、控制港口税源，并通过对外战争和贸易枢纽地位增加收入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to preserve aristocratic support, secure cavalry mobilization, control port revenues, and increase income through warfare and the khanate's role as a trade hub.

description.zh: 汗国军事核心，擅长远程骑射、快速突袭、侦察和大范围草原机动。
description.en: The core of the khanate's military, specializing in mounted archery, fast raids, reconnaissance, and wide-ranging steppe mobility.

description.zh: 由汗廷卫队和各贝伊直属武装组成，承担护卫、主力冲击和政治控制任务。
description.en: Composed of the khan's guard and the direct armed followers of the beys, used for protection, main-force strikes, and political control.

description.zh: 规模较小，多用于守城、港口防御和配合奥斯曼式火器作战。
description.en: Small in scale, used mainly for garrisoning towns, defending ports, and supporting Ottoman-style firearm warfare.

commanderInChief.zh: 明里·格来汗
commanderInChief.en: Khan Mengli Giray

technology.zh: 汗国在骑射、草原后勤和快速袭掠上极具优势，同时逐步吸收奥斯曼火器和攻城技术。其弱点在于重炮与持久围城能力有限，正规步兵较少。
technology.en: The khanate excelled in mounted archery, steppe logistics, and rapid raiding, while gradually adopting Ottoman firearms and siege methods. Its weaknesses lay in limited heavy artillery, reduced endurance in prolonged sieges, and a smaller regular infantry component.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自莫斯科大公国、立陶宛—波兰边境冲突、诺盖势力竞争以及黑海地区的战略变动。
threats.en: Major threats came from the Grand Duchy of Moscow, frontier conflict with Lithuania-Poland, rivalry with Nogai forces, and strategic shifts in the Black Sea region.

populationDescription.zh: 汗国人口由草原游牧群体、山地与河谷农业居民以及沿海多族商人社群组成，族群和宗教结构复杂。城市化程度中等，真正人口密集区主要集中于南岸、山麓和贸易节点。
populationDescription.en: The khanate's population included steppe nomads, mountain and valley agricultural communities, and multiethnic merchant groups along the coast, producing a complex ethnic and religious composition. Urbanization was moderate, with true concentrations of population mainly in the southern coast, foothills, and trade nodes.

socialClasses.zh: 格来王族、贝伊与穆尔扎贵族、乌里玛与法官、城镇商人和工匠、牧民、农民、奴隶与被掳人口。
socialClasses.en: Giray royalty, bey and mirza nobility, ulama and judges, town merchants and artisans, pastoralists, peasants, slaves, and captives

allies.zh: 主要依附并结盟于奥斯曼帝国，同时与部分黑海与草原势力保持务实合作关系。
allies.en: It was chiefly aligned with and subordinate to the Ottoman Empire, while maintaining pragmatic ties with certain Black Sea and steppe powers.

enemies.zh: 莫斯科大公国、部分立陶宛与鲁塞尼亚边境势力，以及不稳定的草原竞争者。
enemies.en: The Grand Duchy of Moscow, some Lithuanian and Ruthenian frontier powers, and unstable steppe rivals.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护黑海通道、配合奥斯曼战略、通过袭掠和威慑控制北方边境，并在东欧诸国间周旋为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving Black Sea routes, supporting Ottoman strategy, controlling the northern frontier through raiding and deterrence, and maneuvering among eastern European powers.

era.zh: 黑海草原晚中世纪—早期近代过渡时代
era.en: Late medieval to early modern transitional age of the Black Sea steppe

keyInnovations.zh: 高机动骑射体系、草原远程补给、奥斯曼式火器输入、港口贸易网络与多法域治理。
keyInnovations.en: high-mobility mounted archery, long-range steppe logistics, Ottoman-style firearm adoption, port trade networks, and multi-jurisdictional governance

strengths.zh: 骑兵机动性极强，贵族军事传统深厚，黑海贸易与奥斯曼支持提供了战略与财政支撑。
strengths.en: It possessed extremely mobile cavalry, a deep aristocratic military tradition, and strategic and fiscal backing from Black Sea trade and Ottoman support.

weaknesses.zh: 内部贵族离心力强，财政波动大，对奴隶贸易和袭掠收入依赖明显，持久攻城能力不足。
weaknesses.en: Internal aristocratic centrifugal forces were strong, finances were volatile, dependence on slave trade and raiding was marked, and capacity for prolonged siege warfare was limited.

outlook.zh: 1500年前后汗国处于强盛阶段，在奥斯曼体系内拥有重要地位；但长期看其结构性脆弱将随着北方国家强化与火器战争发展而加深。
outlook.en: Around 1500 the khanate was in a phase of strength and held an important place within the Ottoman system; in the long run, however, its structural vulnerabilities would deepen as northern states strengthened and firearm warfare advanced.

description.zh: 1500年前后的克里米亚汗国是黑海草原上一支强盛的鞑靼伊斯兰政权，以骑兵、贸易和奥斯曼联系见长。
description.en: Around 1500, the Crimean Khanate was a powerful Tatar Islamic state of the Black Sea steppe, distinguished by cavalry power, trade, and Ottoman connections.

governmentForm.zh: 这是一个以迈伊（君主）为核心、结合伊斯兰宫廷制度与萨赫勒地方贵族网络的君主帝国。
governmentForm.en: This was a monarchy centered on the mai (king), combining Islamic court institutions with a Sahelian network of provincial nobles.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、伊斯兰学者与廷臣、地方酋长、自由农牧民、工匠与商人构成。城市中穆斯林商人与书记阶层影响力较强，而乡村与草原地区仍保留较多传统氏族结构。奴隶也在农业、家政与军事附属体系中占有一定比重。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, Islamic scholars and courtiers, provincial chiefs, free farmers and pastoralists, artisans, and merchants. Muslim merchants and scribal elites were especially influential in towns, while rural and steppe zones retained stronger clan-based structures. Slaves also formed a significant part of agricultural, domestic, and military support systems.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层以赛义法瓦王室及其附属贵族为核心，并依赖伊斯兰法学者和宫廷书记维系政令与合法性。地方上则由效忠君主的总督、军政长官与传统酋长共同支配。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was centered on the Sayfawa royal house and its affiliated nobility, supported by Islamic jurists and court scribes who sustained administration and legitimacy. In the provinces, governors, military officials, and loyal traditional chiefs shared power.

succession.zh: 王位通常在赛义法瓦宗室内部继承，但实际继承常受宫廷派系、军功与地方支持左右。因而王位交替虽遵循王族血统原则，却并非总是平稳。
succession.en: The throne usually passed within the Sayfawa royal line, but actual succession was often shaped by court factions, military reputation, and regional backing. As a result, succession followed dynastic principles but was not always smooth.

structure.zh: 国家以恩加扎尔加穆宫廷为行政中枢，迈伊通过近臣、书记与地方总督统辖湖区与萨赫勒腹地。中央直接控制核心区域，对边缘地区则多采用朝贡、驻军与附庸酋长并行的方式。伊斯兰政治文化增强了文书、司法与财政管理的规范性。
structure.en: The state was centered on the court at Ngazargamu, where the mai ruled through close advisers, scribes, and provincial governors over the Lake Chad basin and Sahelian hinterland. The center directly controlled the core territories, while frontier zones were managed through a mix of tribute, garrisons, and vassal chiefs. Islamic political culture increased the regularity of record-keeping, justice, and revenue administration.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室财产、使节接待与内廷仪式。
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, diplomatic receptions, and palace ceremonial affairs.

function.zh: 征收贡赋、关税、市场税，并统计地方输纳。
function.en: Collected tribute, customs dues, market taxes, and recorded provincial remittances.

function.zh: 统筹骑兵、驻军、边防与战时动员。
function.en: Oversaw cavalry forces, garrisons, frontier defense, and wartime mobilization.

function.zh: 协调卡迪审判、宗教教育与清真寺相关事务。
function.en: Coordinated qadi courts, religious education, and mosque-related affairs.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由总督、军事长官与受册封的地方酋长共同组成。核心地区受中央监督较强，外围地区则更多依赖贡赋关系与定期巡视维持秩序。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out by governors, military commanders, and formally recognized local chiefs. The core regions were more closely supervised by the center, while outer zones relied more on tribute ties and periodic inspections.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系在城市与宫廷中明显受伊斯兰法影响，婚姻、商业与部分刑事案件可由卡迪裁决。乡村和非穆斯林社群仍保留习惯法，形成双重法律结构。
legalSystem.en: The legal system in towns and at court was strongly influenced by Islamic law, with qadis hearing marriage, commercial, and some criminal cases. Rural and non-Muslim communities still retained customary law, creating a dual legal order.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自朝贡、商路关税、牲畜与农产征收以及战俘与奴隶贸易收益。税收在湖区绿洲、集市与跨撒哈拉通道节点最为集中。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from tribute, caravan customs, levies on livestock and agricultural produce, and gains from captives and slave trade. Taxation was concentrated in the Lake Chad zone, oasis markets, and trans-Saharan route nodes.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教
religion.en: Islam

culturalAchievements.zh: 博尔努在1500年前后是中部萨赫勒重要的伊斯兰学习与贸易中心之一，宫廷与清真寺促进了阿拉伯文书与宗教教育传播。其文化成就更多体现在政治整合、商路维系与伊斯兰化进程，而非大型石造纪念建筑。
culturalAchievements.en: Around 1500, Bornu was one of the important Islamic learning and trade centers of the central Sahel, with the court and mosques fostering Arabic literacy and religious education. Its cultural achievements lay more in political integration, trade-route maintenance, and Islamization than in monumental stone architecture.

languageFamily.zh: 主要为尼罗-撒哈拉语系的卡努里语，并广泛使用阿拉伯语作为宗教与书写语言。
languageFamily.en: Primarily Kanuri of the Nilo-Saharan family, with Arabic widely used as a religious and written language.

philosophy.zh: 以逊尼派伊斯兰政治伦理为主，并与萨赫勒地区王权传统和地方习俗相结合。
philosophy.en: Predominantly shaped by Sunni Islamic political ethics blended with Sahelian kingship traditions and local custom.

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯字母书写阿拉伯语，宫廷与宗教场合可能辅以地方语言口传传统。
writingSystem.en: Arabic script for Arabic writing, alongside oral traditions in local languages used in courtly and popular contexts.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 博尔努—卡涅姆经济建立在湖区农业、畜牧业与跨撒哈拉贸易之上。其人均产出在萨赫勒强国中属中上水平，主要得益于商路控制与王权集中。
gdpDescription.en: The Bornu-Kanem economy rested on lake-basin agriculture, pastoralism, and trans-Saharan trade. Its per-capita output was relatively strong by Sahelian standards, mainly because of route control and centralized kingship.

mainIndustries.zh: 农耕、牧畜、跨撒哈拉贸易、皮革加工、织造、盐与奴隶贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, trans-Saharan commerce, leatherworking, weaving, and the trade in salt and slaves

tradeGoods.zh: 奴隶、马匹、盐、布匹、皮革、谷物、苏打、羽毛与进口铜器
tradeGoods.en: Slaves, horses, salt, cloth, leather, grain, natron, feathers, and imported copper goods

name.zh: 混合实物与金属折算体系

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为牲畜、谷仓储粮、纺织品与家族附庸。城市商人和宫廷官员通常拥有更高的金属货币与进口奢侈品储备。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly held in livestock, stored grain, textiles, and dependent labor. Urban merchants and court officials generally held larger reserves of bullion and imported luxuries.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per worker per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 这是一个由王权主导、市场交换活跃且与商队网络紧密相连的农牧复合经济。国家通过控制贸易节点、贡赋体系与军事保护来促进财富集中。
economicSystem.en: This was a royal-led mixed agro-pastoral economy with active markets closely tied to caravan networks. The state concentrated wealth through control of trade nodes, tribute systems, and military protection.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以实物、牲畜、金属与进口奢侈品混合储藏，王室可在战时迅速转为军费。边疆远征与商道失序会显著消耗储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was held in a mix of goods, livestock, bullion, and imported luxuries, allowing the crown to convert reserves quickly into military spending. Frontier expeditions and trade disruption could deplete these reserves significantly.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持商路安全、保障王室骑兵供养并通过赏赐稳固地方忠诚。整体上偏审慎，但在征服和边防时期支出会快速上升。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized caravan security, maintenance of royal cavalry, and patronage to secure local loyalty. It was broadly cautious, though spending rose rapidly during conquest or frontier campaigns.

description.zh: 由王室与贵族供养的精锐机动部队，是博尔努作战的核心力量。
description.en: An elite mobile force maintained by the crown and nobility, forming the core of Bornu warfare.

description.zh: 由矛兵、盾兵、弓箭手和随军辅助人员组成，负责围攻、守备与数量支撑。
description.en: Composed of spearmen, shield-bearers, archers, and support personnel, used for siege work, garrison duty, and numerical strength.

description.zh: 驻守交通节点和附庸地区，负责警戒、征税与快速反应。
description.en: Stationed at route nodes and dependent territories, responsible for surveillance, taxation, and rapid response.

commanderInChief.zh: 迈伊阿里·加吉
commanderInChief.en: Mai Ali Gaji

technology.zh: 军队以骑兵机动、皮甲与锁甲、长矛、弓箭和少量进口马具装备为主，火器尚未形成决定性作用。相较周边小邦，其后勤与指挥组织更成熟。
technology.en: The army relied mainly on cavalry mobility, leather and mail armor, spears, bows, and some imported horse equipment; firearms had not yet become decisive. Compared with neighboring polities, its logistics and command organization were more developed.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自湖区周边反叛附庸、撒哈拉商路竞争者以及豪萨地带和草原游动势力的冲突。若王位继承不稳，地方分裂风险会迅速上升。
threats.en: Main threats came from rebellious dependents around Lake Chad, rivals on Saharan trade routes, and conflicts involving Hausa zones and mobile steppe forces. If succession became unstable, the risk of regional fragmentation increased quickly.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于乍得湖周边可耕地、绿洲与商路节点，农村与半游牧人口占多数。城市人口比例不高，但首都和贸易聚落具有明显的政治与宗教吸引力。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in cultivable lands around Lake Chad, oases, and trade nodes, with rural and semi-pastoral communities forming the majority. Urbanization was limited, but the capital and commercial settlements had notable political and religious pull.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、乌理玛与书记、商人、工匠、自由农牧民、附庸与奴隶
socialClasses.en: Royalty and nobility, ulama and scribes, merchants, artisans, free farmers and pastoralists, dependents, and slaves

allies.zh: 与部分跨撒哈拉商人集团、忠诚附庸酋邦及伊斯兰学者网络保持合作关系。
allies.en: Maintained cooperative ties with certain trans-Saharan merchant groups, loyal vassal chiefdoms, and Islamic scholarly networks.

enemies.zh: 敌对者包括反叛边地势力、商路竞争政权及不稳定的草原部族联盟。
enemies.en: Enemies included rebellious frontier powers, rival trade polities, and unstable steppe tribal coalitions.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制商道、维持湖区霸权和借伊斯兰网络提升合法性为核心。其外交往往与军事威慑、朝贡关系和商队保护相互交织。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling trade routes, preserving hegemony in the Lake Chad basin, and using Islamic networks to enhance legitimacy. Diplomacy was closely intertwined with military deterrence, tributary relations, and caravan protection.

era.zh: 萨赫勒伊斯兰王朝时代

keyInnovations.zh: 改良骑兵运用、伊斯兰文书行政、长距离商队组织、湖区农牧资源整合
keyInnovations.en: Refined cavalry warfare, Islamic scribal administration, long-distance caravan organization, and integrated exploitation of lake-basin agro-pastoral resources

strengths.zh: 拥有较强的骑兵、稳定的王朝传统、重要的商路位置与伊斯兰政治文化支撑。中央对核心地区的控制力在中部萨赫勒较为突出。
strengths.en: It possessed strong cavalry, stable dynastic traditions, an important trade position, and support from Islamic political culture. Central control over the core territories was comparatively strong for the central Sahel.

weaknesses.zh: 对商路收益和附庸忠诚依赖较大，边疆整合成本高。继承纠纷、气候波动和贸易路线转移都可能削弱国力。
weaknesses.en: It depended heavily on trade income and vassal loyalty, and frontier integration was costly. Succession disputes, climatic fluctuations, and trade-route shifts could all weaken the state.

outlook.zh: 在1500年前后前景总体良好，只要维持商道和宫廷稳定，仍可继续作为中部萨赫勒强权存在。若边疆压力与宫廷斗争并发，扩张将转为防御。
outlook.en: Its outlook around 1500 was generally favorable; if caravan routes and court stability were maintained, it could continue as a major central Sahelian power. If frontier pressure and court conflict coincided, expansion would give way to defense.

description.zh: 博尔努—卡涅姆是1500年前后依托乍得湖商路、伊斯兰宫廷与强骑兵而兴盛的萨赫勒帝国。
description.en: Bornu-Kanem around 1500 was a thriving Sahelian empire built on Lake Chad trade routes, an Islamic court, and strong cavalry.

governmentForm.zh: 这是一个由奥巴统治、以宫廷等级制度和城邦—乡村附属网络为基础的集权王国。
governmentForm.en: This was a centralized kingdom ruled by the oba, founded on a hierarchical court system and a network linking the capital to subordinate towns and villages.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由奥巴及王族、世袭酋长、宫廷官员、行会工匠、农民与奴隶构成。贝宁城的宫廷与行会制度高度发达，使城市精英在政治与文化上占据突出地位。乡村地区则通过村社、祭祀传统与地方首领维持秩序。
socialStructure.en: Society was made up of the oba and royal kin, hereditary chiefs, palace officials, guild artisans, farmers, and slaves. The palace and guild system of Benin City were highly developed, giving urban elites a prominent political and cultural role. Rural areas were organized through village communities, ritual traditions, and local headmen.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层核心是奥巴、王室亲族及宫廷中的高级酋长，尤其是与王宫紧密相连的首席贵族和官员。青铜匠、象牙工匠等特许行会虽非贵族，但因服务宫廷而享有特殊地位。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class centered on the oba, royal kin, and the senior chiefs of the palace, especially leading nobles and officials closely tied to the court. Privileged guilds such as bronze casters and ivory workers were not nobles, but enjoyed special status through service to the palace.

succession.zh: 王位通常由王室血统中的合法继承人承袭，但即位过程往往需要宫廷酋长的支持与认可。王位斗争虽然存在，却受到王权仪式和宫廷制度的约束。
succession.en: The throne generally passed to a legitimate heir within the royal line, though accession often required the backing and recognition of palace chiefs. Succession struggles existed, but were constrained by royal ritual and court institutions.

structure.zh: 贝宁王国以贝宁城王宫为行政中心，奥巴通过宫廷官员、世袭酋长和附属地区长官实施统治。中央对首都及其周边控制严密，对远方地区则通过贡赋、仪式臣服和军事威慑维系。王权与宗教祭祀深度交织，使统治兼具政治与神圣性质。
structure.en: The Kingdom of Benin was governed from the palace in Benin City, where the oba ruled through court officials, hereditary chiefs, and administrators of subordinate districts. The center exercised tight control over the capital and its surrounding zone, while more distant areas were managed through tribute, ritual submission, and military intimidation. Kingship was deeply intertwined with ritual authority, making rule both political and sacred.

function.zh: 管理宫廷礼仪、王室仓储、后宫与来使接待。
function.en: Managed palace ritual, royal storage, the inner court, and reception of envoys.

function.zh: 征收贡物、保管粮仓、记录手工业与外来贡纳。
function.en: Collected tribute, supervised granaries, and recorded artisanal and external deliveries.

function.zh: 负责征召军队、修整道路与维持首都周边防御。
function.en: Handled troop mobilization, road maintenance, and defense of the capital region.

function.zh: 协调王室特许工匠行会，特别是铜器、象牙与木雕生产。
function.en: Coordinated royal guilds, especially in bronze, ivory, and wood carving production.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依靠酋长、宫廷派驻官员及朝贡附属社区共同运作。首都附近管理较为直接，而外围地区更依赖象征臣服和定期贡纳。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through chiefs, palace-appointed officials, and tributary communities. Rule was more direct near the capital, while outer regions depended more on symbolic submission and periodic tribute.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、宫廷判决与习惯法为基础，案件常由酋长与宫廷共同审理。宗教禁忌、祖先祭祀与政治忠诚在司法中具有重要地位。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on royal command, palace judgment, and customary norms, with cases often heard jointly by chiefs and the court. Religious taboos, ancestor veneration, and political loyalty played an important role in justice.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖贡赋、战利品、市场征收和对手工业生产的宫廷控制。劳役、物资输送和特定工艺品上缴比统一货币税更常见。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue depended mainly on tribute, war booty, market dues, and palace control over artisanal production. Labor obligations, deliveries in kind, and special craft contributions were more common than standardized money taxes.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 贝宁在宫廷艺术、青铜铸造、象牙雕刻与城市规划方面成就卓著。其王宫建筑、仪式道路与精致的宫廷工艺品在西非同时代国家中极具代表性。
culturalAchievements.en: Benin excelled in court art, bronze casting, ivory carving, and urban planning. Its palace architecture, ceremonial roads, and sophisticated courtly artworks were among the most distinctive achievements of contemporary West Africa.

languageFamily.zh: 埃多语，属尼日尔-刚果语系。
languageFamily.en: Edo language of the Niger-Congo family.

philosophy.zh: 以祖先崇拜、王权神圣性与埃多宗教宇宙观为核心，并强调礼仪秩序与忠诚。
philosophy.en: Centered on ancestor veneration, the sacred nature of kingship, and Edo religious cosmology, with strong emphasis on ritual order and loyalty.

writingSystem.zh: 无广泛使用的本土成文文字体系，政治与历史主要通过口述传统、宫廷记忆与图像艺术传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no widely used indigenous written script; politics and history were preserved mainly through oral tradition, court memory, and visual art.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 贝宁经济以湿润森林地带农业、棕榈产品、手工业和区域贸易为基础。首都的宫廷需求和工匠行会促进了较高水平的财富集中与再分配。
gdpDescription.en: Benin's economy rested on agriculture in the humid forest zone, palm products, artisanal production, and regional trade. Palace demand and urban guilds in the capital supported a relatively high concentration and redistribution of wealth.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、棕榈产品、青铜铸造、象牙雕刻、木雕、织造、区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, palm products, bronze casting, ivory carving, wood carving, weaving, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 胡椒、象牙、布匹、棕榈油与棕榈仁、铜料、珊瑚珠、奴隶与手工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Pepper, ivory, cloth, palm oil and kernels, copper materials, coral beads, slaves, and crafted goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富多表现为耕地、棕榈树、储粮、房舍与家族劳动力。城市精英则更可能拥有铜料、珊瑚、进口珠饰和附属劳役者。
householdWealth.en: For ordinary households, wealth was held in farmland, palm trees, stored food, housing, and family labor. Urban elites were more likely to possess copper, coral, imported beads, and dependent laborers.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per worker per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 这是一个以农业剩余和宫廷再分配为基础、并由特许行会和区域贸易支持的王权经济。国家对高价值工艺品和对外交换保持较强影响力。
economicSystem.en: This was a royal economy based on agricultural surplus and palace redistribution, supported by privileged guilds and regional trade. The state exerted strong influence over high-value craft goods and external exchange.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库除金属与珠饰外，还包括象牙、工艺品、粮食与贡物储备。其财富不仅用于战争，也用于大型祭祀、王宫建设与赏赐体系。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury included not only metal and beads, but also ivory, crafted goods, grain, and tribute stores. Its wealth served not just war, but also major rituals, palace construction, and systems of royal patronage.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调将地方贡赋和工艺产出集中于王宫，再通过仪式赏赐与军事动员重新分配。王国更重视控制财富流向而非标准化货币征税。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized concentrating provincial tribute and artisanal output in the palace, then redistributing them through ritual gift-giving and military mobilization. The kingdom cared more about controlling wealth flows than about standardized monetary taxation.

description.zh: 负责首都、王宫和奥巴人身安全，是最忠于中央的部队。
description.en: Guarded the capital, palace, and person of the oba, and was the force most loyal to the center.

description.zh: 由矛、刀、盾和弓箭武装的步兵构成，适合森林地形作战和围攻。
description.en: Comprised infantry armed with spears, blades, shields, and bows, well suited to forest warfare and siege operations.

description.zh: 由地方酋长提供，战时增援主力并协助镇压边区。
description.en: Provided by local chiefs, these forces reinforced the main army in wartime and helped pacify outlying areas.

commanderInChief.zh: 奥巴奥佐鲁阿
commanderInChief.en: Oba Ozolua

technology.zh: 贝宁军队以训练有素的步兵、道路网络与攻守兼备的森林战术见长。1500年前后可能开始接触葡萄牙贸易带来的少量火器与金属器物，但仍未成为主战装备。
technology.en: Benin's military was distinguished by disciplined infantry, a usable road network, and forest warfare tactics suited to both offense and defense. Around 1500, it may have begun to encounter small numbers of firearms and metal goods through Portuguese trade, but these had not yet become standard battlefield equipment.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自尼日尔三角洲和内陆边境的反抗社区、邻近森林国家竞争以及附庸地区离心。若对外贸易改变区域力量平衡，首都对外围的控制可能受挑战。
threats.en: Main threats came from resistant communities in the Niger Delta and inland frontiers, rivalry with neighboring forest states, and centrifugal tendencies in tributary regions. If external trade altered the regional balance of power, the capital's hold over the periphery could be challenged.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在森林—农耕村落网络中，首都为最重要的城市与仪式中心。贝宁城的人口规模和工匠密度在西非森林地带尤为突出。
populationDescription.en: The population was mainly spread across a forest-agricultural village network, with the capital serving as the main urban and ritual center. Benin City's size and concentration of artisans were especially notable within the West African forest zone.

socialClasses.zh: 奥巴与王族、宫廷酋长、世袭贵族、行会工匠、商人、农民、附庸与奴隶
socialClasses.en: Oba and royal kin, palace chiefs, hereditary nobles, guild artisans, merchants, farmers, dependents, and slaves

allies.zh: 与忠诚附庸地区、部分尼日尔河下游贸易伙伴以及早期葡萄牙商人保持有限合作。
allies.en: Maintained limited cooperation with loyal tributary regions, some lower Niger trade partners, and early Portuguese merchants.

enemies.zh: 敌对者包括边境反叛社区、竞争性森林政体和不愿纳贡的邻近群体。
enemies.en: Enemies included rebellious frontier communities, competing forest polities, and neighboring groups resistant to tribute.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以扩大王权威望、控制区域贸易通道并吸纳外来奢侈品和金属资源为主。其外交常通过贡赋关系、军事远征和礼物交换共同展开。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at expanding royal prestige, controlling regional trade corridors, and acquiring imported luxury goods and metals. Diplomacy proceeded through a mix of tributary relations, military expeditions, and gift exchange.

era.zh: 西非森林王国宫廷工艺时代
era.en: Courtly craft age of the West African forest kingdoms

keyInnovations.zh: 失蜡法青铜铸造、象牙雕刻、城市道路与壕沟体系、王室行会组织
keyInnovations.en: Lost-wax bronze casting, ivory carving, urban roads and earthwork systems, and royal guild organization

strengths.zh: 王权象征力强、首都组织高度发达、工艺技术卓越且区域军事实力可观。其宫廷文化与财富集中能力在周边政体中极具优势。
strengths.en: It had powerful royal symbolism, a highly organized capital, exceptional craft skill, and considerable regional military strength. Its court culture and ability to concentrate wealth gave it major advantages over neighboring polities.

weaknesses.zh: 财政较依赖贡赋与宫廷控制，外围地区整合有限。缺乏广泛书写行政和骑兵优势，也限制了远距离扩张能力。
weaknesses.en: Its finances relied heavily on tribute and palace control, while integration of outer regions remained limited. The lack of a broad literate administration and of cavalry superiority also constrained long-distance expansion.

outlook.zh: 1500年前后贝宁处于上升与稳固阶段，若能继续控制贸易并维持宫廷秩序，仍将保持区域强国地位。与海上贸易接触的加深亦可能增强其财富和军备。
outlook.en: Around 1500, Benin was in a phase of consolidation and strength; if it continued to control trade and preserve court order, it would remain a major regional power. Deeper contact with maritime trade could further increase its wealth and armament.

description.zh: 1500年前后的贝宁王国是以神圣王权、精致宫廷艺术和强大步兵体系著称的西非森林王国。
description.en: Around 1500, the Kingdom of Benin was a West African forest kingdom renowned for sacred kingship, refined court art, and strong infantry forces.

governmentForm.zh: 这是一个以高地王族和亲族联盟为核心、尚处于整合阶段的地方性王国。
governmentForm.en: This was a localized kingdom centered on a highland royal lineage and kin-based alliances, still in an early phase of political consolidation.

socialStructure.zh: 社会主要由王族及地方显贵、自由农民、依附者与奴隶组成。高地村落共同体在经济与防御上十分重要，家族和祖先崇拜是社会组织的核心纽带。与后世统一的梅里纳王国相比，此时的等级结构仍较松散。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted mainly of royal kin and local notables, free cultivators, dependents, and slaves. Highland village communities were crucial for both economy and defense, while kinship and ancestor worship formed the core social bonds. Compared with the later unified Merina state, social hierarchy was still relatively loose.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由高地王族、近支亲属和控制山寨、梯田与劳役的地方头人构成。其权力更多依赖血缘、婚姻网络与仪式威望，而非成熟官僚制度。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was composed of highland royalty, close kin, and local headmen who controlled hill forts, terraces, and labor. Their power relied more on kinship, marriage networks, and ritual prestige than on a mature bureaucracy.

succession.zh: 继承通常在王族内部进行，但常受长幼次序、婚姻联盟和地方头人支持影响。由于国家整合有限，继承争议容易造成分裂或地方独立倾向。
succession.en: Succession normally took place within the royal lineage, but was influenced by seniority, marriage alliances, and the support of local headmen. Because state integration was limited, succession disputes could easily produce fragmentation or local separatism.

structure.zh: 政权以高地聚落和王族居地为中心，王权通过亲族首领、村社头人和祭仪网络进行统治。中央机构规模很小，更多依靠面对面的命令、劳役征集与婚姻纽带维持。其政治形态更接近逐步形成中的高地王国，而非成熟帝国官僚体制。
structure.en: The polity was centered on highland settlements and royal residences, with rule exercised through kin leaders, village headmen, and ritual networks. Central institutions were very small and depended on face-to-face command, labor mobilization, and marriage ties. Its political form was closer to an emergent highland kingdom than to a mature imperial bureaucracy.

function.zh: 管理王族居所、储粮、祭祀供奉与近臣事务。
function.en: Managed royal residences, grain stores, ritual offerings, and close attendants.

function.zh: 组织梯田修筑、聚落防御、集体劳役与战时征召。
function.en: Organized terrace building, settlement defense, communal labor, and wartime levies.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要由村社长老、亲族首领和受王族影响的头人承担。王权对近郊高地控制较强，对更远聚落则依赖联盟和仪式认可。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled mainly by village elders, kin leaders, and headmen influenced by the royal lineage. Royal authority was stronger in nearby highlands, while farther settlements depended on alliances and ritual recognition.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以祖先传统、族群习惯和王族裁断为基础。土地、婚姻和血亲责任等事务多在村社层面解决，重大纠纷才上达王族仲裁。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on ancestral custom, communal tradition, and royal judgment. Matters involving land, marriage, and kin obligations were usually settled at village level, with major disputes referred to royal arbitration.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以劳役、贡物、稻米、牲畜和手工品上缴为主，货币税几乎不存在。王权通过掌握储粮和组织集体工程来体现财政能力。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue took the form of labor service, tribute goods, rice, livestock, and craft items, with almost no monetary taxation. Royal fiscal capacity was expressed mainly through control of stored grain and direction of communal works.

religion.zh: 祖先崇拜
religion.en: Ancestor worship

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就主要体现在高地梯田农业、聚落防御工事、祖先陵墓传统与口头谱系记忆。虽然尚无后世那样的大规模国家建筑，但已形成鲜明的高地社会文化特色。
culturalAchievements.en: Its cultural achievements lay chiefly in highland terraced agriculture, fortified settlements, ancestral tomb traditions, and oral genealogical memory. Although it lacked the large-scale state architecture of later centuries, it had already developed a distinct highland social culture.

languageFamily.zh: 马达加斯加语，属南岛语系。
languageFamily.en: Malagasy, belonging to the Austronesian language family.

philosophy.zh: 以祖先崇拜、神圣地景观念、家族延续与高地共同体伦理为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on ancestor worship, sacred landscape concepts, family continuity, and the ethics of the highland community.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土成文文字体系，历史、谱系与仪式知识主要依靠口述传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no indigenous written script; history, genealogy, and ritual knowledge were transmitted primarily through oral tradition.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 梅里纳高地经济以稻作、块根作物、畜养和村社手工业为主，整体产出低于大型贸易型国家。其优势在于高地环境较利于防御与粮食积累，但市场化程度有限。
gdpDescription.en: The highland Merina economy was based on rice cultivation, root crops, livestock keeping, and village handicrafts, with total output below that of larger trade-oriented states. Its strengths lay in defensive highland ecology and food accumulation, but market development remained limited.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、畜养、木工、陶器、织造、村社手工业
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, livestock keeping, woodworking, pottery, weaving, and village handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、牛只、蜂蜜、陶器、织物、木器与少量区域交换品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, cattle, honey, pottery, textiles, wooden goods, and small quantities of regional exchange goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为稻田、牛群、房屋、祖坟与亲族劳动力。财富差异存在，但总体不像大型王国那样高度集中于城市商人与宫廷。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly held in rice fields, cattle, houses, ancestral tombs, and kin labor. Inequality existed, but wealth was not concentrated as strongly in urban merchants and courts as in larger kingdoms.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per worker per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 这是一个以村社自给、王族贡赋和季节性劳役为特征的高地农业经济。对外贸易和货币化水平较低，但粮食与牲畜储备对政治权威极为重要。
economicSystem.en: This was a highland agrarian economy characterized by village self-sufficiency, royal tribute, and seasonal labor service. External trade and monetization were limited, but grain and cattle reserves were crucial to political authority.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备主要以粮食、牛只、器物和祭祀珍藏形式存在，而非大量金银。国库规模有限，但足以在局部冲突和歉收年份发挥缓冲作用。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves were held mainly in grain, cattle, tools, and ritual valuables rather than in large stores of precious metal. The treasury was modest, but sufficient to buffer local conflict and poor harvest years.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策侧重储粮、维持王族威望和组织公共劳役。其核心不在货币征收，而在确保高地社区持续向中心输送实物与服务。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on grain storage, preservation of royal prestige, and organization of communal labor. Its core lay not in monetary taxation, but in ensuring that highland communities continued to provide goods and service to the center.

description.zh: 由王族亲兵和忠诚家臣组成，负责护卫居地和执行核心命令。
description.en: Comprised of royal retainers and loyal household followers, responsible for guarding royal residences and carrying out core commands.

description.zh: 由各高地聚落在战时集结，使用长矛、木盾、刀和投射武器。
description.en: Raised from highland settlements in wartime, armed with spears, wooden shields, blades, and missile weapons.

description.zh: 驻守防御聚落和险要地形，擅长利用高地地势阻滞进攻。
description.en: Stationed in fortified settlements and rough terrain, skilled at using the highlands to slow attackers.

commanderInChief.zh: 梅里纳国王
commanderInChief.en: King of Merina

technology.zh: 军事技术以高地防御工事、长矛、木盾、刀具和群体征召为主，没有成熟火器体系。其优势在地形适应与社区动员，而非装备先进性。
technology.en: Military technology centered on highland fortifications, spears, wooden shields, blades, and communal mobilization, without a developed firearm system. Its strength lay in terrain adaptation and local mobilization rather than advanced equipment.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近高地与低地社群冲突、内部亲族竞争及粮食歉收后的社会动荡。由于政治整合尚弱，内部裂解比外来征服更现实。
threats.en: Main threats came from conflict with neighboring highland and lowland communities, internal kin rivalry, and social unrest following poor harvests. Because political integration was still weak, internal fragmentation was a more immediate danger than foreign conquest.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在中部高地村落与梯田周边，聚落规模较小但相对密集。真正意义上的大城市尚未形成，政治中心更接近王族居地与防御性聚落的组合。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in central highland villages and around terraced fields, with settlements relatively small but fairly dense. True large cities had not yet formed, and the political center resembled a combination of royal residence and fortified settlement.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与地方显贵、自由农民、依附者、工匠、奴隶
socialClasses.en: Royal kin and local notables, free cultivators, dependents, artisans, and slaves

allies.zh: 主要盟友是通过婚姻与祖先仪式联系起来的邻近高地聚落与亲族集团。
allies.en: Its main allies were neighboring highland settlements and kin groups linked through marriage and ancestral ritual.

enemies.zh: 敌对者多为争夺土地、牛群与劳力资源的邻近社群，而非远方国家。
enemies.en: Its enemies were usually neighboring communities competing over land, cattle, and labor rather than distant states.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以稳固高地联盟、控制耕地和吸纳周边聚落臣服为主。其外交更多表现为婚姻、馈赠与仪式承认，而非正式常设使节制度。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on securing highland alliances, controlling farmland, and drawing surrounding settlements into submission. Diplomacy was expressed more through marriage, gift exchange, and ritual recognition than through a formal permanent embassy system.

era.zh: 马达加斯加中部高地早期王国时期
era.en: Early kingdom period of Madagascar's central highlands

keyInnovations.zh: 高地梯田稻作、山寨防御聚落、祖先陵墓传统、村社劳役组织
keyInnovations.en: Highland terraced rice cultivation, fortified hill settlements, ancestral tomb traditions, and organized communal labor

strengths.zh: 地形易守难攻，社会凝聚力建立在亲族与祖先传统之上，粮食和牛只储备具一定韧性。作为高地政治核心的雏形，其内部组织有继续成长空间。
strengths.en: Its terrain was defensible, social cohesion rested on kinship and ancestor traditions, and grain and cattle reserves provided a measure of resilience. As an embryonic highland political center, it had room to grow institutionally.

weaknesses.zh: 中央机构薄弱、城市化低、对外贸易有限，军事动员主要依赖临时征召。继承与亲族竞争也容易阻碍统一。
weaknesses.en: Central institutions were weak, urbanization was low, external trade was limited, and military mobilization relied mainly on temporary levies. Succession and kin competition also hindered unification.

outlook.zh: 1500年前后其前景偏稳健而非扩张，若能逐步整合高地聚落并加强粮食和劳役控制，未来有形成更强王国的可能。短期内仍将是区域性高地政权。
outlook.en: Around 1500 its outlook was stable rather than expansionist; if it gradually integrated highland settlements and strengthened control over grain and labor, it could eventually become a stronger kingdom. In the short term, it would remain a regional highland polity.

description.zh: 1500年前后的梅里纳王国是马达加斯加中部高地一个依托稻作、亲族联盟和山地防御的早期王国。
description.en: Around 1500, the Kingdom of Merina was an early highland polity in central Madagascar built on rice cultivation, kin alliances, and defensive terrain.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，部族酋邦体系
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty, tribal chiefdom system

governmentForm.zh: 1500年前后的毛利社会由多个独立的部族与亚部族组成，以酋长、长老和宗族网络共同治理。
governmentForm.en: Around 1500, Māori society consisted of many independent tribes and sub-tribes governed through chiefs, elders, and kinship networks.

socialStructure.zh: 毛利社会以亲族和谱系为核心，基本单位包括部族（iwi）、亚部族（hapū）和大家族（whānau）。社会地位受血统、战功、宗教威望与土地控制影响，奴仆和战俘处于较低地位。
socialStructure.en: Māori society was organized around kinship and genealogy, with iwi, hapū, and whānau as core units. Social standing depended on ancestry, military achievement, sacred prestige, and control of land, while dependents and captives occupied lower positions.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是各部族与亚部族的酋长家系，以及拥有神圣权威的祭司和长老。其权力建立在血统、个人威望、馈赠能力和作战领导上。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted mainly of chiefly lineages of tribes and sub-tribes, together with priests and elders holding sacred authority. Their power rested on ancestry, personal prestige, gift-giving capacity, and leadership in war.

succession.zh: 酋长职位通常在显赫家系内部继承，但并非绝对长子继承。继承人需获得亲族支持，并以能力、勇武和威望巩固地位。
succession.en: Chiefly office usually passed within prominent lineages, but not by strict primogeniture. A successor needed kin support and had to secure authority through ability, valor, and prestige.

structure.zh: 毛利人没有统一国家机构，各部族分别管理土地、战争、祭祀与资源分配。重大事务通常由酋长、长老和祭司协商决定。
structure.en: The Māori had no unified state apparatus; each tribe managed land, warfare, ritual, and resource distribution separately. Major affairs were usually decided through consultation among chiefs, elders, and priests.

function.zh: 处理部族联盟、土地分配、纠纷调解与战争决策。
function.en: Handled tribal alliances, land allocation, dispute mediation, and decisions on war.

function.zh: 负责祭祀禁忌、历法、口传知识以及储粮和资源监督。
function.en: Oversaw ritual taboos, calendrical knowledge, oral tradition, and supervision of food stores and resources.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由各hapū自行执行，围绕村落、要塞村与耕地展开。酋长依靠亲族义务和威望而非成文官僚体系施政。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out by individual hapū around villages, fortified pā, and cultivated lands. Chiefs ruled through kin obligations and prestige rather than a written bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、禁忌（tapu）和报复平衡原则为基础。纠纷多通过赔偿、婚姻安排、盟约或武力解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, tapu, and principles of reciprocal balance. Disputes were often settled through compensation, marriage arrangements, alliances, or force.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在国家税收，资源主要通过亲族义务、酋长征集和集体劳动集中。战时可临时征收粮食、武器和人力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no state taxation; resources were mobilized through kin obligations, chiefly requisition, and communal labor. In wartime, food, weapons, and manpower could be levied temporarily.

religion.zh: 波利尼西亚多神教
religion.en: Polynesian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 毛利人擅长远洋航海传统的继承，并在新西兰发展出适应温带环境的农业、渔猎和要塞村建造技术。木雕、仪式歌舞与谱系记忆体系是其文化核心成就。
culturalAchievements.en: The Māori preserved the heritage of long-distance Polynesian navigation and developed agriculture, fishing-hunting practices, and fortified pā construction adapted to New Zealand's temperate environment. Wood carving, ceremonial performance, and genealogical memory were among their central cultural achievements.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系波利尼西亚语族
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Polynesian branch

philosophy.zh: 以祖灵崇拜、神圣秩序、血统记忆和人与自然关系为核心的波利尼西亚宇宙观
philosophy.en: A Polynesian worldview centered on ancestors, sacred order, genealogical memory, and the relationship between people and nature

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，以口传传统、谱系背诵、歌谣和雕刻符号记忆历史
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; history was preserved through oral tradition, genealogical recitation, chants, and carved symbolic forms

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 毛利经济以自给性生产为主，货币化程度极低，财富主要体现为土地、储粮、独木舟、武器和人际义务网络。总体产出有限，但在适宜地区能维持较稳定的人口供养。
gdpDescription.en: The Māori economy was predominantly subsistence-based with very low monetization, and wealth was expressed mainly in land, stored food, canoes, weapons, and networks of obligation. Overall output was limited but could sustain a fairly stable population in suitable regions.

mainIndustries.zh: 园圃农业、渔业、鸟兽捕猎、木器与石器制造、独木舟建造
mainIndustries.en: Horticulture, fishing, bird and game hunting, wood and stone craft production, canoe building

tradeGoods.zh: 绿玉、黑曜石、羽毛饰物、干鱼、编织品、木器、石制工具
tradeGoods.en: Greenstone, obsidian, feather ornaments, dried fish, woven goods, wooden items, stone tools

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在储粮坑、耕地使用权、渔猎工具与亲族支持。首领家庭则拥有更多独木舟、饰物、武器和追随者。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in food storage pits, land-use rights, fishing and hunting tools, and kin support. Chiefly households possessed more canoes, ornaments, weapons, and followers.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以部族共有资源、亲族分配、互惠交换和有限的区域性贸易为特征的部族经济
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy characterized by shared resources, kin-based distribution, reciprocal exchange, and limited regional trade

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Chiefly requisition of food and labor

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在集中式国库，储备主要以粮食、珍贵石材、武器和礼物网络形式分散掌握在酋长手中。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves were held in decentralized form by chiefs as food stocks, valuable stone items, weapons, and gift networks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政调配重在维持储粮安全、酋长威望和战时动员，而非持续性税制。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on preserving food security, chiefly prestige, and wartime mobilization rather than on a permanent tax system.

description.zh: 以近战武器为主，由各hapū按需召集，依托要塞村和伏击作战。
description.en: Primarily close-combat fighters raised by hapū as needed, relying on fortified pā and ambush tactics.

description.zh: 用于沿海机动、袭击和部族间运输，规模较小但灵活。
description.en: Used for coastal mobility, raiding, and inter-tribal transport; small in scale but flexible.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一统帅，由各部族酋长分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander; each tribe was led by its own chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术以木、石、骨制武器和要塞村防御体系为核心，不具备金属武器和火器。
technology.en: Military technology centered on wood, stone, and bone weapons and fortified pā defenses, without metal arms or firearms.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部族间争地复仇、资源竞争及局部迁徙冲突。
threats.en: The main threats came from inter-tribal land feuds, revenge cycles, competition over resources, and localized migration conflicts.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于新西兰北岛和南岛沿海及河谷地区，以北岛较为密集。聚落规模普遍较小，但部分防御性聚落在战争时期可集中较多人口。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed across coastal and riverine areas of the North and South Islands, with greater density in the North Island. Settlements were generally small, though some defensive communities could concentrate larger populations in wartime.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家族、祭司与长老、自由部族成员、依附者与战俘
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, priests and elders, free tribal members, dependents, and captives

allies.zh: 以邻近部族间的婚盟和临时军事同盟为主，无跨全岛持久联盟。
allies.en: Alliances were mainly local marriage ties and temporary military coalitions, with no durable all-island alliance.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象多为邻近竞争部族，而非外来国家。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually neighboring rival tribes rather than foreign states.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系重在维护土地、声望和互惠网络，以局部联盟和报复平衡为主。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on defending land, prestige, and reciprocal networks, relying on local alliances and retaliatory balance.

era.zh: 晚期石器时代的波利尼西亚定居社会
era.en: A late Stone Age Polynesian settler society

keyInnovations.zh: 要塞村营建、远洋独木舟传统的延续、适应温带环境的园圃农业、精细木雕与纤维编织
keyInnovations.en: Fortified pā construction, continuation of ocean-going canoe traditions, horticulture adapted to temperate conditions, fine wood carving, and fiber weaving

strengths.zh: 亲族凝聚力强，地方适应能力高，防御工事和近距离作战经验丰富。
strengths.en: Strong kin cohesion, high local adaptability, and substantial experience in fortification and close combat.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权、金属工具和远距离政治整合能力，经济剩余有限。
weaknesses.en: Lack of unified authority, metal tools, and long-range political integration, with limited economic surplus.

outlook.zh: 在外来冲击到来前，毛利社会将继续扩展区域性部族网络，并在竞争与联盟中保持活力。
outlook.en: Before major outside contact, Māori society was likely to continue expanding regional tribal networks while remaining dynamic through rivalry and alliance.

description.zh: 1500年前后的毛利人是以部族酋邦、要塞聚落和口传传统为特征的南岛语系定居社会。
description.en: Around 1500, the Māori were an Austronesian settler society marked by tribal chiefdoms, fortified settlements, and oral tradition.

governmentForm.zh: 阿拉干王国是以国王为中心、兼具佛教王权与海港商业特色的君主制国家。
governmentForm.en: The Kingdom of Arakan was a monarchy centered on the king, combining Buddhist kingship with a maritime-commercial state structure.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、宫廷贵族、佛教僧团、军官、商人、农民和奴仆构成。沿海贸易促进了多民族人口混居，孟族、若开人、穆斯林商人与俘虏群体共同存在。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, court nobility, Buddhist clergy, military officers, merchants, peasants, and slaves. Coastal trade fostered a multiethnic population in which Rakhines, Bengalis, Muslim merchants, and captive communities coexisted.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层为国王、王族、大臣、军政贵族及与宫廷关系密切的地方豪强。王权依靠港口税收、稻米产区和军力维持。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the king, royal kin, ministers, military-administrative nobles, and local magnates tied closely to the court. Royal authority rested on port revenues, rice-producing zones, and military force.

succession.zh: 王位原则上由王室内部继承，但常受宫廷斗争、军事支持和贵族拥立影响。强势君主可通过任命亲族与控制军队巩固继承秩序。
succession.en: The throne in principle passed within the royal house, but succession was often shaped by court struggles, military backing, and noble support. Strong rulers secured succession by appointing royal kinsmen and controlling the army.

structure.zh: 阿拉干以妙乌都城为政治核心，中央直接控制王室财政、港口贸易和军事征调。地方由受封官员、城镇首长和附属土司共同管理。
structure.en: Arakan was politically centered on Mrauk-U, where the crown directly controlled royal finance, port trade, and military levies. Local administration was shared by appointed officials, town heads, and subordinate chiefs.

function.zh: 起草诏令、保管文书、管理朝觐与王室通信。
function.en: Drafted royal edicts, kept records, and managed court audiences and royal correspondence.

function.zh: 征收关税、管理金银与仓储、监督港口和市场税。
function.en: Collected customs, managed bullion and granaries, and supervised ports and market taxes.

function.zh: 负责军队编制、征发水陆兵、保管军械和边防调动。
function.en: Handled army organization, levies of land and naval troops, storage of arms, and frontier deployment.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托河谷、港口和稻米产区设置，王国通过任命地方官与承认地方首领双轨统治。边缘地区控制力较弱，需依赖朝贡与军事威慑。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized around river valleys, ports, and rice districts, with the kingdom ruling through a dual system of appointed officials and recognized local chiefs. Control in peripheral areas was weaker and depended on tribute and military intimidation.

legalSystem.zh: 法律结合佛教伦理、王令、地方习惯和宫廷审判。商业港口中的穆斯林和外来商人常按社群习惯处理民事事务。
legalSystem.en: Law combined Buddhist ethics, royal decrees, local custom, and court judgment. In commercial ports, Muslim and foreign merchants often handled civil matters according to community norms.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自稻田地租、港口关税、贡赋和劳役。沿海贸易税是王国财富的重要来源之一。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue came mainly from rice-land dues, port customs, tribute, and labor obligations. Maritime customs were one of the kingdom's most important sources of wealth.

religion.zh: 上座部佛教
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 妙乌时期以佛寺、宫城、防御建筑和多语言宫廷文化著称。阿拉干连接缅甸内陆与孟加拉湾贸易网络，在佛教艺术和海港都市文化上表现突出。
culturalAchievements.en: The Mrauk-U period was known for temples, palace-fortress architecture, and a multilingual court culture. Arakan linked inland Burma with Bay of Bengal trade networks and stood out for both Buddhist art and port-city culture.

languageFamily.zh: 若开语属汉藏语系缅语支
languageFamily.en: Arakanese belongs to the Tibeto-Burman branch of the Sino-Tibetan family

philosophy.zh: 上座部佛教王权观，并吸收孟加拉湾贸易世界中的多元宗教与宫廷文化影响
philosophy.en: A Theravada Buddhist conception of kingship shaped by the plural religious and courtly influences of the Bay of Bengal trading world

writingSystem.zh: 使用若开—缅文书写传统，并在行政与商业环境中接触波斯文、阿拉伯文和孟加拉文
writingSystem.en: Used the Arakanese-Burmese writing tradition, while also encountering Persian, Arabic, and Bengali in administration and commerce

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 阿拉干经济兼具稻作农业和港口贸易双重特征。都城妙乌与沿海口岸从转口贸易、关税和手工业中获得较高收益，支撑了相对强健的王权财政。
gdpDescription.en: Arakan's economy combined rice agriculture with port commerce. Mrauk-U and the coastal harbors drew significant income from entrepôt trade, customs, and craft production, supporting a comparatively robust royal treasury.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、港口贸易、造船、渔盐业、佛寺营建、手工业
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, port trade, shipbuilding, fisheries and salt, temple construction, handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、盐、木材、象、象牙、纺织品、蜡、干鱼、输入银币与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, timber, elephants, ivory, textiles, wax, dried fish, imported silver coin, and luxury goods

name.en: Silver coin and cowrie mixed circulation

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富来自稻田、牲畜和家庭储粮；港口商人与官僚阶层则拥有银币、海外货物和奴仆。王室与寺院掌握大量土地和劳动力。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary rural wealth rested on rice land, livestock, and household grain stores; port merchants and officials held silver, overseas goods, and slaves. The crown and monasteries controlled substantial land and labor.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王室控制港口税收和土地贡赋、并与区域海贸结合的农业—商业混合经济
economicSystem.en: An agro-commercial mixed economy in which the crown controlled port customs and land dues while participating in regional maritime trade

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室积蓄以银币、谷物、港口存货和贡赋珍品构成。妙乌作为贸易都会，使阿拉干拥有比多数小王国更高的流动财力。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves consisted of silver coin, grain, port inventories, and tribute luxuries. As a trading capital, Mrauk-U gave Arakan greater liquid resources than many small kingdoms.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持港口税源、保障军事力量和资助佛教王权象征工程。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized preserving customs income, sustaining military power, and funding projects that symbolized Buddhist kingship.

description.zh: 以长矛、刀盾、弓弩和部分火器构成，是王国主要野战力量。
description.en: Armed with spears, swords, shields, bows, crossbows, and some firearms, they formed the kingdom's main field force.

description.zh: 用于海岸巡逻、港口控制、河口作战和掠袭行动，是阿拉干的重要优势。
description.en: Used for coastal patrol, port control, estuary warfare, and raiding, they were one of Arakan's key strengths.

description.zh: 规模不大但象征性强，用于冲击、统帅护卫和战场威慑。
description.en: Limited in number but highly symbolic, they served for shock action, command protection, and battlefield intimidation.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王明腊扎
commanderInChief.en: King Min Raza

technology.zh: 阿拉干军队处于南亚—东南亚交汇地带，已接触火药武器、火绳枪和火炮，但仍以传统步兵、水军和象兵为骨干。
technology.en: Arakan's forces, positioned between South and Southeast Asia, had access to gunpowder weapons, matchlocks, and artillery, though traditional infantry, naval forces, and elephants remained the backbone.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自孟加拉边境争夺、缅甸内陆强权竞争、沿海海盗与附属地区叛乱。
threats.en: The main threats came from struggles along the Bengal frontier, rivalry with inland Burmese powers, coastal piracy, and revolts in subordinate territories.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于沿海平原、河谷与妙乌周边，兼有山区和边地部族居民。港口城市吸纳了来自孟加拉湾各地的商人、工匠和宗教社群。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in coastal plains, river valleys, and the region around Mrauk-U, alongside hill and frontier peoples. Port towns attracted merchants, craftsmen, and religious communities from across the Bay of Bengal.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、僧团、军官与官吏、商人和工匠、农民、奴仆与战俘
socialClasses.en: Royalty and nobility, monastic clergy, officers and officials, merchants and artisans, peasants, slaves, and captives

allies.zh: 与部分孟加拉沿海势力和海上商人社群保持务实合作，也通过朝贡和联姻维持周边关系。
allies.en: It maintained pragmatic cooperation with some Bengal littoral powers and maritime merchant communities, while using tribute and marriage ties to manage neighbors.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手包括孟加拉边境对手、缅甸内陆政权以及挑战王权的地方势力。
enemies.en: Principal enemies included rivals on the Bengal frontier, inland Burmese states, and local forces challenging royal authority.

foreignPolicy.zh: 阿拉干奉行以控制海贸节点、平衡孟加拉与缅甸势力、并维护边境缓冲地带为核心的外交政策。
foreignPolicy.en: Arakan pursued a foreign policy focused on controlling maritime trade nodes, balancing Bengal and Burmese powers, and preserving buffer zones on its frontiers.

era.zh: 晚中世纪至早期近代的南亚—东南亚海贸王国
era.en: A late medieval to early early-modern South-Southeast Asian maritime kingdom

keyInnovations.zh: 港口城市防御体系、区域性造船与航运、早期火药武器使用、多语言商贸行政
keyInnovations.en: Port-city defense systems, regional shipbuilding and navigation, early use of gunpowder weapons, and multilingual commercial administration

strengths.zh: 兼有农业腹地与海港贸易，财政较充裕，海上机动和边境经营能力较强。
strengths.en: It possessed both an agricultural hinterland and maritime trade income, relatively ample finances, and strong naval mobility and frontier management.

weaknesses.zh: 地理狭长、边境压力大，对港口贸易依赖较强，宫廷继承和地方控制存在隐患。
weaknesses.en: Its elongated geography, heavy frontier pressure, strong reliance on port trade, and vulnerabilities in succession and local control were major weaknesses.

outlook.zh: 若能继续掌握孟加拉湾贸易并维持王权稳定，阿拉干仍有扩张和繁荣空间；但一旦遭遇内陆强国或海贸格局变化，其脆弱性将显现。
outlook.en: If it could continue controlling Bay of Bengal trade and preserve stable kingship, Arakan still had room for expansion and prosperity; but its vulnerabilities would emerge if challenged by inland powers or shifts in maritime commerce.

description.zh: 1500年前后的阿拉干王国是以妙乌为中心、结合上座部佛教王权与孟加拉湾海贸网络的繁荣海港王国。
description.en: Around 1500, the Kingdom of Arakan was a prosperous port kingdom centered on Mrauk-U, combining Theravada Buddhist kingship with Bay of Bengal maritime trade networks.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，诸岛酋长体系
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty, an archipelagic chiefdom order

governmentForm.zh: 1500年前后的波利尼西亚诸岛由多个独立酋邦和部族共同构成，政治权力分散于各岛酋长家系。
governmentForm.en: Around 1500, the islands of Polynesia were composed of many independent chiefdoms and tribes, with political authority dispersed among chiefly lineages on each island.

socialStructure.zh: 社会普遍以血缘、航海传统和宗教禁忌组织，常见酋长、祭司、自由民与依附者的等级结构。不同岛群在等级严密程度上有差异，但亲族关系始终是核心。
socialStructure.en: Society was generally organized through kinship, seafaring traditions, and sacred taboo, usually with ranked strata of chiefs, priests, commoners, and dependents. The strictness of hierarchy varied among island groups, but kinship remained fundamental everywhere.

rulingClass.zh: 统治者为各岛高酋、宗教祭司和拥有远祖谱系的贵族家族。其地位来自神圣血统、资源分配能力和航海或战争领导。
rulingClass.en: Rulers were high chiefs, ritual priests, and noble families claiming ancient genealogies. Their status derived from sacred ancestry, control over resources, and leadership in voyaging or war.

succession.zh: 继承方式多在酋长家系内部进行，常由长子、近亲或最具威望者继承。若发生争议，部族支持与武力竞争会决定结果。
succession.en: Succession generally took place within chiefly lineages, often favoring the eldest son, a close relative, or the most prestigious candidate. In disputed cases, tribal support and force could determine the outcome.

structure.zh: 波利尼西亚没有统一中央政府，各岛依据当地传统组织政治生活。酋长会议、祭司团体与大家族首领共同决定土地、航海、战争和祭礼事务。
structure.en: Polynesia had no single central government; each island organized political life according to local tradition. Councils of chiefs, priestly groups, and leading kin heads jointly decided land, voyaging, warfare, and ritual matters.

function.zh: 协调岛内土地分配、盟约、争端与战事。
function.en: Coordinated land distribution, alliances, disputes, and warfare within an island.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、维护禁忌、保存航海知识并监督季节性远航。
function.en: Conducted rituals, maintained taboos, preserved navigational knowledge, and supervised seasonal voyages.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理以单岛、谷地或礁湖社区为单位，由酋长和家族长者执行。行政更多依赖口头命令、宗教权威和劳役义务，而非文书制度。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated at the level of individual islands, valleys, or lagoon communities under chiefs and family elders. Governance relied more on oral command, religious authority, and labor obligations than on written institutions.

legalSystem.zh: 法律基础为习惯法、神圣禁忌和酋长裁断。对亵渎祭地、越界捕鱼或婚姻纠纷等行为，多以赔偿、放逐或仪式惩罚处理。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on custom, sacred taboo, and chiefly judgment. Violations such as desecrating sacred sites, trespassing on fishing grounds, or marriage disputes were often addressed through compensation, exile, or ritual punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有近代意义上的税制，酋长通过贡纳、节庆馈赠和集体劳动取得资源。远航与战争时期会加重对粮食、独木舟和人力的征集。
taxationSystem.en: There was no modern tax system; chiefs obtained resources through tribute, ceremonial gift offerings, and communal labor. Voyaging and wartime increased requisitions of food, canoes, and manpower.

religion.zh: 波利尼西亚多神教
religion.en: Polynesian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 波利尼西亚人的最大成就是跨洋航海传统、恒星导航知识和适应不同岛屿生态的聚落组织。祭祀建筑、独木舟工艺、纹身与歌舞文化高度发达。
culturalAchievements.en: The greatest achievements of the Polynesians lay in transoceanic voyaging, stellar navigation, and settlement systems adapted to varied island ecologies. Ritual architecture, canoe craftsmanship, tattooing, and performance traditions were highly developed.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系波利尼西亚语族
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Polynesian branch

philosophy.zh: 以祖先崇拜、神灵秩序、海洋宇宙观与禁忌制度为核心的波利尼西亚宗教思想
philosophy.en: Polynesian religious thought centered on ancestors, divine order, an oceanic cosmology, and systems of taboo

writingSystem.zh: 无原生文字体系，以口传史诗、歌谣、结绳记忆和雕刻象征保存知识
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; knowledge was preserved through oral epics, chants, mnemonic forms, and carved symbols

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 波利尼西亚经济主要是岛屿自给型生产，依赖块根作物、椰子、渔业和有限的岛际交换。由于资源环境受岛屿面积和淡水条件限制，总体剩余不高，但航海网络增强了互补性。
gdpDescription.en: The Polynesian economy was mainly island-based subsistence production relying on root crops, coconuts, fishing, and limited inter-island exchange. Because resources were constrained by island size and freshwater conditions, overall surplus was modest, though voyaging networks improved complementarity.

mainIndustries.zh: 园圃农业、渔业、独木舟建造、编织、木雕、贝壳与羽饰工艺
mainIndustries.en: Horticulture, fishing, canoe building, weaving, wood carving, shell and feather craft

tradeGoods.zh: 贝壳饰品、羽毛、精制木器、石斧、席垫、椰制品、干鱼
tradeGoods.en: Shell ornaments, feathers, fine wooden goods, stone adzes, mats, coconut products, dried fish

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富通常体现为果树、耕地使用权、渔具、独木舟和亲族支持网络。高酋家庭则拥有更多礼品储备、宗教圣地控制权和劳动力支配力。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth typically appeared in fruit trees, land-use rights, fishing equipment, canoes, and kin support networks. High-chief households held larger gift reserves, control over sacred sites, and command over labor.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以酋长再分配、亲族互惠、贡纳和有限岛际交换为主的海岛部族经济
economicSystem.en: An island tribal economy based on chiefly redistribution, kin reciprocity, tribute, and limited inter-island exchange

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 战争掠获与附属社群贡献
source.en: War booty and subordinate community contributions

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无统一国库，储备分散于各岛高酋和祭司手中，主要为粮食、礼品、贝饰和航海物资。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified treasury; reserves were dispersed among high chiefs and priests on different islands, mainly as food, prestige gifts, shell valuables, and voyaging supplies.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政活动着重保证祭礼、远航与灾荒储备，依赖再分配而非固定税制。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal activity focused on supporting ritual, voyaging, and famine reserves, relying on redistribution rather than fixed taxation.

description.zh: 以长矛、棍棒、石器和近战武器为主，负责岛内冲突和登陆作战。
description.en: Equipped mainly with spears, clubs, stone implements, and close-combat weapons, they fought in island conflicts and landing actions.

description.zh: 用于岛际袭击、护航和跨礁湖机动，规模不大但机动性高。
description.en: Used for inter-island raids, escort, and lagoon mobility; small in number but highly mobile.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一统帅，由各岛酋长分别领兵
commanderInChief.en: No single commander; each island chief led his own forces

technology.zh: 军事技术以木、石、骨制兵器和独木舟机动为核心，不具备金属武器和火药武器。部分岛屿有防御性祭地或土木工事。
technology.en: Military technology centered on weapons of wood, stone, and bone, together with canoe mobility, without metal arms or gunpowder. Some islands maintained defensive ritual enclosures or earthwork positions.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁是岛际争斗、资源匮乏导致的迁移压力、风暴灾害和局部饥荒。
