foreignPolicy.en: Maintained balance diplomacy among France, the Habsburgs, and Italian states, leveraging Alpine geographic advantages.

era.zh: 文艺复兴初期阿尔卑斯公国

keyInnovations.zh: 阿尔卑斯山道工程、城堡防御技术、山地农牧技术改良。
keyInnovations.en: Alpine pass engineering, castle defense technology, and mountain agro-pastoral technique improvements.

strengths.zh: 阿尔卑斯战略位置控制南北通道、过境贸易收入稳定、王朝联姻网络广泛。
strengths.en: Alpine strategic position controlling north-south passages, stable transit trade income, and extensive dynastic marriage networks.

weaknesses.zh: 领土狭小分散、人口有限、夹在法国与意大利大国之间。
weaknesses.en: Small and fragmented territory, limited population, and squeezed between French and Italian great powers.

outlook.zh: 萨伏依将在意大利战争中承受重大压力，但凭借外交灵活性与阿尔卑斯地理可望存续。
outlook.en: Savoy would face heavy pressure in the Italian Wars, but could hope to survive through diplomatic flexibility and Alpine geography.

description.zh: 1500年前后的萨伏依公国是控制阿尔卑斯通道的小型世袭公国，在法国、哈布斯堡与意大利之间维持精妙的平衡外交。
description.en: Around 1500, the Duchy of Savoy was a small hereditary duchy controlling Alpine passes, maintaining delicate balance diplomacy among France, the Habsburgs, and Italian states.

name.zh: 拉古萨亚得里亚商业文明

governmentForm.zh: 贵族寡头共和制，执政官每月轮换，大议会掌握实际权力，向奥斯曼帝国缴纳年贡以换取贸易自由。
governmentForm.en: An aristocratic oligarchic republic with the Rector rotating monthly, the Grand Council holding real power, paying annual tribute to the Ottoman Empire in exchange for trade freedom.

socialStructure.zh: 由注册贵族家族、富商、船主、手工业者、水手与少量农民构成。
socialStructure.en: Composed of registered noble families, wealthy merchants, shipowners, artisans, sailors, and a small peasant population.

rulingClass.zh: 约30个注册贵族家族垄断政权。
rulingClass.en: About 30 registered noble families monopolized political power.

succession.zh: 执政官由大议会从贵族中选举产生，任期仅一个月，不可连任。
succession.en: The Rector was elected by the Grand Council from among nobles, serving only one month and not eligible for re-election.

structure.zh: 大议会为最高权力机构，小议会处理日常行政，执政官为名义元首但实权有限；元老院参与重大决策。
structure.en: The Grand Council was the supreme authority, the Minor Council handled daily administration, the Rector was nominal head with limited power; the Senate participated in major decisions.

function.zh: 管理共和国税收、关税与国库。
function.en: Managed republic taxes, customs, and treasury.

function.zh: 监管航运、商船队、贸易许可与港口运营。
function.en: Supervised shipping, merchant fleet, trade licenses, and port operations.

function.zh: 处理与奥斯曼、威尼斯等国的外交关系与贸易条约。
function.en: Handled diplomatic relations and trade treaties with the Ottomans, Venice, and other states.

localAdmin.zh: 城市核心由议会直接管理，周边小型领地与岛屿由派驻官员治理。
localAdmin.en: The city core was governed directly by councils, with surrounding small territories and islands administered by appointed officials.

legalSystem.zh: 以1272年《拉古萨法典》为基础，融合罗马法与商法，城市条例完善。
legalSystem.en: Based on the 1272 Statute of Ragusa, blending Roman law and commercial law, with well-developed city statutes.

taxationSystem.zh: 关税、港口费、商业税、盐专卖和外交贡金（奥斯曼年贡）为主要来源。
taxationSystem.en: Customs, port fees, commercial taxes, salt monopoly, and diplomatic tribute (Ottoman annual payment) as main sources.

religion.zh: 罗马天主教，教会在城市生活中角色重要。
religion.en: Roman Catholicism, with the Church playing an important role in city life.

philosophy.zh: 人文主义与商业实用主义并行，重视外交智慧与城市共和理想。
philosophy.en: Humanism and commercial pragmatism in parallel, valuing diplomatic wisdom and the city-republic ideal.

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁文、意大利语和克罗地亚语并用。
writingSystem.en: Latin, Italian, and Croatian used concurrently.

culturalAchievements.zh: 杜布罗夫尼克城墙与建筑群、发达的文学与诗歌传统、早期废奴实践（1416年废除奴隶贸易）、药剂学传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Dubrovnik's walls and architectural complex, a developed literary and poetic tradition, early abolition of the slave trade (1416), and pharmaceutical traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系斯拉夫语族（克罗地亚语）与罗曼语族（达尔马提亚意大利语）
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Slavic (Croatian) and Romance (Dalmatian Italian)

gdpDescription.zh: 拉古萨以人均收入极高著称，是亚得里亚海的贸易枢纽，凭借外交手段在奥斯曼帝国领地内享有特殊贸易地位。
gdpDescription.en: Ragusa was noted for very high per capita income, serving as an Adriatic trade hub, enjoying special trading status in Ottoman territories through diplomatic skill.

mainIndustries.zh: 海运与转口贸易、造船、丝绸与布匹加工、盐业专卖、金融与保险。
mainIndustries.en: Maritime shipping and entrepôt trade, shipbuilding, silk and cloth processing, salt monopoly, and finance and insurance.

tradeGoods.zh: 从巴尔干内陆中介白银、铅、毛皮、蜂蜡；从地中海各地转运丝绸、香料、布匹。
tradeGoods.en: Intermediated silver, lead, furs, and beeswax from the Balkan interior; transshipped silk, spices, and cloth from across the Mediterranean.

householdWealth.zh: 贵族与大商人家庭极为富裕，普通市民生活水平也高于多数巴尔干地区。
householdWealth.en: Noble and great merchant families were extremely wealthy, with ordinary citizens also living above the standard of most Balkan regions.

averageIncome.zh: 普通市民年收入约20-30杜卡特，贵族商人可达数百至上千杜卡特。
averageIncome.en: Ordinary citizens earned about 20-30 ducats per year, noble merchants could earn hundreds to thousands.

foreignTradeVolume.zh: 对外贸易是经济命脉，贸易额约占GDP的70%以上。
foreignTradeVolume.en: Foreign trade was the economic lifeline, with trade volume exceeding 70% of GDP.

economicSystem.zh: 以海运贸易和金融中介为核心的高度商业化城市经济。
economicSystem.en: A highly commercialized urban economy centered on maritime trade and financial intermediation.

treasuryDescription.zh: 共和国财政管理审慎，国库充裕，以应对突发危机。
treasuryDescription.en: The republic's fiscal management was prudent, with an ample treasury to handle emergencies.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 审慎财政，以关税和商业税为主，保持财政盈余用于城市防御和外交贡金。
fiscalPolicy.en: Prudent fiscal policy, relying on customs and commercial taxes, maintaining surpluses for city defense and diplomatic tribute.

description.zh: 守卫杜布罗夫尼克城墙的常备部队。
description.en: Standing force guarding Dubrovnik's walls.

description.zh: 战时动员的市民民兵和武装商船。
description.en: Citizen militia mobilized in wartime and armed merchantmen.

commanderInChief.zh: 执政官与小议会共同指挥防务。
commanderInChief.en: The Rector and Minor Council jointly commanded defense.

technology.zh: 坚固城墙防御体系、海岸炮台、武装商船。
technology.en: Strong wall defense system, coastal batteries, and armed merchant vessels.

threats.zh: 威尼斯商业竞争、奥斯曼帝国可能的吞并、海盗。
threats.en: Venetian commercial competition, possible Ottoman annexation, and piracy.

populationDescription.zh: 拉古萨城及周边领地与岛屿人口。
populationDescription.en: Population of the city of Ragusa and surrounding territories and islands.

socialClasses.zh: 注册贵族家族、富商与船主、手工业者、水手、少量农民。
socialClasses.en: Registered noble families, wealthy merchants and shipowners, artisans, sailors, and a small peasant population.

allies.zh: 向奥斯曼帝国缴纳贡金以换取贸易特权，与西班牙和教廷保持友好。
allies.en: Paid tribute to the Ottoman Empire in exchange for trade privileges, maintaining friendly relations with Spain and the Papacy.

enemies.zh: 与威尼斯有长期商业竞争，但一般避免军事冲突。
enemies.en: Long-standing commercial rivalry with Venice, but generally avoiding military conflict.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以外交智慧和金钱维持独立，在奥斯曼、威尼斯和西方基督教世界之间精妙平衡。
foreignPolicy.en: Maintained independence through diplomatic skill and tribute, delicately balancing among the Ottomans, Venice, and the Western Christian world.

era.zh: 文艺复兴时期亚得里亚城市共和国

keyInnovations.zh: 杜布罗夫尼克城墙工程、造船技术、海上保险与商法、早期公共卫生与药剂学。
keyInnovations.en: Dubrovnik wall engineering, shipbuilding, maritime insurance and commercial law, and early public health and pharmacy.

strengths.zh: 外交技巧卓越、贸易网络遍布巴尔干与地中海、城市防御坚固、财政管理审慎、人均财富极高。
strengths.en: Excellent diplomatic skills, trade network spanning the Balkans and Mediterranean, strong city defenses, prudent fiscal management, and very high per capita wealth.

weaknesses.zh: 人口极少、军事力量微弱、完全依赖外交维持独立、面对大国扩张脆弱。
weaknesses.en: Extremely small population, weak military force, entirely dependent on diplomacy for independence, and vulnerable to great-power expansion.

outlook.zh: 拉古萨将凭借外交智慧和贸易价值维持独立地位数百年，是小国生存艺术的典范。
outlook.en: Ragusa would maintain its independence for centuries through diplomatic skill and trade value, exemplifying the art of small-state survival.

description.zh: 1500年前后的拉古萨是亚得里亚海畔的微型商业共和国，以外交智慧与贸易繁荣著称，在大国环伺中维持令人瞩目的独立与繁荣。
description.en: Around 1500, Ragusa was a tiny commercial republic on the Adriatic, renowned for diplomatic skill and trade prosperity, maintaining remarkable independence and flourishing amid surrounding great powers.

governmentForm.zh: 以布尔巴（大王）为核心的松散帝国联盟，下辖卡约尔、瓦洛、巴奥尔等附庸王国，中央权力日趋削弱。
governmentForm.en: A loose imperial confederation under the Bourba (Great King), with vassal kingdoms such as Cayor, Waalo, and Baol, with central authority increasingly weakening.

socialStructure.zh: 由王族（garmi）、自由人贵族（jaambur）、手工业种姓（nyeenyo）、吟游诗人（griot）、奴隶构成的等级社会。
socialStructure.en: A caste society of royals (garmi), freeborn nobles (jaambur), artisan castes (nyeenyo), griots, and slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 恩迪亚耶王族与各附庸王国的王族联盟。
rulingClass.en: The Ndiaye royal family and allied royals of vassal kingdoms.

succession.zh: 王位在恩迪亚耶王族内选举，经长老会议和附庸王推举确认。
succession.en: The throne was elected within the Ndiaye royal family, confirmed by a council of elders and vassal kings.

structure.zh: 布尔巴统辖帝国全域，各附庸王国（卡约尔、瓦洛、巴奥尔、锡内-萨卢姆）保有内政自治权；中央通过贡赋、婚姻联盟和军事威慑维持控制。
structure.en: The Bourba ruled the entire empire, with vassal kingdoms (Cayor, Waalo, Baol, Sine-Saloum) retaining domestic autonomy; central control was maintained through tribute, marriage alliances, and military deterrence.

function.zh: 管理朝政、接待使节与协调附庸关系。
function.en: Managed court politics, received envoys, and coordinated vassal relations.

function.zh: 收取附庸贡赋、管理跨区贸易。
function.en: Collected vassal tribute and managed cross-regional trade.

function.zh: 组织帝国军事力量与对外征战。
function.en: Organized imperial military forces and external campaigns.

localAdmin.zh: 各附庸王国由其国王自治，布尔巴通过贡赋义务和军事同盟维持宗主权。
localAdmin.en: Vassal kingdoms were self-governed by their kings, with the Bourba maintaining suzerainty through tribute obligations and military alliances.

legalSystem.zh: 以口传习惯法和长老裁决为主，融合伊斯兰法影响。
legalSystem.en: Based on oral customary law and elder adjudication, with Islamic legal influences.

taxationSystem.zh: 附庸王国缴纳粮食、牲畜与劳役贡赋，贸易关卡征收过境费。
taxationSystem.en: Vassal kingdoms paid tribute in grain, livestock, and corvée; trade checkpoints levied transit fees.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教与传统非洲宗教并存，精英层倾向伊斯兰教，民间保留强烈的祖灵崇拜与自然信仰。
religion.en: Islam and traditional African religions coexisted, with elites leaning toward Islam while common people retained strong ancestral and nature worship.

philosophy.zh: 口传历史与吟游诗人传统、伊斯兰经学、氏族长老智慧。
philosophy.en: Oral history and griot traditions, Islamic scholarship, and clan elder wisdom.

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯文字用于宗教和商业，日常以沃洛夫语口传为主。
writingSystem.en: Arabic script used for religious and commercial purposes, with daily communication primarily in oral Wolof.

culturalAchievements.zh: 吟游诗人（griot）口传史诗传统、精美的纺织与木雕工艺、沃洛夫语口头文学。
culturalAchievements.en: Griot oral epic traditions, fine textile weaving and woodcarving, and Wolof oral literature.

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系大西洋语族（沃洛夫语）
languageFamily.en: Niger-Congo, Atlantic (Wolof)

gdpDescription.zh: 卓洛夫帝国以粗放农牧业和区域贸易为经济基础，受限于干旱气候和沿海葡萄牙商人的竞争。
gdpDescription.en: The Jolof Empire's economy was based on extensive agriculture-pastoralism and regional trade, limited by arid climate and competition from coastal Portuguese merchants.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟类种植、牛羊畜牧、手工纺织、皮革加工、黄金与食盐转运贸易。
mainIndustries.en: Millet cultivation, cattle and sheep herding, hand-loom weaving, leather processing, and gold and salt transit trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 出口黄金（中转）、皮革、棉布、牲畜；进口食盐、阿拉伯马匹、金属器具。
tradeGoods.en: Exported gold (transit), leather, cotton cloth, and livestock; imported salt, Arab horses, and metal tools.

householdWealth.zh: 王族与自由人贵族以牛群和奴隶为主要财富，普通农民仅维持温饱。
householdWealth.en: Royal and freeborn noble wealth mainly in cattle and slaves; ordinary peasants barely sustained subsistence.

averageIncome.zh: 普通家庭年收入极低，约相当于0.5金衡盎司黄金。
averageIncome.en: Average household income was very low, about 0.5 gold-ounce equivalent per year.

foreignTradeVolume.zh: 跨撒哈拉与沿海贸易约占GDP的20%。
foreignTradeVolume.en: Trans-Saharan and coastal trade accounted for about 20% of GDP.

economicSystem.zh: 以贡赋制和氏族公有制为基础的传统农牧经济。
economicSystem.en: A traditional agro-pastoral economy based on tribute and clan communal ownership.

treasuryDescription.zh: 帝国财政随附庸离心倾向加剧而日益削弱。
treasuryDescription.en: Imperial finances were increasingly weakened as vassal centrifugal tendencies grew.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 依赖附庸贡赋和贸易通行税，缺乏集中化税收体系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Relied on vassal tribute and trade transit taxes, lacking a centralized tax system.

description.zh: 布尔巴直属的精锐骑兵和近卫步兵。
description.en: Elite cavalry and guard infantry directly under the Bourba.

description.zh: 由各附庸国王在战时动员的军事力量。
description.en: Forces mobilized by vassal kings in wartime.

commanderInChief.zh: 布尔巴为最高统帅，各附庸王分统所属部队。
commanderInChief.en: The Bourba was supreme commander, with vassal kings leading their own forces.

technology.zh: 骑兵战术（阿拉伯马）、标枪与弓箭、皮甲防护、围墙定居点防御。
technology.en: Cavalry tactics (Arab horses), javelin and bow, leather armor, and walled settlement defense.

threats.zh: 附庸王国独立倾向（尤其卡约尔）、葡萄牙沿海扩张、邻国竞争。
threats.en: Vassal independence tendencies (especially Cayor), Portuguese coastal expansion, and neighboring state competition.

populationDescription.zh: 卓洛夫帝国全境人口（含附庸王国）。
populationDescription.en: Total population of the Jolof Empire (including vassal kingdoms).

socialClasses.zh: 王族、自由人贵族、手工业种姓、吟游诗人、农民、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royals, freeborn nobles, artisan castes, griots, peasants, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与附庸王国维持松散联盟，与内陆马里帝国残余势力有贸易往来。
allies.en: Maintained loose alliances with vassal kingdoms and trade contacts with remnants of the Mali Empire.

enemies.zh: 附庸王国的独立倾向为主要内部威胁，葡萄牙人在沿海的扩张构成外部压力。
enemies.en: Vassal independence tendencies as the main internal threat, with Portuguese coastal expansion as external pressure.

foreignPolicy.zh: 通过婚姻联盟和贡赋体系维持帝国统一，对葡萄牙采取有限接触态度。
foreignPolicy.en: Maintained imperial unity through marriage alliances and the tribute system, with limited engagement toward the Portuguese.

era.zh: 西非中世纪帝国晚期

keyInnovations.zh: 骑兵饲养与马匹管理、旱地农业技术、棉纺织工艺、口传史学体系。
keyInnovations.en: Cavalry breeding and horse management, dryland farming, cotton textile crafts, and oral historiographic systems.

strengths.zh: 骑兵军事传统、跨区贸易网络、吟游诗人文化凝聚力。
strengths.en: Cavalry military traditions, cross-regional trade networks, and griot cultural cohesion.

weaknesses.zh: 中央权力严重削弱、附庸离心倾向加剧、葡萄牙沿海贸易绕过帝国中介、干旱气候限制农业。
weaknesses.en: Severely weakened central authority, growing vassal centrifugal tendencies, Portuguese coastal trade bypassing imperial intermediation, and arid climate limiting agriculture.

outlook.zh: 卓洛夫帝国正走向解体，附庸王国（尤其卡约尔）将在16世纪中叶实现独立。
outlook.en: The Jolof Empire was heading toward dissolution, with vassal kingdoms (especially Cayor) achieving independence by the mid-16th century.

description.zh: 1500年前后的卓洛夫帝国是塞内冈比亚地区的衰落中霸权，附庸王国日益离心，葡萄牙沿海扩张进一步削弱了帝国的贸易中介地位。
description.en: Around 1500, the Jolof Empire was a declining hegemony in Senegambia, with vassal kingdoms increasingly centrifugal and Portuguese coastal expansion further undermining the empire's trade intermediation.

name.zh: 阿朱兰索马里-伊斯兰文明

governmentForm.zh: 伊斯兰苏丹制，伊玛目兼具宗教与世俗权威，通过氏族联盟和水利控制体系维持权力。
governmentForm.en: An Islamic sultanate with the Imam holding both religious and secular authority, maintaining power through clan alliances and hydraulic control systems.

socialStructure.zh: 苏丹与加伦王族、宗教学者（乌莱玛）、氏族长老、商人、牧民与农民、奴隶。
socialStructure.en: The sultan and Gareen royals, religious scholars (ulama), clan elders, merchants, pastoralists and farmers, and slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 加伦王族与核心宗教-商业精英。
rulingClass.en: The Gareen royal family and core religious-commercial elites.

succession.zh: 王位在加伦家族内传承，经长老和宗教学者协商确认。
succession.en: The throne passed within the Gareen family, confirmed through consultation with elders and religious scholars.

structure.zh: 伊玛目统领全境，通过水利工程控制体系和氏族长老网络施行统治；沿海城市由总督管理，内陆由氏族长老自治。
structure.en: The Imam ruled the territory through hydraulic infrastructure control and clan elder networks; coastal cities were managed by governors, the interior by self-governing clan elders.

function.zh: 管理灌溉水利工程和农业生产。
function.en: Managed irrigation works and agricultural production.

function.zh: 监管摩加迪沙等港口的印度洋贸易。
function.en: Supervised Indian Ocean trade at Mogadishu and other ports.

function.zh: 执行伊斯兰法、管理清真寺与宗教教育。
function.en: Enforced Islamic law, managed mosques, and religious education.

localAdmin.zh: 沿海城市由中央派遣总督，内陆地区通过氏族长老自治，水利节点为控制关键。
localAdmin.en: Coastal cities had centrally appointed governors, interior areas self-governed through clan elders, with hydraulic nodes as key control points.

legalSystem.zh: 以伊斯兰教法（沙斐仪学派）为基础，融合索马里习惯法（xeer）。
legalSystem.en: Based on Islamic law (Shafi'i school), blending Somali customary law (xeer).

taxationSystem.zh: 贸易关税、牲畜税、农业什一税（zakat）和水利使用费。
taxationSystem.en: Trade customs, livestock tax, agricultural tithe (zakat), and hydraulic usage fees.

religion.zh: 逊尼派伊斯兰教（沙斐仪学派），宗教在政治与社会生活中占核心地位。
religion.en: Sunni Islam (Shafi'i school), with religion central to political and social life.

philosophy.zh: 伊斯兰经学、苏菲神秘主义、索马里氏族伦理。
philosophy.en: Islamic scholarship, Sufi mysticism, and Somali clan ethics.

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯文字用于宗教、商业和行政，日常以索马里语口传为主。
writingSystem.en: Arabic script for religious, commercial, and administrative use, with daily communication primarily in oral Somali.

culturalAchievements.zh: 摩加迪沙清真寺建筑群、水利灌溉工程体系、印度洋海上贸易网络、伊斯兰经学传承。
culturalAchievements.en: Mogadishu mosque complexes, hydraulic irrigation systems, Indian Ocean maritime trade networks, and Islamic scholarly traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 亚非语系库希特语族（索马里语）
languageFamily.en: Afro-Asiatic, Cushitic (Somali)

gdpDescription.zh: 阿朱兰苏丹国以印度洋贸易和水利灌溉农业为经济支柱，摩加迪沙是东非主要商港之一。
gdpDescription.en: The Ajuran Sultanate relied on Indian Ocean trade and hydraulic irrigation agriculture, with Mogadishu as one of East Africa's major commercial ports.

mainIndustries.zh: 印度洋海上贸易、灌溉农业、游牧畜牧、造币、纺织、珊瑚与乳香贸易。
mainIndustries.en: Indian Ocean maritime trade, irrigated agriculture, pastoral nomadism, coinage, textiles, and coral and frankincense trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 出口乳香、没药、象牙、牲畜、皮革；进口印度布匹、中国瓷器、波斯地毯、金属器具。
tradeGoods.en: Exported frankincense, myrrh, ivory, livestock, and leather; imported Indian cloth, Chinese porcelain, Persian carpets, and metal tools.

name.en: Mogadishu copper coins and cowrie shells

householdWealth.zh: 港口商人较为富裕，内陆牧民和农民以牲畜和土地为财富，生活水平一般。
householdWealth.en: Port merchants were relatively wealthy, inland pastoralists and farmers counted livestock and land as wealth, with moderate living standards.

averageIncome.zh: 内陆牧民家庭年收入极低，港口商人收入较高。
averageIncome.en: Inland pastoral households had very low income, port merchants earned considerably more.

foreignTradeVolume.zh: 印度洋贸易是经济命脉，约占GDP的35%。
foreignTradeVolume.en: Indian Ocean trade was the economic lifeline, about 35% of GDP.

economicSystem.zh: 以印度洋贸易和水利灌溉农业为核心的混合经济，氏族公有制与市场经济并存。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy centered on Indian Ocean trade and hydraulic agriculture, with clan communal and market economies coexisting.

treasuryDescription.zh: 苏丹国财政依靠贸易关税和水利控制，储备尚可。
treasuryDescription.en: The sultanate's finances relied on trade customs and hydraulic control, with adequate reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以贸易关税和水利使用费为核心，辅以伊斯兰天课制度。
fiscalPolicy.en: Centered on trade customs and hydraulic fees, supplemented by Islamic zakat.

description.zh: 苏丹直属的常备军事力量。
description.en: Standing force directly under the sultan.

description.zh: 各氏族动员的武装力量。
description.en: Armed forces mobilized by various clans.

commanderInChief.zh: 伊玛目为最高军事统帅。
commanderInChief.en: The Imam was supreme military commander.

technology.zh: 骑兵与骆驼骑兵、弓箭与长矛、沿海防御工事。
technology.en: Cavalry and camel cavalry, bow and spear, and coastal fortifications.

threats.zh: 葡萄牙在印度洋的海军扩张、内陆氏族叛乱、邻国阿达尔苏丹国竞争。
threats.en: Portuguese naval expansion in the Indian Ocean, inland clan rebellions, and competition from the Adal Sultanate.

populationDescription.zh: 阿朱兰苏丹国全境人口。
populationDescription.en: Total population of the Ajuran Sultanate.

socialClasses.zh: 苏丹与王族、宗教学者、氏族长老、港口商人、牧民、农民、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Sultan and royals, religious scholars, clan elders, port merchants, pastoralists, farmers, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与印度洋贸易伙伴（印度次大陆、波斯、阿拉伯半岛）维持商业关系。
allies.en: Maintained commercial relations with Indian Ocean trading partners (Indian subcontinent, Persia, Arabian Peninsula).

enemies.zh: 葡萄牙在印度洋的扩张构成主要外部威胁，与阿达尔苏丹国有边境竞争。
enemies.en: Portuguese expansion in the Indian Ocean as the main external threat, with border competition from the Adal Sultanate.

foreignPolicy.zh: 维持印度洋贸易网络自由，抵制葡萄牙海上霸权，保护港口城市贸易自主权。
foreignPolicy.en: Maintained Indian Ocean trade network freedom, resisted Portuguese maritime hegemony, and protected port city trade autonomy.

era.zh: 东非中世纪伊斯兰苏丹国

keyInnovations.zh: 大规模水利灌溉工程（索马里水井与渠道体系）、印度洋航海技术、铸币技术、珊瑚石建筑。
keyInnovations.en: Large-scale hydraulic irrigation (Somali well and canal systems), Indian Ocean navigation, coinage, and coral-stone architecture.

strengths.zh: 印度洋贸易网络发达、水利工程体系独特、摩加迪沙港口繁荣。
strengths.en: Developed Indian Ocean trade networks, unique hydraulic engineering systems, and a prosperous Mogadishu port.

weaknesses.zh: 氏族分裂倾向、军事装备落后于葡萄牙、内陆控制依赖水利体系脆弱。
weaknesses.en: Clan fragmentation tendencies, military equipment inferior to Portuguese, and fragile inland control dependent on hydraulic systems.

outlook.zh: 阿朱兰苏丹国将在16-17世纪面临葡萄牙海上压力和内部氏族叛乱的双重冲击。
outlook.en: The Ajuran Sultanate would face the dual impact of Portuguese maritime pressure and internal clan rebellions in the 16th-17th centuries.

description.zh: 1500年前后的阿朱兰苏丹国是非洲之角的伊斯兰强权，以摩加迪沙为中心的印度洋贸易和独特的水利灌溉体系支撑其繁荣。
description.en: Around 1500, the Ajuran Sultanate was an Islamic power in the Horn of Africa, its prosperity sustained by Indian Ocean trade centered on Mogadishu and a unique hydraulic irrigation system.

capital.zh: 恩特贝附近的早期王宫
capital.en: Early royal court near Entebbe area

governmentForm.zh: 中央集权的世袭王国，卡巴卡拥有至高权力，通过任命酋长（bataka/bakungu）直接管理各省。
governmentForm.en: A centralized hereditary kingdom with the Kabaka holding supreme power, directly managing provinces through appointed chiefs (bataka/bakungu).

socialStructure.zh: 由卡巴卡与王族、氏族首领、任命的地方酋长、自由农民、手工业者与奴隶构成。
socialStructure.en: Composed of the Kabaka and royals, clan heads, appointed local chiefs, free peasants, artisans, and slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 卡巴卡与王室任命的行政酋长（bakungu）体系。
rulingClass.en: The Kabaka and the system of royally appointed administrative chiefs (bakungu).

succession.zh: 王位在王族内继承，由王太后（Namasole）和首席大臣（Katikkiro）协商确定。
succession.en: The throne passed within the royal family, determined through consultation between the Queen Mother (Namasole) and the Prime Minister (Katikkiro).

structure.zh: 卡巴卡统领全境，首席大臣（Katikkiro）主持日常行政；各省（ssaza）由国王任命的酋长管理，氏族首领在地方仍有影响力。
structure.en: The Kabaka ruled the territory, with the Katikkiro (Prime Minister) heading daily administration; provinces (ssaza) were managed by royally appointed chiefs, with clan heads retaining local influence.

function.zh: 协调全国行政、司法与贡赋征收。
function.en: Coordinated national administration, justice, and tribute collection.

function.zh: 组织战时动员和边境防御。
function.en: Organized wartime mobilization and border defense.

function.zh: 管理王室仪式、祭祀和文化活动。
function.en: Managed royal ceremonies, rituals, and cultural events.

localAdmin.zh: 各省由卡巴卡任命的酋长治理，氏族制度在基层仍起重要作用。
localAdmin.en: Provinces governed by Kabaka-appointed chiefs, with the clan system still important at the grassroots level.

legalSystem.zh: 以口传习惯法和国王裁决为主，氏族长老在地方纠纷中仍有裁判权。
legalSystem.en: Based on oral customary law and royal judgment, with clan elders retaining arbitration power in local disputes.

taxationSystem.zh: 以实物贡赋（香蕉、牲畜、树皮布）和劳役为主。
taxationSystem.en: Primarily tribute in kind (bananas, livestock, bark cloth) and corvée labor.

religion.zh: 传统非洲宗教，崇拜巴鲁巴勒众神（Balubaale）与祖灵，神殿由专职祭司管理。
religion.en: Traditional African religion, worshipping the Balubaale deities and ancestral spirits, with shrines managed by dedicated priests.

philosophy.zh: 以氏族伦理、王权神圣性与自然崇拜为核心的世界观。
philosophy.en: A worldview centered on clan ethics, the sacred nature of kingship, and nature worship.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，完全依靠口传传统。
writingSystem.en: No writing system, relying entirely on oral traditions.

culturalAchievements.zh: 树皮布制作工艺、大湖区独木舟技术、口传王族谱系与史诗、音乐与舞蹈传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Bark cloth production, Great Lakes canoe technology, oral royal genealogies and epics, and music and dance traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系班图语族（卢干达语）
languageFamily.en: Niger-Congo, Bantu (Luganda)

gdpDescription.zh: 布干达王国处于早期发展阶段，以香蕉种植和渔业为经济基础，湖上贸易初具规模。
gdpDescription.en: The Kingdom of Buganda was in its early development stage, with banana cultivation and fishing as the economic base, and nascent lake trade.

mainIndustries.zh: 香蕉种植、渔业、树皮布生产、畜牧业、湖上贸易。
mainIndustries.en: Banana cultivation, fishing, bark cloth production, livestock, and lake trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 树皮布、鱼干、铁器、盐、牲畜。
tradeGoods.en: Bark cloth, dried fish, ironware, salt, and livestock.

householdWealth.zh: 以香蕉园、牲畜和树皮布为主要家庭财富。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth mainly in banana gardens, livestock, and bark cloth.

averageIncome.zh: 几乎所有家庭以自给自足农业为生。
averageIncome.en: Nearly all households lived from subsistence agriculture.

foreignTradeVolume.en: Lake regional trade accounted for about 10% of the economy.

economicSystem.zh: 以贡赋制和氏族公有制为基础的自给自足农业经济。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence agricultural economy based on tribute and clan communal ownership.

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室储备以实物为主，规模有限。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves were mainly in kind, limited in scale.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以实物贡赋和劳役为财政基础。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy based on tribute in kind and corvée labor.

description.zh: 卡巴卡直属武装力量。
description.en: Armed force directly under the Kabaka.

description.zh: 各省酋长动员的战时武装。
description.en: Wartime forces mobilized by provincial chiefs.

commanderInChief.zh: 卡巴卡为最高统帅。
commanderInChief.en: The Kabaka was supreme commander.

technology.zh: 长矛与盾牌、弓箭、独木舟水军、防御性围栏。
technology.en: Spear and shield, bow and arrow, canoe naval force, and defensive stockades.

threats.zh: 布尼奥罗王国的军事压力、周边小国竞争。
threats.en: Military pressure from the Bunyoro Kingdom and competition from surrounding smaller states.

populationDescription.zh: 布干达王国早期人口。
populationDescription.en: Early population of the Kingdom of Buganda.

socialClasses.zh: 卡巴卡与王族、氏族首领、地方酋长、自由农民、手工业者、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Kabaka and royals, clan heads, local chiefs, free peasants, artisans, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与周边小国有选择性联盟关系。
allies.en: Selective alliances with surrounding smaller states.

enemies.zh: 布尼奥罗王国为主要竞争对手。
enemies.en: The Bunyoro Kingdom as the main rival.

foreignPolicy.zh: 逐步扩张领土与影响力，从布尼奥罗的阴影中崛起。
foreignPolicy.en: Gradually expanding territory and influence, rising from Bunyoro's shadow.

era.zh: 大湖区铁器时代早期王国
era.en: Early Great Lakes Iron Age kingdom

keyInnovations.zh: 树皮布制作技术、香蕉种植优化、独木舟建造、铁器冶炼。
keyInnovations.en: Bark cloth production, banana cultivation optimization, canoe construction, and iron smelting.

strengths.zh: 中央集权体制、肥沃的香蕉种植带、维多利亚湖水路资源、稳定的粮食供给。
strengths.en: Centralized political system, fertile banana cultivation belt, Lake Victoria waterway resources, and stable food supply.

weaknesses.zh: 经济规模极小、缺乏文字、军事技术原始、受布尼奥罗压制。
weaknesses.en: Extremely small economic scale, lack of writing, primitive military technology, and suppression by Bunyoro.

outlook.zh: 布干达王国将在未来数百年持续扩张，最终成为大湖区最强大的王国之一。
outlook.en: The Kingdom of Buganda would continue expanding over the coming centuries, eventually becoming one of the most powerful kingdoms in the Great Lakes region.

description.zh: 1500年前后的布干达是大湖区一个正在崛起的小型王国，以独特的中央集权体制和香蕉种植经济为基础，尚处于早期发展阶段。
description.en: Around 1500, Buganda was a small rising kingdom in the Great Lakes region, based on a distinctive centralized system and banana cultivation economy, still in its early development stage.

governmentForm.zh: 以神圣王权（bulopwe）为核心的中央集权王国，国王兼具政治与宗教权威，通过血亲和联盟网络管理广大领土。
governmentForm.en: A centralized kingdom based on sacred kingship (bulopwe), with the king holding both political and religious authority, governing vast territory through kinship and alliance networks.

socialStructure.zh: 由穆洛普韦与王族、宫廷贵族、地方酋长、铁匠等手工业者、农民与猎人、奴隶构成。
socialStructure.en: Composed of the Mulopwe and royals, court nobles, local chiefs, smiths and artisans, farmers and hunters, and slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 穆洛普韦王族与宫廷贵族（bambudye兄弟会）。
rulingClass.en: The Mulopwe royal family and court nobles (bambudye brotherhood).

succession.zh: 王位在王族内传承，由bambudye兄弟会与长老协商确定。
succession.en: The throne passed within the royal family, determined by the bambudye brotherhood and elder consultation.

structure.zh: 穆洛普韦为最高统治者，bambudye兄弟会维护王权合法性与王室记忆；地方由任命的酋长和血亲首领管理。
structure.en: The Mulopwe was supreme ruler, with the bambudye brotherhood maintaining royal legitimacy and memory; locally, appointed chiefs and kinship leaders governed.

function.zh: 维护王权合法性、保管王室口传历史与仪式。
function.en: Maintained royal legitimacy, preserved oral royal history and rituals.

function.zh: 收集各地贡赋并分配给王室。
function.en: Collected tribute from various areas and distributed it to the royal household.

function.zh: 组织战时动员与领土扩张。
function.en: Organized wartime mobilization and territorial expansion.

localAdmin.zh: 通过血亲酋长和联盟首领治理地方，神圣王权的精神权威维系帝国凝聚力。
localAdmin.en: Local governance through kinship chiefs and allied leaders, with the spiritual authority of sacred kingship maintaining imperial cohesion.

legalSystem.zh: 口传习惯法，以国王裁决和长老仲裁为核心。
legalSystem.en: Oral customary law, centered on royal judgment and elder arbitration.

taxationSystem.zh: 实物贡赋（铁器、铜器、象牙、粮食）和劳役。
taxationSystem.en: Tribute in kind (ironware, copperware, ivory, grain) and corvée labor.

religion.zh: 传统非洲宗教，以祖灵崇拜和神圣王权信仰为核心，精灵崇拜与占卜仪式广泛流行。
religion.en: Traditional African religion, centered on ancestral worship and sacred kingship beliefs, with widespread spirit worship and divination rituals.

philosophy.zh: 神圣王权理念（bulopwe）、氏族伦理与宇宙观。
philosophy.en: Sacred kingship concept (bulopwe), clan ethics, and cosmological worldview.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，以lukasa记忆板和口传传统保存知识。
writingSystem.en: No writing system, with knowledge preserved through lukasa memory boards and oral traditions.

culturalAchievements.zh: lukasa记忆板（独特的信息编码系统）、精美木雕与铜雕艺术、bambudye仪式传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Lukasa memory boards (unique information encoding system), fine wood and copper sculpture, and bambudye ritual traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系班图语族（基卢巴语）
languageFamily.en: Niger-Congo, Bantu (Kiluba)

gdpDescription.zh: 卢巴王国经济以农业和铁铜冶炼为基础，区域贸易网络初步形成。
gdpDescription.en: The Luba Kingdom's economy was based on agriculture and iron-copper smelting, with a nascent regional trade network.

mainIndustries.zh: 铁器冶炼、铜器制造、农业（粟、木薯）、渔业、象牙和棕榈油贸易。
mainIndustries.en: Iron smelting, copper working, agriculture (millet, cassava), fishing, and ivory and palm oil trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、铜十字锭、象牙、棕榈油、盐。
tradeGoods.en: Ironware, copper cross ingots, ivory, palm oil, and salt.

householdWealth.zh: 以铁器、牲畜和粮食储备为主要家庭财富。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth mainly in ironware, livestock, and grain reserves.

averageIncome.zh: 绝大多数家庭为自给自足农业。
averageIncome.en: The vast majority of households practiced subsistence agriculture.

foreignTradeVolume.en: Regional trade accounted for about 10% of GDP.

economicSystem.zh: 以贡赋制和氏族公有制为基础的自给自足经济。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence economy based on tribute and clan communal ownership.

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室以铜器、象牙和铁器为储备物资。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves were in copper, ivory, and iron goods.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以实物贡赋为核心，通过重新分配维护联盟体系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Centered on tribute in kind, maintaining the alliance system through redistribution.

description.zh: 穆洛普韦直属武装。
description.en: Armed force directly under the Mulopwe.

description.zh: 各地酋长动员的武装力量。
description.en: Forces mobilized by local chiefs.

commanderInChief.zh: 穆洛普韦为最高统帅。
commanderInChief.en: The Mulopwe was supreme commander.

technology.zh: 铁制长矛与战斧、弓箭、盾牌、防御性村落。
technology.en: Iron spears and battle axes, bows, shields, and fortified villages.

threats.zh: 周边部族竞争、内部继承纷争。
threats.en: Competing surrounding tribes and internal succession disputes.

populationDescription.zh: 卢巴王国早期人口，分布于刚果中南部。
populationDescription.en: Early population of the Luba Kingdom, distributed across south-central Congo.

socialClasses.zh: 穆洛普韦与王族、bambudye兄弟会、地方酋长、铁匠等手工业者、农民与猎人、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Mulopwe and royals, bambudye brotherhood, local chiefs, smiths and artisans, farmers and hunters, and slaves.

allies.zh: 通过联姻和bulopwe精神权威维系周边附庸关系。
allies.en: Maintained surrounding vassal relations through marriage alliances and bulopwe spiritual authority.

enemies.zh: 周边竞争部族与未臣服的酋邦。
enemies.en: Competing surrounding tribes and unsubmitted chiefdoms.

foreignPolicy.zh: 通过神圣王权理念与贸易网络扩大影响范围。
foreignPolicy.en: Expanded influence through the sacred kingship concept and trade networks.

era.zh: 中非铁器时代班图王国
era.en: Central African Iron Age Bantu kingdom

keyInnovations.zh: 铁铜冶炼技术、lukasa记忆板信息编码、农业轮耕技术。
keyInnovations.en: Iron and copper smelting, lukasa memory board information encoding, and agricultural rotation techniques.

strengths.zh: 神圣王权理念提供强大的政治凝聚力、铁铜冶炼技术先进、贸易网络初具规模。
strengths.en: Sacred kingship providing strong political cohesion, advanced iron-copper smelting, and nascent trade networks.

weaknesses.zh: 经济规模小、缺乏文字、军事技术原始、扩张过程中的控制力有限。
weaknesses.en: Small economic scale, lack of writing, primitive military technology, and limited control during expansion.

outlook.zh: 卢巴王国将在16-17世纪达到鼎盛，其政治理念（bulopwe）将影响整个中南非洲。
outlook.en: The Luba Kingdom would reach its zenith in the 16th-17th centuries, with its political concept (bulopwe) influencing all of central-southern Africa.

description.zh: 1500年前后的卢巴王国是中非一个以神圣王权（bulopwe）和铁铜冶炼为基础的新兴王国，正处于扩张与制度化的早期阶段。
description.en: Around 1500, the Luba Kingdom was an emerging Central African kingdom based on sacred kingship (bulopwe) and iron-copper smelting, in the early stages of expansion and institutionalization.

ruler.zh: 穆瓦塔·雅姆沃（最高首领）

governmentForm.zh: 以穆瓦塔·雅姆沃为核心的早期王权体制，深受卢巴bulopwe政治理念影响，通过亲属网络和贡赋体系管理领土。
governmentForm.en: An early kingship system centered on the Mwata Yamvo, deeply influenced by Luba bulopwe political concepts, governing territory through kinship networks and a tribute system.

socialStructure.zh: 穆瓦塔·雅姆沃与王族、tubungu（贵族议会成员）、地方酋长、铁匠、农民与猎人、奴隶。
socialStructure.en: Mwata Yamvo and royals, tubungu (noble council members), local chiefs, smiths, farmers and hunters, and slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 穆瓦塔·雅姆沃王族与tubungu贵族核心。
rulingClass.en: Mwata Yamvo royal family and the tubungu noble core.

succession.zh: 王位在王族内传承，由tubungu贵族议会推举确认。
succession.en: The throne passed within the royal family, confirmed by the tubungu noble council.

structure.zh: 穆瓦塔·雅姆沃为至高统治者，tubungu贵族参与决策；各地由任命的酋长和世袭首领分层管理。
structure.en: The Mwata Yamvo was supreme ruler, with tubungu nobles participating in decisions; locally, appointed chiefs and hereditary leaders governed in layers.

function.zh: 参与重大决策与王位继承事务。
function.en: Participated in major decisions and royal succession matters.

function.zh: 收集地方贡赋和管理区域贸易。
function.en: Collected local tribute and managed regional trade.

function.zh: 组织领土扩张和防御。
function.en: Organized territorial expansion and defense.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由与王室有血亲或联姻关系的酋长治理，通过贡赋义务维系对中央的效忠。
localAdmin.en: Local chiefs governing through blood or marriage ties to the royal house, with tribute obligations maintaining loyalty to the center.

legalSystem.zh: 口传习惯法和王室裁决。
legalSystem.en: Oral customary law and royal judgment.

taxationSystem.zh: 实物贡赋（铜器、象牙、盐、食物）和劳役。
taxationSystem.en: Tribute in kind (copperware, ivory, salt, food) and corvée labor.

religion.zh: 传统非洲宗教，以祖灵崇拜、自然精灵信仰和神圣王权为核心。
religion.en: Traditional African religion, centered on ancestral worship, nature spirit beliefs, and sacred kingship.

philosophy.zh: 受卢巴bulopwe理念影响的神圣王权信仰与氏族伦理。
philosophy.en: Sacred kingship beliefs influenced by Luba bulopwe concepts, combined with clan ethics.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，以口传传统为主。
writingSystem.en: No writing system, relying on oral traditions.

culturalAchievements.zh: 独特的政治联盟体系（perpetual kinship）、铁铜工艺、口传王族史诗。
culturalAchievements.en: Unique political alliance system (perpetual kinship), iron-copper craftsmanship, and oral royal epics.

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系班图语族（基隆达语）
languageFamily.en: Niger-Congo, Bantu (Kilunda)

gdpDescription.zh: 隆达王国经济以自给自足农业为主，铜贸易和象牙贸易为辅，处于早期国家形成阶段。
gdpDescription.en: The Lunda Kingdom's economy was primarily subsistence agriculture, supplemented by copper and ivory trade, in the early state formation stage.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业（粟、木薯）、狩猎、铜器制造、盐贸易、象牙采集。
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture (millet, cassava), hunting, copper working, salt trade, and ivory collection.

tradeGoods.zh: 铜器、象牙、盐、皮革、铁器。
tradeGoods.en: Copperware, ivory, salt, leather, and ironware.

householdWealth.zh: 以牲畜、铁器和粮食为主要财富。
householdWealth.en: Wealth mainly in livestock, ironware, and grain.

averageIncome.zh: 绝大多数家庭为自给自足。
averageIncome.en: The vast majority of households were self-sufficient.

foreignTradeVolume.en: Regional trade accounted for about 8% of the economy.

economicSystem.zh: 以氏族公有制和贡赋为基础的自给自足经济。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence economy based on clan communal ownership and tribute.

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室储备以铜器和象牙为主，规模尚小。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves mainly in copper and ivory, still small in scale.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以实物贡赋为财政基础，通过重新分配维持联盟体系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Tribute in kind as the fiscal base, maintaining the alliance system through redistribution.

description.zh: 穆瓦塔·雅姆沃直属武装。
description.en: Armed force directly under the Mwata Yamvo.

description.zh: 各地酋长动员的武装力量。
description.en: Forces mobilized by local chiefs.

commanderInChief.zh: 穆瓦塔·雅姆沃为最高统帅。
commanderInChief.en: The Mwata Yamvo was supreme commander.

technology.zh: 铁制长矛与弓箭、盾牌、防御性村落。
technology.en: Iron spears and bows, shields, and fortified villages.

threats.zh: 周边部族竞争、扩张过程中的抵抗。
threats.en: Competing surrounding tribes and resistance during expansion.

populationDescription.zh: 隆达王国早期人口。
populationDescription.en: Early population of the Lunda Kingdom.

socialClasses.zh: 穆瓦塔·雅姆沃与王族、tubungu贵族、地方酋长、铁匠、农民与猎人、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Mwata Yamvo and royals, tubungu nobles, local chiefs, smiths, farmers and hunters, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与卢巴王国有血亲联系和政治借鉴关系。
allies.en: Blood ties and political borrowing relations with the Luba Kingdom.

enemies.zh: 扩张过程中与周边未臣服部族的冲突。
enemies.en: Conflicts with surrounding unsubmitted tribes during expansion.

foreignPolicy.zh: 通过perpetual kinship（永久亲属关系）体系扩展联盟网络。
foreignPolicy.en: Expanded alliance networks through the perpetual kinship system.

era.zh: 中非铁器时代新兴王国
era.en: Emerging Central African Iron Age kingdom

keyInnovations.zh: 铁铜冶炼、perpetual kinship政治制度创新、农业轮耕。
keyInnovations.en: Iron-copper smelting, perpetual kinship political innovation, and agricultural rotation.

strengths.zh: 创新性的perpetual kinship联盟体系、受卢巴影响的制度建设、铜铁贸易网络。
strengths.en: Innovative perpetual kinship alliance system, Luba-influenced institution building, and copper-iron trade networks.

weaknesses.zh: 国家形成极早期、经济规模极小、缺乏文字、中央控制力有限。
weaknesses.en: Very early state formation, extremely small economic scale, lack of writing, and limited central control.

outlook.zh: 隆达王国将在16-18世纪急速扩张，成为中南非洲最大的政治联盟网络之一。
outlook.en: The Lunda Kingdom would rapidly expand in the 16th-18th centuries, becoming one of the largest political alliance networks in central-southern Africa.

description.zh: 1500年前后的隆达王国是中非一个受卢巴政治理念影响的新兴小国，以独特的perpetual kinship联盟体系正在构建跨区域政治网络。
description.en: Around 1500, the Lunda Kingdom was an emerging small state in Central Africa influenced by Luba political concepts, building a cross-regional political network through its unique perpetual kinship alliance system.

governmentForm.zh: 游猎营地。
governmentForm.en: Mobile hunting camps.

socialStructure.zh: 高度平等。
socialStructure.en: Highly egalitarian.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 桦皮独木舟与雪鞋工艺成熟。
culturalAchievements.en: Mature canoe and snowshoe craftsmanship.

languageFamily.zh: 阿萨巴斯卡/阿尔冈昆语系
languageFamily.en: Athabaskan/Algonquian

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 驯鹿狩猎和毛皮采集。
gdpDescription.en: Caribou hunting and fur collecting.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、捕鱼、毛皮
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fishing, fur processing

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、肉干
tradeGoods.en: Furs, dried meat

householdWealth.zh: 独木舟和猎具。
householdWealth.en: Canoes and gear.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给生产和部族内部再分配为主的经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: "国库"主要为首领控制的食物与工具储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of food and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 饥荒和气候。
threats.en: Famine and climate.

populationDescription.zh: 加拿大亚寒带森林。
populationDescription.en: Canadian boreal forest.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 雪鞋、独木舟、陷阱。
keyInnovations.en: Snowshoes, canoes, traps.

strengths.zh: 极端适应力。
strengths.en: Extreme adaptation.

weaknesses.zh: 低密度。
weaknesses.en: Low density.

outlook.zh: 欧洲毛皮商开始接触。
outlook.en: European fur traders beginning contact.

description.zh: 1500年亚寒带森林游猎部族。
description.en: Subarctic forest hunting peoples in 1500.

name.zh: 北美太平洋沿岸采集者
北美太平洋沿岸采集者

governmentForm.zh: 世袭首领治理的复合社会。
governmentForm.en: Complex societies with hereditary leaders.

socialStructure.zh: 贵族、自由民和奴隶。
socialStructure.en: Nobles, commoners, and slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 图腾柱和夸富宴文化鼎盛。
culturalAchievements.en: Totem pole and potlatch culture at peak.

languageFamily.zh: 萨利希/瓦卡什语系
languageFamily.en: Salishan/Wakashan

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 鲑鱼捕捞和海洋资源丰富。
gdpDescription.en: Rich salmon fishing and marine resources.

mainIndustries.zh: 鲑鱼、木工、编织
mainIndustries.en: Salmon, woodworking, weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 鲑鱼干、雪松制品
tradeGoods.en: Dried salmon, cedar products

householdWealth.zh: 独木舟和铜器。
householdWealth.en: Canoes and coppers.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

weaknesses.zh: 分散。

outlook.en: Continue.

description.zh: 1500年高原渔猎民。
description.en: Plateau fishers in 1500.

governmentForm.en: Villages and riverine chiefdoms.

socialStructure.zh: 部分地区等级化。
socialStructure.en: Some areas stratified.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。

rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 易洛魁联盟正在形成。
culturalAchievements.en: Iroquois Confederacy forming.

threats.en: European contact beginning.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 玉米农业、弓箭、贝壳串珠。
keyInnovations.en: Maize farming, bows, wampum.

strengths.zh: 人口多，联盟化趋势。

weaknesses.zh: 即将面对殖民冲击。
weaknesses.en: About to face colonial impact.

outlook.zh: 欧洲接触将根本改变北美。
outlook.en: European contact would transform North America.

description.zh: 1500年北美东部林地民族，正处于欧洲接触前夕。
description.en: Eastern North American woodland peoples in 1500, on the eve of European contact.

taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

culturalAchievements.zh: 土丘建造传统延续，但规模缩小。
